name: Classic loop – running to the Rhune from Olhette
name_original: Boucle classique – Courir à la Rhune depuis Olhette
description:
This pretty loop takes you to one of the emblematic peaks of the Basque Country: the Rhune. Its short ascent and its gentle and pleasant descent make it an ideal route for runners looking for elevation gain and panoramas.

The route begins in the car park located at the end of Chemin Xurien Borda, in the Olhette district towards Ascain opposite the Trabenia hotel.


You cross the barrier at its end to take the GR 10. This gradually climbs to the Col des Trois Fontaines which already offers interesting views of the Basque coast behind you.

From the pass, you leave the GR 10 and attack the last part of the climb to the summit. As you come out of the kindling, the trail rises at the cost of a few switchbacks and higher percentages.

The top of La Rhune offers a 360 degree view of the Basque Country. Your gaze is lost on the horizon of the Atlantic Ocean to the west and you can discern the peaks of the Pyrenees chain to the east.

The descent begins on a wide track that you leave at the foot of Zizkuitza to follow a small path that goes to the right. This path goes around the Larrun Txiki for a little less than two kilometers until you reach the venta Yasola, ideal for taking a short break.

After the venta, you continue the descent until you reach the GR 10 path which brings you back to your starting point and concludes this superb loop.

name: Loop via Ziburu Mendi – running to the Rhune from Olhette
name_original: Boucle par Ziburu Mendi – Courir à la Rhune depuis Olhette
description:
This beautiful route makes you discover the surroundings of the Rhune before offering you the almost infinite panoramas that this summit offers.

The loop begins in the car park at the end of Chemin Xurien Borda, in the Olhette district towards Ascain, opposite the Trabenia hotel. From the path, you cross the small stream using a lying pylon, then you engage on the GR 10 which goes into the forest. About 300 meters after crossing the barrier, you leave the GR by taking a path that climbs to the right. This ascending path guides you to a sheepfold. A few meters further, you take a new path which goes up on your left. It will take you to the top of Ziburu mendi, the mountain of Ciboure. Enjoy the beautiful view of the Rhune, your next objective.

A short descent takes you to the Col de Descarga de Haundi before going back up via one of the variants of the HRP (Haute Route Pyrénéenne) which leads to the Venta Yasola. At this point, you leave the HRP path to embark on a single track that runs along the Larrun Txiki for a little less than two kilometers until you reach a wide track whose end is none other than the summit of the Rhune. .

After enjoying the 360 degree view, a technical descent awaits you. Several bends lead you to the little wood which in turn leads to the col des Trois Fontaines and its beautiful views of the coast.

From there, all you have to do is follow the GR 10 path which takes you back to your starting point.

name: Loop via the Emperor's ascent – running to the Rhune from Olhette
name_original: Boucle par la montée de l'empereur – Courir à la Rhune depuis Olhette
description:
This beautiful loop intended for intermediate level trail runners suggests an ascent of the Rhune in two stages, which offers more variety in the elevation.

The route begins in the car park at the end of Chemin Xurien Borda, in the Olhette district, towards Ascain opposite the Trabenia hotel. You cross the small stream using a recumbent pylon, then you take the GR 10 to the Descarga Haunti pass. This is where you leave the GR path towards Venta Yasola via the HRP (Haute Route Pyrénéenne).

From the venta, you take a single track that runs along the Larrun Txiki for just under two kilometers until you reach a wide track. A few hundred meters after joining the track, you dive on a new, very pleasant single track that goes off to the right. Along the laces, you discover a changing panorama of the Pyrenees which present themselves in front of you. This 3.5 kilometer descent is one of my favorites around La Rhune. It ends at the foot of the Emperor's climb which you are about to attack. The ascent begins with a rocky single that zigzags through the vegetation before continuing through an open meadow.

You are on one of the many variants of the HRP which joins the Rhune from the east by skirting the summit of Pilotalekugaña before reaching the summit which offers you an exceptional panorama of the Basque Country, its coastline and several peaks of the chain of the Pyrenees.

Finally, it is a long descent that awaits you to your starting point. This begins with a first technical part up to the col des Trois Fontaines. From there, you find the GR 10 path which gradually descends to the car park.

name: Loop via Ziburu and the Emperor's ascent – running to the Rhune from Olhette
name_original: Boucle par Ziburu et la montée de l'empereur — Courir à la Rhune depuis Olhette
description:
This beautiful outing is suitable for intermediate level trail runners looking for variations in elevation. The 16.5 kilometer loop has a cumulative drop of 1,250 meters and gradually leads to the top of the Rhune before descending directly to the starting point.

The route starts from the car park at the end of Chemin Xurien Borda, in the Olhette district towards Ascain, opposite the Hotel Trabenia. You cross the small stream using a lying pylon, then you engage on the GR 10 which you leave quickly to climb the mountain of Ciboure as a warm-up.

Then you go down to the pass before going up towards the venta Yasola. From there, you take a single track that runs along the Larrun Txiki for just under two kilometers until you reach a wide track. A few hundred meters after joining the track, you dive on a new, very pleasant single track that goes off to the right. Along the laces, you discover a changing panorama of the Pyrenees which present themselves in front of you. The descent ends at the foot of the Montée de l'Empereur, which you are about to tackle. The ascent begins with a rocky single track that winds through the vegetation before emerging into an open meadow.

You are on one of the many variants of the HRP (Haute Route Pyrénéenne) which joins the Rhune from the east, bypassing the summit of Pilotalekugaña before reaching the summit which offers an exceptional panorama of the Basque Country, its coastline. and several peaks of the Pyrenees chain.

Finally, it is a long descent that awaits you to your starting point. This begins with a first technical part up to the col des Trois Fontaines. From there, you find the GR 10 path which gradually descends to the car park.

name: Panoramic loop – running to the Rhune from Olhette
name_original: Boucle des panoramas — Courir à la Rhune depuis Olhette
description:
This loop of almost 29 kilometers for 1,750 meters of elevation gain is designed for experienced trail runners who are looking for beautiful panoramas over the Basque Country.

The start is in the parking lot at the end of Chemin Xurien Borda, in the Olhette district towards Ascain, opposite the Hotel Trabenia. You start by taking the HRP (Haute Route Pyrénéenne) which goes west and goes around the mountain of Ciboure before joining the GR 10 along a stream. From there you climb to the Descarga Haundi pass and then take the direction of the Yasola venta from which you climb to the Larrun Txiki.

A wide descending false flat leads you to the undergrowth near the col des Trois Fontaines. From there, the climb steepens and meanders to the top of the Rhune which awaits you with its breathtaking panoramas. The path resumes on a wide track before plunging onto a beautiful and very pleasant downhill path which leads to the foot of the Montée de l'Empereur. Back on one of the HRP variants, you climb towards the Athekaleun breach.

Quickly, you join the GR 10 path which descends for several kilometers to the outskirts of the village of Sare. Then it's a new gradual climb that awaits you to the balcony of the Altsanga revealing a breathtaking view of the Atlantic Ocean. You reach the col des Trois Fontaines before descending to your starting point following the GR 10.

name: Big loop – running to the Rhune from Olhette
name_original: La grande boucle — Courir à la Rhune depuis Olhette
description:
This large loop of 33 kilometers and 2,200 meters of elevation gain is aimed at experienced trail runners who wish to discover the surroundings and the secrets of the Rhune massif while enjoying extraordinary views of the Basque Country.

The route starts from the car park located at the end of Chemin Xurien Borda, in the Olhette district towards Ascain, opposite the Trabenia hotel. You start your outing with the short climb of the mountain of Ciboure before heading towards the venta Yasola. The path continues in the woods on a small single track that connects a crossing with two variants of the HRP (Haute Route Pyrénéenne). You take the one that goes up to the Rhune following a small path that intersects the track several times to the foot of the Zizkuitza. Stay a few meters on the track before embarking on a new path which goes to the right and descends mainly for five kilometers to the D406 road.

From now on, a climb of just over six kilometers awaits you to take you to the Rhune passing by the superb ridges of Fageko Harria and Athekaleun. After having taken advantage of the exceptional panorama offered by the summit, you turn back slightly on the track before embarking on a descent which joins the GR 10 which you follow to the outskirts of the village of Sare.

You leave the GR 10 to tackle your last long ascent of the loop. This leads to the Altsanga balcony and its magnificent view of the Basque-Landes coastline. From then on, you join the GR 10 at the Col des Trois Fontaines, stay on the marked trail until you return to your starting point.

name: The belvederes of Ossau – running on the Pic du Midi d'Ossau
name_original: Les belvédères d'Ossau — Courir au pic du Midi d'Ossau
description:
This beautiful and large loop of 34.3 kilometers and 2,300 meters of elevation is intended for trail runners who wish to discover all the secrets of the Ossau valley. This route takes you on a route that delivers the most beautiful views of one of the most emblematic peaks of the Pyrenees, the Pic du Midi d'Ossau.

The starting point of this odyssey is in the free Bious-Oumettes car park. You start with a gradual climb in the forest which continues on beautiful grassy expanses before ending at the Col de Suzon. A pleasant false flat leads you to the Pombie refuge before diving towards the Gave du Brousset.

From the river, the path climbs again on the sides of several peaks that delimit the Franco-Spanish border. From the peak of Astú, a beautiful portion of ridge guides you to the peak of the Moines while dancing on the border. This spectacular summit offers a magnificent panorama over the Ossau valley and the Spanish side.

The trail descends again to Lac de Gentau via the pretty Lac Bersau before climbing back up to the Col d'Ayous. A wonderful new view of the Ossau valley and its lakes is revealed to you. From the pass, you take a new ridge line that runs to the Aule valley, passing through the soum d’Aas and the turon Garié.

This magnificent valley suggests a last glimpse of the Pic du Midi d'Ossau before plunging into the forest and reaching your starting point for images full of your head.

name: Stage 1: Vielle-Aure to La Mongie — Grand Raid des Pyrénées
name_original: Étape 1 : De Vielle-Aure à La Mongie — Grand Raid des Pyrénées en off
description:
This first stage from Vielle-Aure is the shortest of your adventure. Its 30.9 kilometers are mostly in elevation gain in order to cross several mountain passes while meandering among many lakes.

The first flat kilometers towards Saint-Lary allow you to warm up before tackling a first climb of around seven kilometers above the village and its valley. Then, a slight flat area allows you to catch your breath before climbing to the Col de Portet. From there, a slight descent takes you to the heights of the superb Lac de l'Oule.

A long false flat gradually takes you up to the Col de Bastanet while discovering the magnificent Bastan lakes. In the middle is the refuge of the same name which invites you to take a nice break in a sublime setting. Take the opportunity to recharge your water and possibly eat a small snack before completing your climb to the pass. Perched between the peaks of Bastan and Portarras, it reveals a magnificent panorama of the various lakes below that you are about to discover.

The lakes of Hourquette, Campana and Gréziolles follow one another in a descent which extends over almost seven kilometres. Finally you have a short climb, followed by the same distance downhill before reaching the ski resort of La Mongie which marks the end of your stage.

name: Stage 2: La Mongie to Soulom — Grand Raid des Pyrénées
name_original: Étape 2 : De La Mongie à Soulom — Grand Raid des Pyrénées en off
description:
To start this second stage, you will benefit from a slightly descending false flat of a little less than three kilometers as a warm-up. You then begin a long portion of ascent of eight kilometers with 1,200 meters of elevation gain. This takes you to the top of the emblematic Pic du Midi de Bigorre with its observatory and restaurant. Savor the exceptional panorama it offers over a good part of the Pyrenees chain.

The descent is made by turning back to Lake Oncet, then you continue towards Lake Bleu de Lesponne. Between the two bodies of water, the course oscillates without however taking the side of one or the other. A true Pyrenean jewel, the Lac Bleu de Lesponne surprises as much by its beauty as by the view it reveals over the valley below.

A short climb leads you to the Col de Bareilles and its striking panoramas. You then descend to discover Lake Ourrec and its pretty pastoral plateaus. Then you tackle the last real climb of the day, which includes 200 meters of elevation gain. A false flat descending for several kilometers leads you gradually to Hautacam.

From the ski resort, you still have about ten kilometers of descent to the end point of this stage. It is the small village of Soulom that reaches out to you. Attached to that of Pierrefitte-Nestalas, it offers all the amenities and therefore represents a good opportunity to refuel.

name: Stage 3: Soulom to Luz-Saint-Sauveur — Grand Raid des Pyrénées
name_original: Étape 3 : De Soulom à Luz-Saint-Sauveur — Grand Raid des Pyrénées en off
description:
This third stage of your adventure is the longest since it includes 48.9 kilometers including 2,840 meters of elevation gain up to Luz-Saint-Sauveur.

From the first meters you begin an ascent that stretches over more than 13 kilometers with an elevation gain of about 1,700 meters. So take the time in this long climb which does not involve any particular difficulty. It leads you to the foot of the Pic de Cabaliros which you go around before descending towards Cauterets at the level of the Col de Contente.

The beginning of the descent is quite steep and winds through several switchbacks before softening to take you more gently to the beautiful spa town of Cauterets. Here, the possibilities to eat are absolutely not lacking. You're about halfway through the ride, so take the time to recharge the batteries while enjoying yourself.

The rest is expressed in a climb that takes you to the heights of Cauterets before joining the route of the GR 10. Take advantage of the red and white marks of the path to guide you to the Col de Riou. The last part of the climb gets tougher as the switchbacks intensify. Arrived at the top, savor the new view before you descend slightly to the Col de Luz-Ardiden.

From the pass, a new long descent awaits you. Indeed you will lose 1,200 meters of elevation over almost 16 kilometers. This last section takes you to Luz-Saint-Sauveur, which welcomes you in a warm atmosphere to close this stage. Dynamic, the city is full of restaurants and accommodation.

name: El Cenicero and Puerto Lápice Windmills Loop
name_original: Ermita de Valdehierro - El Cenicero - Molinos Puerto Lápice – circular desde Puerto Lápice
description: This moderate 23.6-mile mountain biking loop from Puerto Lápice features varied terrain, challenging descents, and iconic windmills.

name: Stage 4: Luz-Saint-Sauveur to Vielle-Aure — Grand Raid des Pyrénées
name_original: Étape 4 : De Luz-Saint-Sauveur à Vielle-Aure — Grand Raid des Pyrénées en off
description:
This last stage aims to bring you back to the starting point: the village of Vielle-Aure. Overall, the profile is divided into two parts: a long gradual climb to the middle of the course and then a slightly hilly descent to your final destination. This stage comes down to flirting alternately with the GR 10 trail.

The route begins by climbing on the sides of the soum de Nère, with beautiful parts in the forest. The first kilometers follow the departmental road which goes to the Col du Tourmalet, part of it is on the GR 10.

At the hamlet of Tournaboup, you cross the road to enter the magnificent Gaubie valley. You find the GR 10 to sink into this peaceful and preserved valley. Bridges and ruins bear witness to centuries-old pastoral activity as you progress. The slope rises gradually to harden between the huts of Aygues Cluses and the hourquette Nère, the highest point of the stage. At the pass, a magnificent landscape awaits you with superb panoramas of the lakes towards which you begin a technical descent.

As you progress, several lakes are revealed to you, including the superb Port Bielh lake. The rest of the descent plunges into a beautiful forest before revealing the immense Lake Oule. From there, you embark on the last climb of your journey to the Col du Portet. From then on, you follow again the markup of the GR 10 which you left at the edge of Hourquette Nère. It is at the foot of Cap de Pède that you leave it to dive on a track towards your final destination.

You reach the heights of Vielle-Aure via the neighboring village of Vignec. A few hundred meters later, you have arrived at the end of your formidable Pyrenean odyssey. This authentic village offers you a last moment out of time to perfectly end your adventure.

name: Heights of Fontarabie – running in the Basque Country
name_original: Les hauteurs de Fontarabie — Courir au Pays basque
description:
Superb loop of 23 kilometers in the Spanish Basque Country, very close to the French border.
In proportion to the distance, the elevation gain of approximately 650 meters is affordable. Nevertheless, it remains relatively demanding given the terrain.

The route begins at Cape Higuer in the town of Hondarribia, “Hondarribia” in Spanish. This seaside resort faces Hendaye in the bay of Chingoudy. A campsite car park allows you to park a few meters from the starting point.

The first nine kilometers are mostly uphill. The first portion takes tracks and a bit of road that runs through small forests. As you progress, the landscape clears up and very quickly some superb viewpoints open up.
You go around Fort Guadeloupe before climbing onto Mount Erramutz. Take the time to turn around to admire the superb view of the bay.

The path continues to climb up to Jaizkibel, the highest point of your outing. Easily recognizable by its large antenna, it suggests a magnificent 360 degree panorama. You can admire the Atlantic facade and guess its extension which sinks into Spain. On the other side, there are several peaks of the Basque Country including the Rhune.

The first part of the descent is on a rocky section before continuing on a wider track that leads to the coast. This winding portion is really nice. At the cost of short climbs and descents, you discover pretty little coves.

All you have to do is go back up along the coast to find your starting point.
I advise you to take enough water and a small snack, because you will not find anything to sustain yourself on the way.

name: Sara Korrika Trail – running in the Basque Country
name_original: Sara Korrika Trail en off — Courir au Pays basque
description:
The Sara Korrika Trail is a superb race in the Basque Country. Its many participants and the special fervor that reigns there make it a must in the area. Its 22 kilometers, in a loop, survey beautiful ridges and are close to the summit of the Rhune, thus justifying the 1,500 meters of elevation gain. The event is usually held at the end of March, departing from the magnificent village of Sare.

Backed by the Pyrenees chain, Sare is ranked among the most beautiful villages in France. High place of Basque traditions (balls, songs, dances, Basque strength competitions, etc.), this locality is full of authenticity and surprises that make it unique.

Coming back to the course, this one doesn't give you much time to warm up. Indeed, barely two kilometers after the start, the route rises to almost never go down to the top of the Rhune.

Along the way, the trails quickly gain height and take you to a pretty balcony that offers breathtaking views of the Atlantic Ocean. Then, the approach to La Rhune is through the breach of Athekaleun. Less frequented than the main trails, the gap launches you into a last stretch of technical ascent but without great difficulty.

A few meters before the summit of the Rhune, you plunge into a descent of several kilometers which gradually sinks into a beautiful forest. When you reach the departmental road, the last big part of the climb awaits you, including the formidable climb of the emperor which is the subject of a special time on the day of the race.

Finally, you follow a last ridge, that of Fageko Harria, before descending to the village of Sare. This beautiful outing ends by embarking on a Roman road that gently climbs into the city center.

For more information on the race:
https://www.sarakorrika.com/

name: SENPEREKO Trail – running in the Basque Country
name_original: SENPEREKO Trail en off — Courir au Pays basque
description:
The SENPEREKO Trail is a race of approximately 21 kilometers for 1,000 meters of elevation gain which takes place every year at the beginning of March. It leaves from the emblematic village of Saint-Pée-sur-Nivelle.

A worthy representative of the Basque Country with its atypical architecture, "Saint-Pée" enjoys a rich cultural history. From the witch hunt to the birth of the chistera (wicker glove), anecdotes abound.

The route starts in the Ibarrun district, very close to the pala pediment. The route follows good quality tracks and paths. Concretely, it consists of climbing the three main peaks of the area: Suhalmendi, Esnaur and Bizkartun. Each of them offers its share of panoramas over the neighboring valleys, the Rhune or the Atlantic Ocean.

The first kilometers follow a wide track which gradually takes you up to the Suhalmendi, the highest point of the race. A descent leads very close to the Ignace pass before going up towards Esnaur. About three kilometers later, you are again perched. It is the Bizkartun that offers a last quality panorama before starting the final part of your outing.

On the way, do not miss the Uzkaingo Erreka waterfall which suggests a beautiful moment of freshness. You only have a few kilometers left before reaching your starting point.

During the same weekend, the SPUC LASTERKA club also offers the following races:
Tontorrez Tontor, 80 kilometers for about 5,000 meters of elevation gain
Gotorlekuen Itzulia, 42 kilometers with 2,800 meters of elevation gain
Ibar Run, 10 kilometers with 350 meters of elevation gain

https://www.spuclasterka.fr/senpereko-trail/

name: Ursuia's delights – running in the Basque Country
name_original: Les Délices d'Ursuia en off — Courir au Pays basque
description:
Les Délices d'Ursuia is a race of about 21 kilometers for 1,200 meters of elevation gain which has taken place at the beginning of April in Hasparren since 2007. Located at the foot of Mount Ursuia, it is one of the of the most extensive Basque Country with many scattered districts. Although its origins date back to the Roman Empire, Hasparren developed mainly through agriculture and the shoe industry.

The start is in the Urcuray district towards Cambo-les-Bains. The first five kilometers are almost exclusively uphill, until you reach the central element of the route: Mount Ursuia. As you approach its summit, you begin a small descent on a pretty path. The route meanders and oscillates on the sides of the mountain until it really reaches it after 14 kilometers.

From then on, you only have a slight descent left to complete your outing. This passes through the Iralmada pass before following a pretty little stream leading to your starting point.

The Ursu Mendi association has recently offered a shorter format over 12 kilometers with 500 meters of elevation gain, as well as a hike of around 15 kilometers.

You can find more information about the event here:

name: Ridge run – running in the Basque Country
name_original: Course des crêtes en off — Courir au Pays basque
description:
The ridge race is a must for trail running in the Basque Country. Organized for more than 40 years, the event takes place in a festive atmosphere that does not neglect the sporting challenge. The loop of approximately 27 kilometers and 1,000 meters of elevation gain starts from the village of Espelette each year at the end of June - beginning of July.

If Espelette owes its fame to the cultivation of peppers, it is also a pretty little village nestled in the heart of the Basque Country. There are many craftsmen and shops that highlight an exceptional heritage.

Departure is in the town center of the village. The first three kilometers, relatively flat, allow you to warm up before attacking the first ascent towards Mondarrain.
The incline gets steeper as you get closer to the top, but once you get there the views are worth it.

You then follow the side of the Bizkaïluze ridge to the Spanish border before turning around. This time you move directly on the ridge before joining another line that takes you to Mount Errebi. On a sunny day, the panorama extends to the Atlantic Ocean.

From the mountain, there is practically only a descent to Espelette. This takes the Pinodieta pass, surrounded by pepper fields. You then follow a nice, rolling path back to where you started.

During the same weekend, the ASC Napurrak club also offers the following races:
a 45 kilometer race
a 21 kilometer race
a 14 kilometer race
a 7.5 kilometer night run
a 7 kilometer race
a free hike of 15.5 kilometers
a 16.7 kilometer yum-yum walk
a 14 kilometer Nordic walk
You can find more information about the entire event here:
https://www.coursedescretes.com/

name: Baztandarrak – running in the Basque Country
name_original: Baztandarrak en off — Courir au Pays basque
description:
The Baztandarrak is a trail race which has the particularity of changing course almost every year. Therefore, here I offer you the route of the 2022 edition. It is a demanding loop of about 22 kilometers which includes 1,600 meters of elevation gain.

The event generally takes place during the second half of July and starts from the village of Bidarray. Nestled at the foot of the magnificent Iparla ridges, Bidarray offers spectacular views of the surrounding mountains. Atypical village of the Basque Country, there reigns a peaceful and welcoming atmosphere.

From the first meters the route rises uphill following part of the GR 10. After three kilometers a switchover takes place and you take a rolling descent to the river below. Enjoy the path winding through a stunning forest as the path rises sharply thereafter.

A steep climb guides you to the edge of the Espalza pass. Then you take a magnificent path that runs along impressive cliffs. Savor the immensity of the panoramas that are gradually revealed. Take the opportunity to catch your breath because two nice portions await you.

At first you climb on the peak of Iguzki, from which you see your ultimate ascent: the Artzamendi. These two summits give you magnificent 360-degree panoramas over the valleys of the Basque Country.

From the last summit, there is practically only a long downhill portion to the village of Bidarray. Let yourself embark on its pleasant paths while admiring the view it provides. After crossing the river one last time, a very short last climb awaits you before sliding down to the village on a stretch of road.

The Baztandarrak association also offers another race of about 42 kilometers.

For more information on the event:
https://www.baztandarrak.fr/

name: Lakako Lasterkaldia – running in the Basque Country
name_original: Lakako Lasterkaldia en off — Courir au Pays basque
description:
Starting from the small village of Ossès, the Laka race offers a loop of 21 kilometers and about 1,200 meters of elevation gain. In the heart of the Basque Country, in the province of Basse-Navarre, this village is ideally located between Bidarray, Saint-Étienne-de-Baïgorry and Saint-Jean-Pied-de-Port.

The course starts from the center of the village then rises gradually on a track. You do most of the elevation on your first ascent, which really begins after just over a kilometer.

A little before the fifth kilometer, you come out on a beautiful ridge marked by the presence of ruins. You bypass the Haltzamendi and then climb the Laina. You evolve on a new ridge which then leads you to Mount Baïgura, the highest point of your outing at 897 meters above sea level. Savor for a moment the magnificent panorama that opens on the ridges of Iparla, Artzamendi and Rhune.

The route ends with a long and beautiful descent that brings you back to Ossès. You follow several pretty little streams until you find your starting point.

Usually the race takes place during the last week of April. You can find more information here :
https://coursedulaka.net/

name: Skyrhune – running in the Basque Country
name_original: Skyrhune en off — Courir au Pays basque
description:
The Skyrhune is undoubtedly the most emblematic race in the French Basque Country. Registered in the Golden Trail Series, it brings together some of the best trail runners in the world in a popular fervor. From Ascain, it is done in a loop in the Rhune massif. The course is quite demanding since its 21 kilometers include approximately 1,700 meters of elevation gain.

The few hundred meters after the village allow you to warm up before attacking the first ascent to Miramar. This belvedere reveals what awaits you next: the Ciboure mountain and the Rhune.

The descent that follows leads into a pretty undergrowth that you leave to climb the mountain of Ciboure. Again, the Rhune stands in front of you. You descend slightly before attacking its ascent by one of its most demanding slopes. The path takes you on a path that crosses a pretty ridge before reaching a small forest. You only have the last few meters of climb left which rise sharply before reaching the summit.

At the top, the panorama rewards your efforts. You see the Atlantic Ocean on one side, while on the other you guess several peaks of the Pyrenees chain. You are at the highest point of your journey. There is mostly only descent to Ascain.

It begins with a fairly technical portion that includes several bends up to Trois Fontaines. From there, the slope becomes gentler and you just have to unroll to your starting point.

name: Ttutturu trail – running in the Basque Country
name_original: Ttutturu trail en off — Courir au Pays basque
description:
The Ttutturru trail is held in an exceptional environment, among the most remarkable sites in the Basque Country. The competitions they offer are accessible to all, combining discovery, local produce and a good atmosphere.

This pretty loop of 24 kilometers and 1,300 meters of elevation gain starts from the small village of Lecumberry. The route crosses many pastoral areas. In fact, some paths are not permanent or frankly discernible.

The beginning of your outing is mainly uphill to reach the superb peak of Béhorléguy. The last part of its ascent ends on a rocky portion that requires vigilance, without involving any real difficulty. At the top, the view of the Basque Country is breathtaking, you can also see your next objective there: the Hauzkoa.

At a similar altitude to Béhorléguy, this new summit also has its share of striking panoramas. Gradually, you begin your descent which oscillates between the peaks of Mendibil and Harribiribila. Then, all that remains is to find your starting point by rolling down a final portion of pleasant descent.

The event also offers a vertical kilometer in May as well as other formats including a marathon. You can find more information here: http://ttutturutrails.com/accueil/

name: From Gorramendi to Iparla – running in the Basque Country
name_original: Des crêtes de Gorramendi à celles d'Iparla — Courir au Pays basque
description:
This very pretty loop of 24.4 kilometers and 1,500 meters of elevation will take you to conquer the beautiful ridges of the Spanish and French Basque Country. This route is ideal for progressing on technical trails which include an interesting kilometer/elevation ratio. I advise you to leave early in the morning to benefit from a special light that highlights the already magnificent landscapes even more.

The loop starts from a small parking lot at the end of a secondary road coming from the village of Bidarray. The first few meters are done down to the level of the river. A little further on, your first ascent begins. Without real difficulty, it seizes you from the start because it is relatively demanding. The climb runs along a ridge towards Alkaxuri. With a bit of luck, you will be able to observe the vultures perched on the large rocks that litter this portion.

You come out on a vertiginous ridge which offers a magnificent panorama. On one side the Artzamendi, on the other, the ridges of Iparla which are part of the rest of your itinerary. As a real tightrope walker, you continue to the peak of Gorramendi which welcomes you with new viewpoints.

Then, it's a downhill portion that allows you to unroll on a comfortable track before climbing towards the crests of Iparla with the Astate in your sights. A final steep section takes you to the summit which rewards your efforts. Once again, the view is breathtaking. You admire the immense natural wall formed by the ridges of Iparla while seeing the high Pyrenean peaks on the horizon.

This impressive ridge marks the border between France and Spain. You go up it by following the GR 10 path. Its markings facilitate your progress to the peak of Iparla. From then on, a long portion of descent awaits you until you reach your starting point.
It begins on a grassy plateau before taking a wider track that allows you to unroll to end with a small path to the car park.

name: Loop on the heights of Urepel – running in the Basque Country
name_original: Boucle sur les hauteurs d'Urepel — Courir au Pays basque
description:
This beautiful loop of about 23 kilometers and 1,000 meters of elevation gain takes you to the heights of the Basque Country, dancing on the Franco-Spanish border. It starts from the small village of Urepel, nestled in the Aldudes valley.

The first kilometers include little elevation. They allow you to enjoy beautiful views of the village, then go up a beautiful track that rushes into the forest along a peaceful stream. A first steep but short climb takes you above this wooded part. You are about to enter Spain when you find a wider track.

Moving again in a magnificent forest, you make a slight downhill portion before climbing to finally lead to a wide ridge introduced by the Enokerri.

Here, the landscape is discovered and the panoramas open up. A very pleasant trail takes you from peak to peak, such as Artsalgo Gaina and Argintzu. As you advance, the horizon expands and you discover some of the mysteries of the Basque Country.

You conclude this magnificent portion by bypassing the Lohiluze, before plunging into a long downhill section. This rushes into a magnificent and generous forest until it brings you back to your starting point, in the village of Urepel.

name: Loop on the roof of the Basque Country – running in the Basque Country
name_original: Boucle sur le toit du Pays basque — Courir au Pays basque
description:
This magnificent route offers you a trip to the top of the Basque Country while exploring its depths. Departing from Larrau, this great loop of 28.5 kilometers and 1,900 meters of elevation gain is a truly immersive journey in Basque land.

It's simple, from the city center the path continues to rise to the Pic d'Orhy, the highest point in the Basque Country at 2,017 meters above sea level. In the last part of the climb you evolve on a beautiful ridge without any real difficulty. At the top, the 360 degree panorama is spectacular. The Atlantic coast, the Basque Country, Béarn, the Pyrenean peaks: your eyes no longer know where to rest.

You then continue by taking a new, very playful portion of the ridge to the Col de Larrau. From the pass, a superb relatively flat portion allows you to unwind in a grandiose setting. You then begin a long downhill section that slowly sinks into the forest.

You evolve in a completely different but equally striking environment until you reach the Holzarte footbridge. A remarkable natural site, it spans the gorges of Olhadubi, the depth of which is endless. You continue along the gorges of Holzarte which lead you to the road. All you have to do is unwind to the village to conclude this magnificent adventure.

name: Monte Hijedo Forest Trail

name: The forest of Iraty and its heights – running in the Basque Country
name_original: La forêt d'Iraty et ses hauteurs — Courir au Pays basque
description:
The largest beech forest in Europe, the Iraty forest is one of the jewels of the Basque Country. Its 20,000 hectares are divided between France and Spain and bring together many remarkable plant and animal species: beeches, firs, deer, roe deer, etc.

Through this route, I suggest you immerse yourself in this thousand-year-old massif, before taking to the heights to admire its immensity. The loop of 22 kilometers and 1,200 meters of elevation gain starts from the Iraty chalets at the Bagargiak pass. This starting point is ideal since it includes parking, a restaurant, a shop and even the possibility of sleeping there.

From the first meters, the crests of Organbidexka suggest a magnificent panorama of the summit of the Basque Country: the Pic d'Orhy. A short climb takes you to the foot of Odeizugaña before plunging into the mysterious forest of Iraty. You evolve on a beautiful and long portion of descent which winds through the vegetation until you come out on a road.

A first bump devoid of trees is offered to you before crossing the road again. Once crossed, you undertake a long portion of ascent which leads you to a very beautiful ridge. You gradually discover the Mendibel, the Arthanolatze and the Pic des Escaliers, the highest point of the outing. These summits offer their share of vertiginous and infinite panoramas, while overlooking the majestic forest of Iraty.

From the summit a short descent leads you back into the forest. From then on, you only have a short climb left before returning to your starting point at the chalets.

name: Gravel route of upper Cidacos
name_original: Ruta gravel del alto Cidacos
description:
The Route starts and ends in Calahorra and runs through the Alto Cidacos region. From Calahorra it gradually gains height to border mountains such as Gatún or Valdelavia. Due to the distance, 91 kilometres, and the accumulated difference in altitude of 1450 positive metres, it is a demanding route, which runs mainly along tracks and paths.

As soon as you leave the urban center of Calahorra, you pass through the Laguna de la Estanca and then you enter a wooded area, between Aleppo pine and stone groves. After crossing the road from Soria to Tafalla in Autol, a long ascent of more than 17 kilometers begins to the Yerga mountains, between holm oak and kermes oak groves. Make sure you carry enough water and provisions in Autol, since a section of more than 40 kilometers awaits you in which you will not pass through any population or source.

After passing very close to the summit of Mount Gatún, you begin the descent towards Vilarroya. The route reaches this town practically with the sole mission of being able to supply you, since you have 20 kilometers ahead of you until Arnedo in which you will not find services. Leaving Vilarroya, you pedal northwest, always with a view of the imposing Isasa rock on the horizon.

After Arnedo, take a stretch of the Cidacos greenway to the castle of Quel. From here and until the end of the route in Calahorra, you cross cultivated fields in a gentle descent, and, shortly before reaching your destination, you pass next to the Perdiguero reservoir.

name: Cantejeira Waterfall Trail
name_original: Cascada de Cantejeira
description: Hike to the stunning Cascada de Cantejeira, an easy 1.9-mile (3.1 km) route perfect for families, featuring a beautiful waterfall and villag

name: Ocón and Jubera valleys – Mymerak gravel routes
name_original: Ruta gravel de los valles de Ocón y de Jubera
description:
The valleys of the Ocón and Jubera rivers are the protagonists of this Route. With 57 kilometers in length, this Route presents an undulating profile that in total adds more than 1000 meters of accumulated positive slope. It does not have big climbs except in the final part, where you approach the Sierra de la Hez. It is the last ascent before descending to return to Tudelilla, the origin and end of the Route.

Parts of Tudelilla between fields of vines and olive groves gently rising towards the Ocón valley. In this valley you visit the towns of Molinos de Ocón and Pipaona. The route separates them by taking a small detour between the dry crops in the area.

After leaving the Ocón valley you head west, towards the Jubera valley. Here the landscape mixes the scrublands of deciduous trees such as walnuts and poplars with vineyards and cereals. It is worth making the short detour that marks the route to see the reconstruction of a windmill, not very common in the area.

Arriving at Santa Engracia de Jubera marks the entrance to the Jubera valley, whose course you follow north until you reach the small village of Ventas Blancas, where the route turns east towards Galilea and back to Pipaona, where the most important climb of the day begins.

The foothills of the Sierra de la Hez mark a short and hard climb, barely three and a half kilometres, before beginning the final descent to return to Tudelilla.

name: Bilbao Green Ring MTB Loop
name_original: Parque Venta Alta – circular por Gangoiti y Periurbano Arraizmedi desde Bibao
description: Explore the Parque Venta Alta – circular por Gangoiti y Periurbano Arraizmedi, a moderate 18.4-mile mountain biking route near Bilbao.

name: Gravel tour around Peña Soto
name_original: Vuelta gravel a la peña Soto
description:
With only 37.4 kilometers and 1,000 meters of accumulated positive elevation gain, this is a demanding Route, marked by two major ascents. The first and most important one surrounds the Peña Soto, an enormous mass of conglomerate that houses an important colony of vultures on its steep walls. The second ascent starts from Albelda in the direction of Clavijo and marks the final descent to Ribafrecha, the origin and end of the Route.

The Route begins in Ribafrecha, in the middle of the alluvial plain of the Leza River, an area of abundant dryland crops characterized by forming intricate mosaics. With Mount Laturce always on your left, you advance across the plain, first crossing La Unión, until you head south to gradually climb up to Peña Soto.

Before you go through Clavijo, whose imposing castle looks out into the void from the rock of the same name. In Clavijo it is a good idea to get supplies, since until Nalda, the next town on the Route, you will not find any type of service.

After Clavijo you continue going up tracks surrounded by pine forests, until you almost surround the Soto rock. After the descent to Nalda, its castle awaits you, which crowns the town right in its center. From Nalda to Albelda de Iregua, you pedal along the Iregua Roman road, which runs along the river that gives it its name.

From Albelda a short ascent begins, barely three kilometers, but intense. The climb leads to Clavijo, but you leave it at the Cuatro Caminos crossroads. There you turn left to take a paved path that takes you back to La Unión. From there you take a dirt track gently downhill to Ribafrecha, the end of the Route.

name: Paseo de los Molinos Trail
name_original: Paseo de los Molinos – ida y vuelta desde Folgoso de la Ribera
description: Enjoy an easy 3.3-mile (5.2 km) hike on the Paseo de los Molinos Trail, featuring historical mills and a scenic waterfall.

name: Mymerak gravel route of the monasteries and pilgrims
name_original: Ruta Mymerak gravel de los monasterios y peregrinos
description:
The Route, which starts from Ventosa, has 77 kilometers and 1,100 meters of positive elevation gain and offers you a tour of the Rioja Alta region. In its first part, the route follows the French Camino de Santiago to the west, until it turns south, to visit the two monasteries located in San Millán de la Cogolla. The return is made through the towns of Badarán and Cárdenas, until returning to Ventosa.

You leave Ventosa to the northwest to immediately take the French Camino de Santiago in a westerly direction to Cirueña. At the height of Nájera you briefly leave the Camino to enter the town and when you leave it enjoy a path that runs parallel to the Najerilla river.

In Cirueña the route heads south, in a gentle ascent towards the first foothills of the Sierra de la Demanda. The climb is progressive, with few hard ramps. Two magnificent monasteries await you in San Millán de la Cogolla, Suso and Yuso.

Before going back to the east, there is one last climb to Alto de Villaverde to face 10 kilometers of descent between cultivated fields. Halfway down this section of descent you pass through Badarán, a good place to get supplies and prepare for the final section of the Route.

Once past Cárdenas, where it is advisable to stop at its source, you cross the Najerilla River again and begin a gentle ascent towards Cerro Alto. Once you overcome this last obstacle, you will only have three kilometers left until the end of the route in Ventosa.

name: Mymerak gravel routes to the roof of La Rioja
name_original: Rutas Mymerak gravel al techo de La Rioja
description:
With 69 kilometers and 1,800 meters of accumulated positive elevation gain, this Route will delight climbers, as it includes long and demanding climbs. It starts from Ezcaray to climb almost to the top of Mount San Lorenzo, the roof of La Rioja. The return to Ezcaray is almost all by track. It runs through hills that surround some wonderful valleys, until in its final stretch it enters a beautiful beech forest that accompanies you until the end of the journey.

The first kilometers after leaving Ezcaray are very flat. You head north along the greenway of Oja to the height of Santurde de la Rioja. There you turn east to take a narrow secondary road that gains altitude towards the town of Pazuengos.

After this town, the asphalt becomes a well-compacted dirt track. From here you find the tougher ramps, but then you have some rest before the big climb of the day. The ascent to Mount San Lorenzo is done by track, it is 11 kilometers long and begins by going up through a narrow valley that when it reaches the top becomes a spectacular pass before turning north again.

On the way back you pass the Valdezcaray ski resort. There the gravel track becomes a road for a few kilometers until the route returns to the tracks. The final descent back to Ezcaray is through a beautiful beech forest where you will pedal along a narrow paved forest track.

name: Mymerak gravel route from Haro
name_original: Ruta Mymerak gravel desde Haro
description:
This Route runs through the western part of the Haro region and does not have great unevenness: in its 70 kilometers it barely accumulates 800 meters of positive unevenness. The route crosses the typical mosaic of crops that combines dry land, vineyards and leafy poplars. In addition, the area is crossed by numerous watercourses such as the Ea river, the Tirón, the Oja, as well as the Ebro river, which you find next to Haro.

Parts of Haro in a southwesterly direction following the course of the Tirón river. You cross numerous towns such as Anguciana, Casalarreina, or Leiva, so you will not have any difficulties getting supplies or finding a rest area.

In Leiva, where it is advisable to stop to see its magnificent rectangular castle, the route heads north first and then east. Again you cross numerous towns, such as Treviana, Fonzaleche or Sajazarra. In Sajazarra you cannot miss its walled castle, now converted into a winery.

Before returning to Haro, the Route heads towards the Sierra de Toloño / Montes Obarenes, the natural border between La Rioja, the Basque Country and Castilla y León. There, after crossing Villalba de Rioja, you ascend briefly to surround some of the rocks and hills in the area, before starting the return towards Haro.

name: Mymerak gravel route between wine cellars
name_original: Ruta Mymerak gravel entre bodegas de vino
description:
The 80 kilometers of this Route will bring you closer to some of the most famous wineries in La Rioja and the Basque Country. The circular route follows the contours of the southern sierras of Álava to the east and returns to the starting point in Haro following the Ebro river.

You start from Haro and go up for about 13 kilometers towards the Toloño mountain, from where you can admire the views of the Ebro valley. On the descent towards San Vicente de la Sonsierra you pass by the medieval necropolis of the Bardallo graves, a peculiar sample of life in these parts in the 10th century.

On your way east you pass by wineries whose architecture is as important as the quality of their wines: the Baigorri winery and the Ysios winery will not leave you indifferent. It is also worth stopping at the town of La Hoya, an archaeological site that includes a small museum.

In the town of El Ciego there is another sample of spectacular architecture, the Elciego wineries, designed by Frank Gehry. The return to Haro, following the course of the Ebro river, passes by two castles, that of Davalillo and the castle-fortress of San Vicente de la Sonsierra. The last kilometers, very flat and next to the river, take you back to Haro, the end of the Route.

name: Route 6: Taillon – 15 peaks over 3,000 metres in the Pyrenees
name_original: Circuit 6 : Le Taillon — 15 sommets de plus de 3 000 mètres dans les Pyrénées
description:
The Pic du Taillon is one of the easiest peaks to access at over 3,000 meters in the Pyrenees. It culminates at 3,144 meters above sea level on the Franco-Spanish border in the Mont-Perdu massif.
Le Taillon offers a breathtaking view of several remarkable sites in the Pyrenees.

The start of the route is at the Col des Tentes car park located upstream of the Gavarnie ski resort. The first section remains relatively flat until you reach the waterfall of the Taillon glacier. Be careful if some snow remains when you cross the waterfall. The climb becomes steeper on rocky terrain which leads you to the Sarradets refuge. Recently renovated, it suggests a very nice break with a direct view of the famous Roland Breach and further on the Cirque de Gavarnie and its huge waterfall. Take the opportunity to fill up with water before climbing into the breach.

This imposing wall marks the border with Spain. When you cross it, a completely different landscape is offered to you and you can discern the Ordesa canyon in the distance. You walk along the cliffs of the breach on the Spanish side towards Le Taillon. You pass a large rocky mound nicknamed the Taillon finger, then the final ascent begins.

At the top, the spectacle is simply grandiose. The diversity of natural elements seems unlimited: the Cirque de Gavarnie, the Breche de Roland, Mont Perdu, the Ordesa Canyon and the Vignemale.

This hike can be done in one day, but you can absolutely sleep at the Sarradets refuge or bivouac at the top of Taillon. I strongly recommend it to offer you an unforgettable sunrise and sunset. You can also find a few pitches at the foot of the Breche de Roland, on the Spanish side.

name: Route 11: Pic du Perdiguère – 15 peaks over 3,000 metres in the Pyrenees
name_original: Circuit 11 : Le pic du Perdiguère — 15 sommets de plus de 3 000 mètres dans les Pyrénées
description:
The Perdiguère peak stands on the border between Spain and France at 3,222 meters above sea level. The highest peak in the Luchonnais, it offers a magnificent panorama. This hike allows you to climb several peaks exceeding 3,000 meters around the peak.

I suggest a route that starts from the Granges d'Astau in the Oô valley. From the free car park, take a track that goes up through the forest towards Lake Oô. This site is one of the most visited in the Pyrenees, which is quickly understood when you discover the beauty of this place. The waterfall that feeds the lake helps to make the setting even more majestic.

The climb continues, still meandering through the forest which opens at the Col d'Espingo. Below are the refuge and the lake of the same name. You continue in the direction of Lake Saussat via a pretty plateau of summer pastures. From there, the path rises again to reach the Portillon refuge. It is ideally located for a welcome break after a long stretch of climb. The refuge offers a surprising new setting, with its lake in the foreground, the impressive Aiguille du Litérole, and your objective: the Perdiguère.

Here I suggest a loop that takes you to the lower Litérole pass then to the spectacular Litérole needle. Several peaks over 3,000 meters follow one another here: Litérole point (3,132 meters), Royo peak (3,121 meters), Royo point (3,098 meters), Litérole tuca (3,095 meters). Be sure to stay on the ridge and not move on its sides. This passage requires you to have a mountain foot so as not to be impressed by some dizzying passages. By staying on the path, you will not encounter any major difficulties.

Like a tightrope walker, you walk on the ridge that delimits the Franco-Spanish border. Your progress gradually brings you closer to the final ascent to Perdiguère. Less vertiginous, it nevertheless includes some steep passages. At the top, the spectacle is breathtaking, the Aneto and the Posets, the highest Pyrenean peaks are revealed to you. You can also see the Pic de Maupas or the Pic Lézat.

For the return, you turn back to the upper Litérole pass which is at the foot of the Tuca de Litérole. You then dive into the upper valley of Litérole to head towards the Lac du Portillon. This is the normal way to reach the Perdiguère. It leads directly to the summit without taking the Aiguille du Litérole is its 3,000 meters. You then go around the lake to find the path that slides to the refuge. From there, the return route follows the same route as you took on the outward journey.

This hike is flexible as desired. Indeed, although achievable in one day, it remains reserved for the most seasoned. I strongly advise you to stop at the Portillon refuge, or to bivouac at the lakes of Espingo or Saussat. The passage over the Aiguille du Litérole does not involve any major difficulty, but does require some experience in the mountains. You can also access Perdiguère by going directly to the Litérole upper pass.

name: Peyresourde Pass – emblematic passes of the Pyrenees
name_original: Circuit 2 : Col de Peyresourde — Les cols emblématiques des Pyrénées
description:
The Col de Peyresourde is an emblematic passage in the Pyrenees and a classic of the Tour de France. Since 1910, it has been climbed more than 60 times by runners on the great loop. Culminating at 1,589 meters above sea level, it is a historic passage between Luron, Larboust and Luchon. The pass is located between Bagnères-de-Luchon and Arreau and provides access to the resort of Peyragudes and its altiport.

For this itinerary, I suggest a departure and an arrival from Bagnères-de-Luchon. This beautiful spa town includes an SNCF train station. From the first meters you are launched to attack the pass. The climb of about 14 kilometers passes through several villages and hamlets during a section with low elevation. It is the end of the climb that includes the highest percentages in order to hoist you up to the pass. Enjoy the beautiful view of the valley that the last meters grant before reaching the summit.

If your goal is just to pin this mythical pass to your list, you can turn around to find Bagnères. Here I suggest you continue in order to find another beautiful climb less known and less frequented. For this, you make the switch and dive towards Loudenvielle through a beautiful descent. Once at the bottom, you go around the lake through the village of Génos before undertaking the second ascent of the day, the one that leads to the Col d'Azet.

The climb is just over seven kilometers long and includes some passages at more than 13%. This one has several switchbacks that quickly take you above the valley and its lake. The Col d'Azet culminates at 1,580 meters, it therefore offers a remarkable landscape throughout its ascent but also at its summit.


The descent is on the same side as your previous ascent. Returning to the valley, I suggest you take a welcome break in the peaceful village of Loudenvielle. The shores of Lac de Génos are also ideal for a pleasant moment of rest. You will actually need to regain your strength to attack the third and final climb of the day. That of the Col de Peyresourde taking its other side. Back at the pass, take advantage of the beautiful descent which takes you back to your starting point.

name: Ruta del Zarzalar Loop
name_original: Ruta de El Zarzalar — circular desde Nerpio
description: Hike the moderate Ruta del Zarzalar Loop, a 5.6-mile trail in Nerpio offering diverse terrain and stunning canyon views along the Taibilla R

name: Luz-Ardiden – emblematic passes of the Pyrenees
name_original: Circuit 10 : Luz-Ardiden — Les cols emblématiques des Pyrénées
description:
Luz-Ardiden is a beautiful climb in the Pyrenees and one of the busiest in the Tour de France since 1985. Its many switchbacks wind their way up to the ski resort of the same name which is 1,720 meters above sea level. The start of the course is in the center of Luz-Saint-Sauveur. Dynamic and charming, this mountain village is also at the foot of the Col du Tourmalet.

As a warm-up, I suggest you embark at the bottom of the valley. On the outskirts of Argelès-Gazost, I invite you to turn around to present yourself at the foot of the ascent classified out of category. The first three kilometers remain affordable before the slope does not rise any more seriously.

It is the middle of the climb that includes the largest percentages. The last section, it is done by borrowing many laces. Enjoy little by little, the view that opens on the horizon. Once you reach the top, you enjoy the masterpiece you have just walked.
You will find something to eat there before undertaking the descent by the same road until you find the center of Luz-Saint-Sauveur.

name: Hautacam – emblematic passes of the Pyrenees
name_original: Circuit 11 : Hautacam — Les cols emblématiques des Pyrénées
description:
The ascent of Hautacam tends to become a classic of the Pyrenees and the Tour de France. The Tour de France took it for the first time in 1994, before repeating the passages there since. Culminating at 1,520 meters above sea level, it shades the Tramassel pass which is a few meters further on.

The route starts at the SNCF station in Lourdes. Known worldwide for its religious history, the city therefore offers a wide choice of accommodation and restaurants. The route begins with a relatively flat section that runs along the Gave de Pau via a greenway. You make a loop to the village of Soulom before descending into the valley to find the foot of the ascent. It officially begins in the village of Ayros-Arbouix.

The climb is one of the toughest around. Indeed, the many breaks in the slope make it impossible to maintain a regular rhythm. As you progress on an irregular slope, the panorama opens on the Azun valley and you can see the peaks of the Vignemale massif. The end of the climb takes place in a superb pastoral setting.

This ascent being a dead end, you take the same path to descend. When you reach the bottom of the valley, all you have to do is follow the greenway which takes you quietly back to Lourdes.

name: Aubisque-Soulor – emblematic passes of the Pyrenees
name_original: Circuit 4 : Aubisque-Soulor — Les cols emblématiques des Pyrénées
description:
The Aubisque and Soulor passes are two of the most emblematic passes in the Pyrenees and the Tour de France. The first was climbed for the first time in 1910 while the second had to wait until 1935 before entering the history of the great loop. I suggest you discover them as part of a loop that starts from Louvie-Juzon which is at the gates of the Ossau valley.

The first portion up to Laruns is on a slightly rising false flat which allows you to warm up before undertaking the mythical ascent. From the charming village, the climb begins. It extends over more than 16 kilometers crossing the spa town of Eaux-Bonnes then the ski resort of Gourette. The last part includes the most important percentages.
Perched at 1,709 meters above sea level, you are at the highest point of the route.

A magnificent section then takes you to the Col du Soulor. You borrow a balcony which offers spectacular sights on the circus of Lithor below. You then have to overcome a final climb to reach the 1,474 meters of altitude of Soulor. The descent takes place in the very beautiful valley of Ferrières then continues along the Ouzom. In the village of Arthez-d'Asson you leave the main axis to embark on a less busy road which brings you back to your starting point.

name: Guadiana Natural Trail

name: Somport Pass – emblematic passes of the Pyrenees
name_original: Circuit 15 : Le col du Somport — Les cols emblématiques des Pyrénées
description:
Located on the border between France and Spain, the Col du Somport peaks at 1,632 meters above sea level. It was climbed in 1991 for the first time by the riders of the Tour de France. It is above the tunnel of the same name which is an important crossing point of the Pyrenees.

The departure is at the SNCF station in the village of Bedous, located in the heart of the magnificent and historic Aspe valley. Due to its location, this valley often played a key role in the Franco-Spanish wars. This route, which goes back in time, has the particularity of going back and forth to the Col de Somport. The beginning of the route therefore goes back to the bottom of the valley.

I suggest a detour to Lescun. This magnificent village nestled in a glacial cirque is reached at the cost of a short but relatively steep climb. You then find the bottom of the valley by descending by the same route. It is in the village of Urdos that the ascent to the Col de Somport really begins. Along the way, Peilhou Lake suggests a pleasant break in a magnificent natural setting.

The last section of the climb includes the most demanding percentages. At the end of a last effort you arrive at the top of the cross-border pass. Savor the beautiful views it offers before returning to your starting point along the same route.

name: From Facture-Biganos to Bordeaux
name_original: De Facture-Biganos à Bordeaux
description:
The smell you will smell when you get off the train: it is the stationery of Facture-Biganos, depending on the direction of the wind, you can even smell this smell as far as Bordeaux on certain days.

Paradoxically, you will go in the opposite direction to which Bordeaux is located. This allows you to avoid a very busy axis and to engage in singles that will put your arms to the test until Pierroton.

Once out of this immense straight line, you can let go and have fun in a long sequence of singles at Cestas level, these parts are not too technical, they are playful to allow you to apprehend good sensations in gravel.

A section then continues along the Eau Bourde which runs into the Garonne.

Crossing the Parc du Moulin d’Ornon and the Priory of Cayac, you enter a walking area, so be respectful of the walkers who find themselves there.

This part is a real green thread that leads to the gates of Bordeaux.

name: Marie-Blanque Pass – emblematic passes of the Pyrenees
name_original: Circuit 6 : Le col de Marie-Blanque — Les cols emblématiques des Pyrénées
description:
Marie-Blanque is a mythical pass in the Pyrenees and a historic part of the Tour de France. Since 1978, it has been crossed fifteen times by the big loop. Uniting the beautiful valleys of Ossau and Aspe, it culminates at 1,035 meters above sea level. This rural pass owes its name to the Egyptian vulture that inhabits the region.

The start is in the town center of Louvie-Juzon, a charming village at the gates of the Ossau valley. The route plunges into the valley for four kilometers before embarking on the ascent of the pass. The first laces lead to the Bénou plateau which allows you to catch your breath before tackling the final portion of the climb.

With the exception of a first hairpin, the descent slides almost straight into the bottom of a valley which leads to the village of Escot. You will find a beautiful balcony road that winds up to Lurbe-Saint-Christau. From there, you take an uncrowded and hilly road which takes you to the gates of Louvie-Juzon. Along the way, a slight climb awaits you up to the Maquis du Bager stele.
