name: Loop 3: Lizarrieta and Lizuniaga passes from Sare – Basque Country passes
name_original: Circuit 3 : Les cols de Lizarrieta et Lizuniaga depuis Sare — Cols du Pays basque
description:
From the village of Sare to Exaltar via Bera, this route takes you to meet authentic and charming villages of the French and Spanish Basque Country. Two passes are added to the program to complete this discovery with beautiful panoramas.

The start is in the center of Sare, considered one of the most beautiful villages in France. Nestled at the foot of the Rhune, Sare has a warm architecture and atmosphere. From the village, the first kilometers are on a slight uphill false flat. The slope then intensifies at the edge of a beautiful forest. .

The climb to Lizarrieta is easy with a maximum passage of 6.3%. The pass marks the separation between France and Spain. A beautiful descent including several hairpins leads to the picturesque village of Exaltar. Animated by many traditions, such as wood pigeon hunting, it is worth exploring.

You continue on a hilly section which leads to the village of Bera. Here begins the second ascent of the day to the Col de Lizuniaga. About four kilometers long, the climb includes passages a little higher than the previous pass. Arrived at the top take the time to appreciate the view before making the switch towards France to find the village of Sare.

name: Loop 2: Passes around Ascain – Basque Country passes
name_original: Circuit 2 : Les cols autour d'Ascain — Cols du pays basque
description:
This pretty loop of about thirty kilometers offers a route that takes accessible passes in the surroundings of Ascain. This route criss-crosses the valleys between France and Spain, completely skirting the Rhune, the emblematic peak of the region.

The start is in the authentic little village of Ascain. Its architecture and atmosphere are ideal for starting and ending a great outing in the Basque Country. The first kilometers are on hilly terrain which then leads to the ascent to the Col d'Ibardin.

The climb, relatively regular, includes short passages above 10%. Arrived at the pass, you switch to Spain, which explains the presence of many shops called ventas. Take the opportunity to take a break to refuel while enjoying the beautiful view of the bay of Saint-Jean-de-Luz. Then comes the beautiful descent punctuated by beautiful turns which leads to the village of Bera. From there you continue on a new climb to the Col de Lizuniaga.

Shorter than the previous one, it announces an imminent return to France. Shortly after the flip, you're there. A pleasant descent leads to the picturesque village of Sare, nestled at the foot of the Rhune. The small pass of Saint-Ignace separates you from your return to Ascain. Simple formality after the two previous ones, it remains very significant. At the top, an inn allows you to enjoy a last break before completing this outing. If your schedule allows it, you can also take the little train there to discover the summit of La Rhune.

The last downhill portion begins in the forest before you find the first houses in the village of Ascain.

name: Loop 1: The Jaizkibel – Basque Country passes
name_original: Circuit 1 : Le Jaizkibel — Cols du Pays basque
description:
This small loop of around thirty kilometers takes place entirely in the Spanish Basque Country. Starting from Fontarrabie (or Hondarribia in Basque) it runs along the foothills of the Jaizkibel massif before flirting with its summit.

The start is located on the outskirts of the maritime town of Hondarribia. Separated from Hendaye by the bay of Txingui, it is at the gates of the Cantabrian Sea at the mouth of the Bidassoa. Its architecture and atypical houses are worth a stroll.

The route runs along the Jaizkibel massif, meandering at its feet on small roads with little traffic. A small bump allows you to warm up a bit before returning to Pasaia Donibane. You reach this small bucolic village by its north bank. If you have time for a visit, a boat takes you to the southern part of the village and its authentic main street.

It is on the heights of Pasaia Donibane that your ascent to Jaizkibel begins. The classic climb is just over seven kilometers long and includes an average of 6%. The beginning of the ascent is the steepest part while the last meters are the easiest. In the middle, a flat area of a few meters allows you to catch your breath.

Arrived at the top of the pass, you can make a small detour to reach the summit, easily recognizable with its antennas. You are on the westernmost mountain range of the Pyrenees. The view extends from the chain and its first mountains before losing itself off to the Atlantic Ocean.

The downhill portion is very nice. It delivers its share of panoramas over France and the town of Hondarribia which you are about to join to complete this pretty loop between land and sea.

name: Loop 4: Otxondo Pass – Basque Country passes
name_original: Circuit 4 : Col d’Otxondo — Cols du Pays basque
description:
This loop starting from Ainhoa offers a beautiful and authentic excursion in the Spanish Basque Country. From the intimacy of its valleys to the beauty of its panoramas, you will discover a beautiful sample.

The route starts from the center of the village of Ainhoa, nestled at the foot of the Spanish border. Its architecture faithful to the Basque Country and its small shops give it an incomparable charm. Quickly, the road rises towards Dantxaria and its ventas (shops).

It is at the exit of this first Spanish village that the ascent for the Otxondo pass really begins. Regular, she understands few difficulties. As you progress, the panorama opens on the Rhune to your right. When you enter the forest, the slope becomes gentler until Otxondo. At the pass, some vestiges of fortifications remain as well as a beautiful picnic area.

In the descent, be sure to turn right on a road that climbs slightly before embarking on a section as unfrequented as it is magnificent. Crisscrossing among several preserved peaks and mountains, you evolve in a remarkable environment. A new climb, in two stages, includes some difficult percentages before undertaking a nice descent towards Etxalar.

In the village, take the time to take a break to appreciate its architecture before setting off to attack the last pass of the day, that of Lizarrieta. A few hairpins punctuate this climb, the maximum percentages of which barely exceed 6%. At the top, enjoy the view before falling into a long descent that begins in the forest. The valley opens up as you approach Ainhoa.

name: Loop 5: Ispéguy Pass – Basque Country passes
name_original: Circuit 5 : Col d’Ispéguy — Cols du Pays basque
description:
This loop from Itxassou offers an incredible trip to the heart of the Basque Country. Shared between France and Spain, the route covers a beautiful diversity of landscapes and terrains.

The route starts in the center of the charming village of Itxassou, at the foot of its pediment, a few meters from an excellent restaurant. Quickly, you plunge into beautiful gorges where the Nive flows. The first part of the route is like going up the stream on a very beautiful road. A few steep slopes may surprise you and announce the color.

Arrived at Bidarray you embark on the road which leads to Saint-Étienne-de-Baïgorry in a superb valley. At the foot of the Iparla ridges, the Col de Gastangarte is more than a warm-up. Some passages flirt with 18%. However, as you progress, the landscapes never cease to amaze.

You then arrive at the village of Saint-Étienne-de-Baïgorry where the longest ascent of the route begins towards the Col d'Ispéguy. Classic of the Basque Country, this pass has seen the Tour de France take it a few times. Relatively open, it allows you to enjoy the view throughout the climb. Regular, it does not include real difficulties. The summit, straddling the border, offers beautiful views of the French and Spanish sides. The hostel there allows you to take a very pleasant break and recharge the batteries for the future.
 
The descent includes several switchbacks at the start before softening and heading towards the village of Erratzu. a fountain allows you to fill the cans if you have not stopped earlier. A little after the village, when you join the departmental road, begins the second ascent of the day towards the Otxondo pass. Its slopes, which are gentler and straighter, allow you to appreciate the view of the Gorramendi which stands out on your right.

Arrived at the top, a beautiful picnic area invites you to a gentle shady break before continuing on a beautiful descent that leads to Dantxaria. Last Spanish village, it induces your imminent return to France. A last hilly section closes the route before returning to Itxassou.

name: La Mota Castle and Rueda Vineyards Loop
name_original: Nava del Rey desde Medina del Campo – circular por Mojados y Matapozuelos
description: This moderate 60.6-mile road cycling route from Medina del Campo explores the rural Valladolid province, passing through Nava del Rey, Mojad

name: Pontón Alto Reservoir Loop
name_original: Presa del Pontón Alto — circular
description: Hike the moderate 5.3-mile Pontón Alto Reservoir Loop in Spain, offering reservoir views and historical landmarks.

name: Xicu 3 and Aymerigalls
name_original: Xicu 3 & Aymerigalls
description:
On this tour you need strong nerves, excellent driving skills and a bit of courage. Because the trail program includes: Xicu 3 and Aymerigalls.

These two descents are among the trickiest in the entire area. A fireworks display of technical hurdles awaits you that need to be overcome. Sometimes you will drive down endless stone slabs and look for the best line. Other times you race across the dry forest floor, which is peppered with roots and loose stones.

Look forward to exciting, spectacular and wild trails that make this round a very special mountain bike adventure. Anyone who masters all parts with flying colors will not only be pumped full of adrenaline, but will also arrive back at the starting point full of happiness hormones.

name: Bolets and Kangur
name_original: Bolets & Kangur
description:
The trails on the menu for this varied mountain bike adventure are: Bolets, Kangur and La Llosa.

You start at the parking lot right at the entrance to San Salvador Park and wind your way up a pleasant gravel road until you reach the entrance to Bolets at around 380 meters above sea level.

Experienced mountain bikers who like to circle themselves and their bike over tricky stone fields will love this challenging route. With your eyes shining and pumped full of adrenaline, the trail spits you out on a gravel road.

Then you pedal hard again. After a few meters in altitude there is a downhill that promises pure fun. The Kangur Trail is technical and scores points above all with a spectacular route over huge boulders and large stone slabs.

Once at the bottom, you pedal again up a steep gravel path to the big finale of this round. Things get rough and fast on the La Llosa Trail before you roll comfortably back to the starting point.

name: Dragon Khan and Spiderman
name_original: Dragon Khan & Spiderman
description:
The main characters of this round are Dragon Khan and Spiderman. What sounds like a well-known roller coaster and a superhero are the names of two mountain bike trails in the MTB hotspot of Santa Coloma de Farners. Both are extremely popular and are among the top trails in this region.

You start the tour on a wide gravel road that initially climbs gently, but becomes a bit nasty towards the end. After this short ramp you will reach the entrance to the Dragon Khan Trail, which is about 280 meters above the starting point, and is particularly impressive because of its versatility. Here you race down fast passages at full throttle and occasionally overcome tricky sections that require your full concentration. In short: This route has everything a mountain biker's heart desires.

And if you think it can't be topped, wait until you reach the Spiderman Trail, which winds its way down the slope for almost three kilometers with plenty of flow. You definitely shouldn't miss this signature trail. It promises pure riding fun for MTB newbies. And all experienced bikers simply take on the many small jumps, which gives the trail a very special flavor. But don't worry: you can simply skip the jumps or avoid them if you prefer to keep your tires on the ground. The trail is definitely fun, we promise!

name: Chorro de Navafría and Refugios Loop
name_original: Chorro de Navafría y Mirador de las Cebadillas — circular por el Refugio de la Fragua: Parque Natural Sierra Norte de Guadarrama
description: This moderate 7.0-mile hike in Parque Natural Sierra Norte de Guadarrama features the Chorro de Navafría Waterfall and panoramic views.

name: Vía Verde del Eresma Trail
name_original: Vía Verde Valle de Eresma — ida y vuelta desde Hontanares de Eresma
description: Hike the easy 4.8-mile (7.8 km) Vía Verde del Eresma Trail, exploring diverse landscapes and old railway history near Hontanares de Eresma.

name: Attack from Castillo de La Mota – circular by Las Salinas and Medina del Campo
name_original: Ataquines desde Castillo de La Mota – circular por Las Salinas y Medina del Campo
description: This moderate 38.1-mile road cycling route circles Castillo de La Mota, offering scenic views and historical landmarks near Medina del Campo

name: Hikes from Castronuño
name_original: Alaejos desde Castronuño – circular por Nava del Rey y el río Duero
description: A moderate 45.1-mile road cycling route from Castronuño, offering scenic Duero River views and passing through a nature reserve.

name: Loop 6: Artzamendi from Espelette – Basque Country passes
name_original: Circuit 6 : L'Artzamendi depuis Espelette — Cols du Pays basque
description:
classic from the Basque Country. At the top, the panorama stretches from the Pyrenees to the Atlantic.

The start is in the world famous village of Espelette. The facades of the houses are decorated with peppers at the origin of its fame. A few kilometers after the start stands in front of you the Col de Légaré. More than a warm-up, this pass puts you in the entry bath with passages reaching 20%.

A slight descent allows you to recover a little before tackling the "Basque hell". The climb begins with low percentages before seriously hardening in the final kilometers. You find portions that flirt with 19% before the last meters calm down a bit.

From the top the view rewards all your efforts. Indeed, beyond the Basque mountains, you can guess the high Pyrenean peaks in the distance. On the other side, the infinity of the Atlantic Ocean stretches out to the horizon. Savor these beautiful panoramas before descending.

You start by following the same route as for the climb before continuing until you reach the banks of the Nive. A very beautiful portion surveys a canyon dug by the stream in the direction of Itxassou. From the village a last hilly section guides your return to Espelette.

name: Senda del Oso
name_original: Senda del Oso desde Cervera de Pisuerga — circular por Parque Natural Montaña Palentina
description: Mountain bike the Senda del Oso, a moderate 8.4-mile route in Parque Natural Montaña Palentina, featuring tunnels and river views.

name: Loop 13: Col d'Osquich – Basque Country passes
name_original: Circuit 13 : Col d’Osquich — Cols du Pays basque
description:
This 60 kilometer loop crosses emblematic villages of the Basque Country. In between, the Col d'Osquich allows you to take a little height and enjoy pretty views of the region.

It is in the former capital of Navarre, in Saint-Palais, that the route begins. You embark in the direction of Mauléon-Licharre on a road with little traffic. Hilly, it crosses at the foot of Oïhergaïn, Leizeaga Mendi and Agerrea. You discover Mauléon-Licharre by entering its lower town separated from the upper by the Saison.

The section that leads to Musculdy is relatively flat and allows you to regain your strength before tackling the ascent of the Col d'Osquich. Short and without real difficulty, the climb leads to a pretty plateau. The restaurant located there allows you to take a pleasant break.

The descent follows the foothills of several peaks that brighten up this portion. You then find a wide road that slips to Uhart-Mixe. After the village, a last small bump separates you from the finish in Saint-Palais.

name: Laguna Salada de Pétrola Loop
name_original: Vuelta a la Laguna Salada de Pétrola — circular desde Pétrola
description: This easy 21.8-mile road cycling route circles the unique Salty Lagoon of Pétrola, offering birdwatching and expansive views.

name: Madrigueras and Júcar Gorge Loop
name_original: Valdeganga, Motilleja y Modrigueras — circular desde Jorquera
description: This moderate 50.4-mile road cycling route from Jorquera explores the scenic Júcar River valley, passing through Valdeganga, Motilleja, and

name: Loop 10: Errozate Pass – Basque Country passes
name_original: Circuit 10 : Col d’Errozate — Cols du Pays basque
description:
From the historic heart of Saint-Jean-Pied-de-Port to the heights of the Errozate pass, the landscapes do not fail to surprise. With, in addition, a passage through the Iraty forest, this loop offers a nice summary of the Basque Country.

The route starts at the gates of the medieval city located at the crossroads of the roads that enliven the region. The course escapes on a small road towards Estérençuby. This is where the climb towards the Errozate pass officially begins. About 10 kilometers long, it includes an average close to 10% with passages at 19%.

Several switchbacks inaugurate the ascent before reaching open terrain which gradually reveals beautiful panoramas. The road continues to meander as large percentages step up the effort. Finally, at the foot of the summit of the same name, the Col d'Errozate is revealed. Take the time to appreciate the view and guess the path taken so far.

A downhill section then flirts with the Spanish border before continuing on a false flat rising towards the Irau pass. This very pleasant portion continues until you reach a small body of water located in the heart of the Iraty forest.

You are now evolving within the largest beech forest in Europe. A small uphill section leads to the Burdincurutcheta pass. From there you begin a beautiful descent towards Lecumberry. Along the way, a few hairpins slow down the pace while offering beautiful panoramas.
From the village, a long false flat descending accompanies the end of this beautiful loop.

name: Mayorga from Melgar de Abajo – circular by Santervás de Campos
name_original: Mayorga desde Melgar de Abajo – circular por Santervás de Campos
description: This moderate 45.7-mile road cycling route, Mayorga from Melgar de Abajo – circular by Santervás de Campos, explores historic villages and g

name: Argimon and Osta
name_original: Argimon & Osta
description:
Off to Argimon! At the summit of the 465 meter high mountain there is an impressive chapel called Mare de Déu d'Argimon and a great viewpoint of the entire region. But before you reach this destination, you have to pedal hard.

The first climb is waiting for you right after the start. A gently sloping sandy slope takes you up to the start of the Bolets Trail, which is slightly below the Turó de les Gatoses. This downhill has a great mix of easy and tricky passages.

You then turn onto a gravel road, which you follow to Argimon. On this section you can admire the magnificent church from afar. A wonderful sight!

Once at the top, you pass a barbecue area and then branch off onto the El Polvorí Trail and shortly later onto the Argimon Trail, where you master some tough spots downhill. There is undoubtedly a certain thrill involved. Only when you have crossed the gravel road twice does it become easier and smoother again.

Afterwards you crank uphill again on a steep slope. If your legs are no longer completely fresh, you may have to push up a short distance. But it's worth it, because there's a final trail descent on the varied Osta Trail, which only takes you out not far from the starting point.

name: Pedraza Loop Trail
name_original: Castillo de Pedraza — circular por el Parque Nacional Sierra de Guadarrama
description: Hike a moderate 6.5-mile (10.4 km) circular route around Castillo de Pedraza, exploring the medieval village and Sierra de Guadarrama Nation

name: Loop 9: Route de Napoléon – Basque Country passes
name_original: Circuit 9 : Route de Napoléon — Cols du Pays basque
description:
Although this loop of about 45 kilometers does not include any passes, it still offers its share of elevation. In the footsteps of the Camino de Compostela, the itinerary embarks on a road with panoramas as diverse as they are striking.

The route starts from the village of Saint-Jean-Pied-de-Port. With a rich history and heritage, the town also delights nature lovers with its location at the foot of the mountains. As soon as you leave Saint-Jean-Pied-de-Port, the road rises very steeply.

Although the forest remains omnipresent, few passages are shaded. This allows you to see the landscapes taking shape as you progress. The first section of the climb is also the steepest, the percentages soften as you progress. Along the way, you discover the amazing refuge of Orisson. Its terrace invites you to take a pleasant break to savor the view of the valley.

Once again in the saddle, you must continue to progress uphill. The peak of Orisson marks the beginning of a series of summits near which a superb panoramic road crosses. Gradually the slope fades and towards the Arnosteguy pass you begin a beautiful downhill section. This plunges into the heart of the valley, crossing a thick forest before emerging on the Nive. The river then accompanies you until you return to Saint-Jean-Pied-de-Port.

name: Caste 1 and Caste 2
name_original: Kaste 1 & Kaste 2
description:
On this tour, spectacular rock formations, magnificent views and trails with that certain something await you, which have been named Caste 1 and Caste 2.

From the parking lot you roll to the entrance to San Salvador Park and pedal up the wide gravel road until you reach the entrance to Caste 2 after a little more than four kilometers. This enchanting path winds through a unique landscape full of boulders. Sometimes you leave them behind and sometimes the impressive giant stones are integrated into the trail and you just roll over them. What a great choice of line – cheers to the trail builders!

Then you go back up on a steep gravel path until you reach the entrance to Kaste 1 after 150 meters of altitude. This narrow path is not too difficult and that's why it's a lot of fun. And just when you think that's it: puff cake! The big finale of this round is on the last section of the legendary Spiderman Trail.

With a big grin on your face, you arrive at the El Racó restaurant downstairs, where you can review the round with delicious Catalan snacks and cool drinks.

name: Loop 12: Col d'Inharpu – Basque Country passes
name_original: Circuit 12 : Col d’Inharpu — Cols du Pays basque
description:
From the authentic village of Mauléon-Licharre, this great loop goes to meet one of the most beautiful passes in the Basque Country. Encircling the forest of Arbailles and the peaks that surround it, it offers many spectacular views.

The start is in the heart of Mauléon-Licharre, capital of the espadrille. A traditional Basque village, its architecture includes Béarnaise-type houses as well as beautiful castles. The route begins by climbing the small Col d'Osquich as a warm-up. Then, between Saint-Just and Lecumberry, a second pass is on the menu, that of Gamia. Very pretty, it offers one of the most beautiful views of the valley. Take advantage of the descent to recover and the village to fill the cans.

From Lecumberry, you begin the 14 kilometer climb that leads to the Inharpu pass. The road climbs gradually on the sides of the Pic de Béhorléguy. This pretty summit accompanies you for a good part of the climb before having Bohorkortea in your sights. The summit of the pass is at the foot of this one. A few meters after the pass is the Auberge d’Ahusquy. Its terrace invites you to take a nice break with a view of the valley.

You then embark on a long descent that plunges into the forest. A few hairpin turns slow the pace before you find the Season. The stream then takes care of bringing you back to your starting point in Mauléon-Licharre.

name: Slick Rock & Gavarres & Scream
description:
This tour is just WOW! If you like varied single trails that are peppered with technical challenges and meander through enchanting landscapes, then you will quickly fall in love with this round.

It starts in a small parking lot just outside of Tremp. After a short stretch on the road, you turn onto a wide gravel road that takes you to the summit of the Roc de Neret at 906 meters. Once at the top, you can take a breather and enjoy the magnificent view of the St. Antoni reservoir.

What comes next is pure single trail fun! The "Slick Rock" trail starts right at the summit. As soon as you have left the turnoff to the "Arizona Cactus" trail on the right, you roll through a unique cream-colored rock landscape. You always follow the white lines that are painted on the rock and mark the way. This passage is really fun. You will be amazed how much grip the rock has and how beautiful the landscape is.

The "Gavarres" trail follows, which, after a steep climb, spits you out again on the wide gravel road that you already know from the beginning of the tour. Another short climb and you are at the start of "Scream", the grand finale of this round. This path is also excellently integrated into the rocky landscape. Technical levels require your full concentration once again. With a big grin on your face, you then roll back to the starting point of the tour.

name: Loop 11: Les cols d'Iraty – Basque Country passes
name_original: Circuit 11 : Les cols d'Iraty — Cols du Pays basque
description:
From the village of Saint-Jean-le-Vieux to the unfathomable forest of Iraty, passing through impressive passes, this route will delight lovers of cycling and nature. The requirement of the passes combines with the beauty of the landscapes to offer a remarkable excursion on the heights of the Basque Country.

Not far from Saint-Jean-Pied-de-Port, Saint-Jean-le-Vieux largely owes its existence to the Roman Empire. At the crossroads of history and nature, this charming little village is the ideal starting point for many nature outings. It is from here that the route begins, following the meanders of the Laurhibar. It is away from the stream that the road really begins to rise.

On the program, a start of ascent that leads to the Burdincurutcheta pass before leading to the Bagargui pass a little higher. Large yaw turns allow you to quickly gain height and open up wide panoramas. On your left stands the rocky peak of Béhorléguy. The climb ends by entering the forest of Iraty. Largest beech forest in Europe, it gives a mystical character at the end of the ascent.

At the Col de Bagargui, located in Iraty, there are many chalets as well as a grocery store and a restaurant, perfect for a break. In the distance, you can see the peak of Orhy, the highest point in the Basque Country at 2,017 meters above sea level.

From the pass you continue on a hilly portion that leads to the Inharpu pass, bypassing the Escaliers peak and passing through Arhansus. This beautiful portion continues until the descent that leads to Lecumberry. Along the way you find the peak of Béhorléguy, at the foot of which the descent progresses. From the village, all you have to do is take the same portion as on the outward journey to find Saint-Jean-le-Vieux.

name: Loop 15: Port de Larrau and La Pierre Saint-Martin – Basque Country passes
name_original: Circuit 15 : Port de Larrau et La Pierre Saint-Martin — Cols du Pays basque
description:
This large loop of just over 100 kilometers includes a nice positive elevation ratio. And for good reason, the route takes mountain passes culminating in mountain passages at more than 1,500 meters above sea level. On the program, the port of Larrau in the Basque Country, then the Pierre Saint-Martin pass in Béarn.

The start is in the pretty little village of Licq-Athérey which is more or less between the climbs. The start of the route gradually goes up the Gave de Larrau towards the village of the same name. It is a little before that, at the Auberge de Logibar, that the climb to the pass begins. With a length of about 16 kilometers, it includes an average percentage around 7%. A small softer section allows you to breathe before finishing the climb with the highest percentages. Throughout, the view of the Larrau valley and the Iraty forest becomes more beautiful as you go up the bends.

At the pass, at 1,585 meters you switch to Spain. The descent is on a beautiful road that must then be left to head towards Paso Laza. Small pass located in the middle of the forest, it leads to a long section that runs through the valley along two pretty streams. It is that of Belagua which gently begins the ascent towards Pierre Saint-Martin. When the stream is more discreet, the slope hardens and rises drastically through several hairpin bends.

Arrived at Portillo de Eraize the road softens slightly. Without knowing it, you are flirting with the border by staying on the Spanish side. A succession of bends takes you up the last few meters of altitude necessary to get out of the pass. At the end of a large bend, the panorama opens up magnificently on the French side.


The descent finally begins. Along the way, it passes near the village of Pierre Saint-Martin, before crossing the Col du Soudet. Classic of the Tour de France, it does not induce any effort in the direction of this route. Plunging into the forest, several large bends dictate the pace before leading to the village of Saint-Engrâce. From there, the Ühaïtxa stream connects you to the Gave de Larrau so that you can find your starting point in Licq.

name: Loop 14: Col de Bagargui from Mauléon – Basque Country passes
name_original: Circuit 14 : Col de Bagargui depuis Mauléon — Cols du Pays Basque
description:
This loop of about 80 kilometers is a magnificent summary of what the Basque Country can offer. From the capital of the espadrille to a thousand-year-old forest, the route fluctuates from valleys to passes for the greatest pleasure of the eyes.

The start starts from the village of Mauléon-Licharre. Considered the capital of the espadrille, it is a place faithful to Basque heritage and culture. From the village the road rises gradually to Aussurucq. This is where the climb towards the Auberge d’Ahusquy really begins. First of all it is a beautiful and dense forest which frames the road before the terrain is discovered at the foot of the Lomendi ridge. Once bypassed, it gives you the hostel in addition to a spectacular panorama. Its terrace allows you to savor the landscape for a moment before continuing.

The following portion continues to climb although it is more hilly than the previous one. The small descents allow you to cut the effort a little. It is by going around the Escaliers peak that you enter the Iraty forest. The largest beech forest in Europe finally gives you the Col de Bagargui, the highest point of the route.

At the pass, a grocery store and a restaurant allow you to get supplies before rolling out the beautiful descent that leads to Larrau. The view of the Pic d'Orhy, the highest point in the Basque Country at 2,017 metres, is remarkable. If you haven't made it to Iraty, the village of Larrau suggests a nice break. The Auberge de Logibar, a little later, is also an excellent place to stop.
It is here that you join the Gave de Larrau.

You stroll along the stream and the bends in the road. This gentle stroll continues when the waterway connects to the Season. From then on, you just have to follow it until you find the village of Mauléon-Licharre.

name: Arizona Cactus & MAF & Lo Búnquer
description:
The three main protagonists of this round are the trails "Arizona Cactus", "MAF" and "Lo Búnquer", all of which have their own charm and are really fun.

It starts with an easy climb on a wide slope that takes you to the top of the Roc de Neret. The last piece is a bit stonier and steeper and will get your pulse racing. You will be rewarded with an enchanting view of the St. Antoni reservoir. Take a deep breath and the downhill fun can begin.

You start the downhill on the "Slick Rock" trail, which is peppered with small jumps in the upper part and meanders super fast through the terrain. Then you turn right onto the "Arizona Cactus" trail, which surprises with a few crisp uphills and then fun downhill again.

A wonderful path follows in the middle of the forest, which takes you to the start of "Rock & Roll". Leg strength is required here again, because this short ascent is quite challenging. As soon as you see the "MAF" sign, the ordeal is over and you can look forward to a great, not too difficult descent over gigantic slabs of rock.

On a gravel road you crank uphill one last time to the "Lo Búnquer" trail, which spits you out with a lot of flow at the hydroelectric power station and a former World War II bunker. Then you roll further down the stairs and in no time you are back at the starting point of the tour.

name: Loop 7: Sorogain and Ibañeta – Basque Country passes
name_original: Circuit 7 : Sorogain et Ibañeta en boucle — Cols du Pays basque
description:
Starting from the picturesque village of Saint-Étienne-de-Baïgorry, this loop leads to the assault of two passes located in the Spanish Basque Country. This route is introduced and concludes with two beautiful valleys that revolve around your starting and finishing point.

Nestled at the foot of the Iparla ridges, Saint-Étienne-de-Baïgorry is a pretty little village in the Basque Country. Cradled by the runoff of the stream that flows there, the town is a great introduction to the Aldudes valley. The route therefore starts from here and goes up a good part of this watercourse.

You cross Banca, known for its trout farming, before reaching Urepel. It is at the exit of the village that the slope rises seriously in the direction of Sorogain. Just over seven kilometers long, the climb includes demanding portions over 18%. The start of the ascent is in the forest before the terrain uncovers itself and offers a nice panorama of the valley from which you arrive. A few meters after the border, you reach the pass which is on a vast grassy plateau.

The descent is not as steep as the ascent. You glide quietly in the hollow of a valley lined with beautiful peaks. When the terrain opens up, you branch off to undertake a new ascent towards the port of ibañeta. It begins with a first section with some high percentages before turning into a long false flat. It is in the last portion that the slope becomes really steep. At the top of the pass is a small chapel which symbolizes the end of the climb.

You then begin one of the longest descents in the Basque Country. Until Saint-Jean-Pied-de-Port you lose more than 900 meters of altitude in almost 25 kilometers. In the village, weather permitting, take a nice break on one of the many terraces there. You then only have a few kilometers left to find the village of Saint-Étienne-de-Baïgorry.

name: Roques Pelades
description:
You start in Tremp and roll over quiet side roads and gravel roads to Talarn, where you branch off onto the first fun trail. This is followed by a long climb on a quiet, paved road that takes you past vineyards and to the start of the "Roques Pelades" trail.

This path is a real gem and belongs on the to-do list of all single trail fans. The route over rock steps and steep slabs is unique and will make every trail lover's eyes light up.
After about one and a half kilometers you will pass a beautiful vantage point that invites you to take a break. Here you can enjoy the magnificent view of the deep blue St. Antoni reservoir and the Pyrenees peaks in the distance.

Then you roll on with full concentration. When you see the sign "El Llom" you can leave the path for a moment, turn right and descend over a super steep slab of rock. If that's not your thing, then you can simply skip this short detour. After five kilometers and lots of challenging passages, your trail adventure is over and you pedal relaxed back to the starting point of the tour.

name: Dragon Khan & Serengueti
description:
This loop is not for the faint of heart and will give you some adrenaline rushes, I promise! It goes northwest from Tremp to the legendary trails "Dragon Khan" and "Serengueti".

Dragon Khan is pure thrill! This terracotta-colored ridge path is the flagship and probably the most famous path of Tremp. The almost one kilometer long, extremely narrow path really packs a punch. The path does not forgive any mistakes and there are only very few places where you can interrupt the journey and continue on foot. It's best to take a close look at what's coming beforehand and then decide whether you want to walk the fine line. A tiny wobble and you're lying in the gorge below.

Then it's much easier on the nerves. Via the strenuous uphill trail "Pujada de la Muerte" you crank up to the well-known "Serengueti" trail, which resembles a fun roller coaster ride. This path is comparatively easy to ride and promises a lot of flow. Here, too, there are occasional exposed spots that require your full concentration.

name: Els Escorpins
description:
Anyone who likes technical trails and likes to pedal a few meters uphill will have a lot of fun on this varied circuit.

It starts on roads with little traffic and gravel roads in the direction of Talarn. After enjoying a short descent, the longest climb of this tour begins. Almost 500 meters in altitude on a quiet, paved road have to be mastered before you get to the start of Roques Pelades.

Your single trail adventure begins here. The path leads spectacularly over stones, steps and ledges along the ridge, so there is no boredom. After almost one and a half kilometers you will reach a great vantage point that invites you to take a breather.

When you've had enough of the breathtaking view of the St. Antoni reservoir, the downhill fun continues on the "Els Escorpins" trail. This path leads spectacularly along the edge of a cliff and then partly flowy, partly tricky downhill. But don't worry: You can simply avoid difficult spots.

With a big grin on your face you reach the end of the trail and then roll comfortably back to the starting point of the tour.

name: Carrilet I (Olot - Girona)
description:
Carrilet I greenway is a route that follows the old route of the train that connected Olot and Girona. It is an ideal route to do by bike or on foot, as it has little gradient and crosses very varied and beautiful landscapes. The route has a length of 56 kilometers and passes through twelve towns in three counties: Garrotxa, Gironès and Selva.

The route starts in Olot, in the heart of the Garrotxa volcanic area, where you can admire the natural and cultural heritage of this region. From Olot, the route goes to Les Preses, Sant Esteve d'en Bas and Sant Feliu de Pallerols, where you can see the Hostoles castle. Afterwards, the route climbs up to the En Bas pass, the highest point on the itinerary, at 589 meters above sea level. From there, the route gently descends to Girona, following the valleys of the rivers Fluvià, Brugent and Ter.

Along the way, you can visit charming towns such as Les Planes d'Hostoles, Amer, La Cellera de Ter, Anglès and Bonmatí. Finally, the route reaches Girona, where you can enjoy the history and culture of these cities. In Girona, the route connects with the second section of Carrilet, which goes to Sant Feliu de Guíxols.

More information: www.viesverdes.cat

name: Cercavins Route – hiking in Catalonia
name_original: Ruta del Cercavins
description:
The landscape of the Segarra region is strongly marked by its dry crops. It is a mosaic of cereal fields, almond trees and vines. The latter make up part of the Costers del Segre designation of origin. The Cercavins Route follows the course of the river of the same name and visits towns such as l'Ametlla de Segarra and Montornès de Segarra. These small nuclei of accentuated rural character hide an interesting cultural and historical heritage that you do not want to miss.

The Route starts from l'Ametlla de Segarra, where you can visit the tower of the old castle, from the 11th century, and also the church of Sant Pere, of which its square bell tower with four eyes topped by a pinnacle stands out. You leave the town towards the south, towards Montoliu de Segarra and the Cercavins river, through paths surrounded by fields. The route does not go through Montoliu, but from the road you have a good view of this picturesque town perched on a hill.

Follow the path, parallel to the river, in the direction of Montornès de Segarra. At the entrance to the town, to your right, you will find the town's cemetery, in a modernist style and very peculiar due to its corners topped by pagodas covered in glazed ceramic. Montornès also houses an interesting heritage: the remains of its castle, attached to the parish church, or the old school, now converted into the town's social premises. It is highly recommended to visit the streets of its old town, whose houses preserve the old voussoired portals. After the visit to Montornès, you return to l'Ametlla again, crossing a varied succession of cultivated fields.

description: Hike the moderate 10.5-mile Fuente del Caño and Sierra del Moral Loop, exploring diverse landscapes and the unique Fuente del Caño.

name: Fangar Lighthouse – hiking in Catalonia
name_original: Faro del Fangar
description:
The description of this walk to the Fangar lighthouse may surprise you, since it involves a walk along the beach to the lighthouse and back. However, the peculiar environment you are in makes it truly unique. The lighthouse is located on the Fangar peninsula, a strip of sand located in the northern part of the Ebro delta. Like the delta itself, this peninsula is the product of erosion, transport and sedimentation of the materials that the river has dragged along its way to the sea.

You start the Route in the parking lot that leads to the Marquesa beach, next to a restaurant in front of it. The path takes you towards it along the shore, leaving a dune system on your left that houses a protected nesting area for seabirds. Some of the specimens are seagulls and terns. In addition, this area is an important habitat for reptiles, amphibians, small mammals, and insects.

It is worth doing this walk at sunrise or sunset, since the light in those hours gives the dune landscape spectacular warm tones. Also, on very sunny days, if you look towards the sea, you can see the optical effect called “fata morgana” in which the boats seem to float in the air. In the lighthouse area it is important not to leave the marked paths. This is to respect the local wildlife protection zones.

After the visit to the lighthouse, you return to the starting point along the same path.

name_original: Vuelta al Tossal d’Isòvol
description:
A “tossal” is the Catalan name by which the small elevations or hills of calcareous origin typical of the Pyrenean sub-Mediterranean mountains are known. The tossal d'Isòvol is included in the Tossals d'Isòvol and Olopte Protected Natural Area, in Baixa Cerdanya, and contains a very unique fauna, made up of Mediterranean species. It also has a rich and varied flora of continental and steppe species, many of which are unique to the area.

The Route begins in the town of Olopte, which you leave behind to continue south following the course of the Durán River. To the left you can see the tossal d'Isòvol whose ascent you start shortly before reaching Isòlvol. Until you reach the foot of this imposing hill, you walk through a pretty grove of trees along the river, ideal for keeping cool on warm days.

At the top of the tossal d'Isòvol you can access the viewpoint. Since it is located in the geographical center of the Cerdanya plain, you enjoy a privileged panoramic view of the region and also of the Segre river. From the viewpoint you can also observe some of the birds of prey typical of the area, such as the golden eagle or the boreal owl. From the top of the tossal, the Route continues descending again to Olopte, where you can visit the church of Sant Pere d'Olopte, located on a promontory that dominates the town.

name: Trails steeped in history in Escornalbou – hiking in Catalonia
name_original: Caminos empapados de historia en Escornalbou
description:
The Escornalbou barony brought together various towns in the Sierra de l'Argentera, around the castle and monastery of Sant Miquel d'Escornalbou. Founded in 1153, the monastery underwent a spectacular and controversial restoration at the beginning of the 20th century. It was from the hand of the Indian and philanthropist Eduard Toda. On this Route through cobbled paths I suggest a walk through two of the barony's towns, l'Argentera and Duesaigües, as well as a visit to the unique castle and monastery of Sant Miquel d'Escornalbou.

You start the journey in l'Argentera, a picturesque town known for its cobbled streets and the flowers that decorate its houses. As soon as you leave the town you begin the ascent to the castle, which can be visited throughout the year. You go up the cobbled path that the monks used to go to and from l'Argentera. The castle promontory has a hermitage at its highest point from which you can enjoy spectacular views ranging from the Prades mountains and the Montsant peaks to the north, to, on a clear day, the Ebro Delta or even the Tramuntana mountains in Majorca.

After leaving the castle, you take the road to Duesaigües, which runs through an oak grove and where you find the Vilamanya fountain, donated to the town by Eduard Toda. Shortly before reaching Duesaigües, you come across the laundry, built at the end of the 19th century which, in addition to collecting rainwater, has a pond to store irrigation water. The last section of the Route follows the Camino Real until it returns to l'Argentera.

name: Loop 1: The four passes – 10 Pyrenean itineraries
name_original: Boucle 1 : Les quatre cols — 10 itinéraires pyrénéens
description:
This first loop is one of the best known and most appreciated by all fans of road cycling in Ariège. From Tarascon-sur-Ariège, it has the particularity of crossing no less than four passes, which in the region are called ports (which in the Gascon language, translates as pass).

The landscapes are splendid, because you evolve in the very heart of the Regional Natural Park of the Ariège Pyrenees. The warm-up is gradual. We enter the hard when we leave the valley of Niaux at the level of Vicdessos and a little before Auzat. It is the small road of Suc which leads you after 12 kilometers of ascent to the port of Lers. A short descent follows to the Etang de Lers to quickly climb the Col d'Agnès which is reached after only eight kilometres.

The descent to Aulus-les-bains is a pure delight, because the road is winding to perfection and has many bends.

Arrived in this spa village, you are halfway through, take the opportunity to take a break. Its tranquility and the course of the Garbet are very pleasant.

The second part of the route presents less difficulty, because the passes are lower than the previous ones, but the distance and the fatigue help, they will certainly not be easy.

In addition to Aulus-les-Bains and Tarascon which are not lacking in charm, you pass close to the cave of Bédeilhac known for its monumental porch where you could land small light planes. After this loop, do not hesitate to visit this cavity, as well as the world-famous Niaux cave, famous for its parietal paintings and its excellent acoustics.

Shortly after the Col de Saraillé you will discover one of the essentials of Couserans. The Cominac stone barns here offer fantastic views of Mount Valier.

name: Loop 2: The Péguère wall – 10 Pyrenean routes
name_original: Boucle 2 : Le mur de Péguère — 10 itinéraires pyrénéens
description:
This loop of almost 73 kilometers from Foix is a real challenge. The wall of Péguère (the nickname given to the climb of the pass of the same name) fully deserves this nickname. Scheduled during the 1973 Tour de France, its ascent was refused because the descent was deemed too dangerous. It was not until 2012, 39 years later, that the organizers of the Tour de France decided to go there again.

You take the Devil's Bridge at the eighth kilometer, then follow the course of the Ariège to Tarascon. The small departmental road 618 runs parallel to the national road 20 and thus avoids car traffic.

Once at kilometer 20, the serious things begin, first gradually passing through the high line, then after 40 kilometers, you arrive at the foot of the wall. A few very trying kilometers, and here you are at the top.

At the pass, a pastoral shelter and an orientation table, allow you to catch your breath and your mind.

The beautiful descent that follows takes you through the Col de Jouels. It is singular enough to be noted. It's not every day that you cross a pass in this way.

Back in Foix, take the opportunity to visit its castle. This jewel, which dates from the 12th century, stands on a rocky platform and dominates the city. It is the perfect example of what a stately home was like in the Middle Ages. A very beautiful exhibition on armory at that time awaits you within its walls.

name: Camino Primitivo: Salas - Tineo Loop
name_original: Mirador de Letizia y Salas — circular desde Tineo
description: This difficult 29.3-mile mountain bike loop on the Camino Primitivo from Salas to Tineo offers challenging climbs and historic Asturian scen

name: Loop 3: Le col d'Agnès – 10 Pyrenean routes
name_original: Boucle 3 : Le col d'Agnès — 10 itinéraires pyrénéens
description:
This 107 kilometer loop is very balanced, because the difficulties and the difference in height of the four passes go to a crescendo. It is the ideal course for those who want to train in splendid landscapes.

From Saint-Girons, after having skirted the Lez and past the village of Moulis, you begin as a warm-up the ascent of the Col de Portech followed by the Col de Catchaudégué which always surprises a little when you leave Alos .

After discovering the village of Seix, you begin the beautiful climb that takes you along deserted roads along the Alet river to the Col de Latrape. The short descent will be followed by the ascent of the Col d'Agnès which culminates at 1,571 meters.

This pretty course offers very beautiful views of many castles. There are five of them: the Château des Vicomtes in Saint-Girons, the castles of Lagarde and Mirabat located on a summit ridge at the level of Pont de la Taule. And between the Trein d'Ustou and Sérac D'Ustou are the ruins of the Castel de Margie and the Château de La Coste.

name: Loop 4: Les sabots de Bethmale – 10 Pyrenean routes
name_original: Boucle 4 : Les sabots de Bethmale — 10 itinéraires pyrénéens
description:
It is a very pretty loop of about 65 kilometers that I invite you to discover. With its passes of La Core, Portech and Catchaudégué, and its small roads without traffic, you will be seduced. This part of Couserans offers you, in addition to very beautiful views of the unmissable Mont Valier, the possibility of tasting the cheeses of this micro-region of Ariège.

Departing from Saint-Girons, you pass through Engomer then through Castillon-en-Couserans where the main resource besides tourism and livestock is the production of milk for cheese.

From this village, you begin to climb departmental road 4 which gradually takes you first to L'Étang de Bethmale then to the Col de Corre. A few sections at 19% before reaching the flat area make you understand why this pass was chosen several times by the organization of the Tour de France.

After thirty kilometers, you begin a long descent, then a much gentler climb than the previous one. You reach the Col de Catchaudégué after kilometer 40 of the route.

After the Col de Portech, you return to Saint-Girons.

name: Loop 6: The Beille plateau and Ariège valley – 10 Pyrenean routes
name_original: Boucle 6 : Le plateau de Beille et la vallée de l'Ariège — 10 itinéraires pyrénéens
description:
This very beautiful loop takes place on both sides of the upper Ariège valley. The starting point is the spa town of Ax-les-Thermes. You leave it by taking the corniche road which, on the hillside, takes you to the descent which leads to the village of Verdun.

The Verdun coast around kilometer 30 is worth the trip, because taking its hairpin bends is pure pleasure. After crossing the bridge over the river, you arrive at Les Cabannes where the official start of the ascent of the Plateau de Beille takes place.

This 16 kilometer climb is difficult and has significant percentages. On this plateau, a beautiful 360° view rewards you for all your efforts.

After having taken advantage of the altitude, you will have to think about going down. You return to your starting point by taking a road that follows the course of the Ariège.

name: Loop 7: Carla-Bayle and Mas D'Azil – 10 Pyrenean routes
name_original: Boucle 7 : Carla-Bayle et le Mas D'Azil — 10 itinéraires pyrénéens
description:
This loop can easily be transformed into a day of discovery as the possibilities of stops are numerous and interesting. It is one of my favorite routes. The diversity and tranquility of the roads on this side of the Pyrenean Piedmont are dotted with must-sees.

After the charming villages of Carla-Bayle and Montbrun-Bocage, the forest cover becomes denser to the village of Mérigon located at the foot of the Volp gorges. From there, a small departmental road takes you to an unlisted pass at the place called Les Moulis where you will discover a herd of Angora goats.

The dream descent that follows takes you to the intersection with the D 119, which you take to the left until you reach the Grotte du Mas-d'Azil. The place is impressive! To avoid having to take lights, the route passes over the caves. The tunnel is indeed a bit dangerous for bikes.

After Sabarat, a substantial climb on a winding road passes close to an observatory. This is an opportunity to take a last look at these beautiful mountains.

It is by a long flat straight line of about five kilometers, starting at the level of the village of Artigat that you return to your starting point of Fossat.

name: Logrosán and Sierra Brava Reservoir Loop
name_original: Desde Logrosán hasta el Embalse de Sierra Brava — circular en BTT
description: This moderate 44.8-mile mountain biking loop from Logrosán to the Sierra Brava Reservoir offers varied landscapes and scenic views.

name: Loop 8: Lake Mondely – 10 Pyrenean routes
name_original: Boucle 8 : Le lac de Mondely — 10 itinéraires pyrénéens
description:
This route is the most accessible of these 10 loops that I offer in Ariège. It starts in the village of Lescure to immediately cross the Baup and take the Piedmont V81 cycle route which connects Saint-Girons to Foix. This old railway line converted into a cycle path can be taken by road bike.

These are 17 kilometers of pure tranquillity. This bike path is made up of very fine gravel interspersed with tunnel crossings.

You leave this greenway by a tiny little busy secondary road to reach the Mondely reservoir. A place conducive to relaxation. After having taken advantage of this preserved space, you find the road which takes you to the village of Mas d'Azil, where another tunnel, this time natural, awaits you. It is a cave dug by the Arize river and not railway tunnels. Do not forget your lamp to cross this one kilometer long passage.

On the other side, the cave house offers drinks, pies and dishes. It is very practical in case of a little hunger. The beautiful meadow below invites you to take a break here.

To end this route, you take a very beautiful road that runs along the Arize then the Pujol to Lescure. This is the guarantee of freshness all the way.

name: Loop 9: L'ascension de Soulcem – 10 Pyrenean routes
name_original: Boucle 9 : L'ascencion de Soulcem — 10 itinéraires pyrénéens
description:
Leaving from the village of Niaux, I advise you to start this discovery of Soulcem with a visit to the prehistoric cave of Niaux. Located almost at the end of the valley, it overlooks the starting village.

This impressive monumental cavity has been known for a very long time. It is worth a visit to better understand the men and women who lived in this superb valley around 14,000 years ago.

After going up the past, it's time to go back up the valley. To do this, you will successively pass the villages of Capoulet, Laramade, Vicdessos and Aussat. After this last town, the road becomes narrower and steeper to finally reach the artificial lake of Soulcem.

You are then very close to the border with Spain and the Principality of Andorra. If you do not opt for the bivouac or the hike, you will have to think about turning back and retracing your steps.

If it gets late, you always have the option of dining and spending the night in Aussat.

name: Granada Geopark: Castilléjar, Huéscar, and Galera Loop
name_original: Castilléjar a Huéscar a Galera — circular por la Carretera de Galera a Contijo de Cura
description: This 26.3-mile mountain bike route takes you through the diverse landscapes of Granada, Spain, connecting Castilléjar, Huéscar, and Galera.

name: Loop 10: Col de Calzan – 10 Pyrenean routes
name_original: Boucle 10 : Le col de Calzan — 10 itinéraires pyrénéens
description:
This final route that starts from the town of Pamiers offers an easier alternative compared to the difficulties of the previous loops.

There are many stops, starting with the superb town of Mirepoix, which you may have already discovered during loop 5.

The must-see on this route for me is the incredible Notre-Dame de Vals church. One of a kind, it is definitely worth a look. I'm not particularly sensitive to architecture and Christian art, but here you are in front of a very special church that leaves no one indifferent. You will go as you explore from discovery to discovery. It is located at the start of the route shortly after leaving the town of Pamiers around the 16th kilometer.

This visit done, you then follow a quiet stream to Mirepoix.

After Mirepoix, it is the Pyrenean foothills that you find. Expect more rugged terrain, but punctuated by beautiful views that will culminate at Col de Calzan. This pass presents beautiful landscapes.

After this last effort, it will be the descent towards the valley of Crieu. You will successively pass through Saint-Félix-du-Rieutord and Verniolle before returning to Pamiers, birthplace of the musician Gabriel Fauré.

name: Calella to the Punta de la Tordera
name_original: De Calella a la Punta de la Tordera
description:
From Calella to the mouth of the Tordera, this bike route offers you to travel the long stretch of coast that joins the towns of Calella, Pineda de Mar, Santa Susanna and Malgrat de Mar, in the Maresme region of Barcelona. Delimited by the promontory where the Calella lighthouse is located to the south and the Tordera delta to the north, on this coastal walk you always pedal along the bike path. During the journey you can take advantage of both the beaches and the services that you find along the Route: from bars and restaurants to beach sports activities. This Route can be done with almost any type of bicycle, since the path is paved or is a dirt track in good condition.

Departs from Calella, on the Garbí beach, just below the Calella lighthouse. You cycle along the bike path to reach the next beach, known as Platja Gran de Calella. Almost without realizing it, you leave Calella to enter Pineda de Mar, where two more beaches await you: known as Platja dels Pescadors and Platja dels Pins. The seafront of Santa Susanna, which is the one you will find below, is different from that of Calella and Pineda de Mar, more touristy and urbanized. Farms predominate here and the area is quieter.

When you arrive at Malgrat de Mar you return to a busier coastline. But as you get closer to the Tordera river and its delta, the tall buildings disappear and once again the beach is flanked by campsites and agricultural areas. In order to reach the mouth of the Tordera by bike paths, the Route goes briefly inland to take you right up to the Tordera delta. Here there is a bird observation post from where you can observe the varied avian fauna of the area.

name: GR 11: La Sarra to Respomuso Refuge Trail
name_original: Subida al Embalse de Respomuso — ida y vuelta por el Circo de Piedrafita
description: Hike to the stunning Respomuso Reservoir and through the Circo de Piedrafita, covering 9.0 miles with 2374 feet of elevation gain.

name: Vía Verde Ruta del Ferro i del Carbó
description:
Water, iron and coal. The presence of these three elements created an important mining and iron forging industry in the Catalan region of Ripollès, which prompted the construction of a train line that connected the region with Barcelona. This train line, now in disuse, has become a greenway that connects the towns of Ripoll, Sant Joan de les Abadesses and Sant Pau de Segúries. On this route I suggest you go along the old railway line and enjoy the charms of the Ruta del Ferro i del Carbó greenway. In this video you can get an idea of what you will find:
 
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ECCJhX5LmOM

In this case, the Route goes in a descending direction, it starts in Sant Pau de Segúries and ends in Ripoll, but it can be done in both directions, since the unevenness is very gentle (an average of 1% in its 18 kilometres). The greenway is paved with asphalt conglomerate, so you can use practically any bicycle.

You start from Sant Pau de Segúries to cross the river Ter by a bridge and enter the greenway. You now descend through the Ter valley to the Perella bridge, built in stone and with a single semicircular arch. This valley forms one of the typical landscapes of the area: a mosaic made up of leafy forests and harvest meadows dotted with small horticultural plots. Your next destination is Colònia Llaudet, where some of its residential buildings still fulfill their original function. Immediately you arrive at Sant Joan de les Abadesses, where a visit to the monastery and its collection of sacred art is a must.

Leaving Sant Joan de les Abadesses you cross its old bridge, known as Pont de Sant Joan, which has a spectacular Gothic arch with one of the largest lights in the entire Iberian Peninsula. Now continue through the valley in the direction of Ripoll, where the end of the Route awaits you and another monastery that is worth visiting: the monastery of Santa Maria de Ripoll, also founded by Guifré el Pilós. You cannot miss its portal, from the 12th century, one of the most important works of Catalan Romanesque.

name: Along Lleida's canals
name_original: Ruta de las acequias de Lleida
description:
This Route that surrounds the city of Lleida is strongly marked by the presence of the Segre River. The Pyrenean river, before contributing its flow to the Ebro, leaves its mark on the area thanks to an extensive network of ditches. The Route runs between fruit orchards and herbaceous crops, wetlands or peri-urban parks such as the Parc de la Mitjana. And he never loses sight of the city of Lleida and the unmistakable profile given by the Seu Vella, the singular cathedral that presides over it. You can use a mountain bike or gravel bike, since the Route runs along dirt roads, as well as paved roads and bike lanes.

The Route starts from the Lleida train station, in the center of the city. Immediately you head towards the Segre river, crossing it by a footbridge to head north, to the Parc de la Mitjana. This park crosses irrigation canals and the Segre river itself, and has its own microclimate that has favored a wonderful riverside forest and a varied fauna and flora. Leaving the park, you begin to make a wide detour to the city of Lleida, passing through numerous orchard plots that take advantage of the river's water through canals such as the Pinyana canal, the oldest irrigation canal in Catalonia.

After crossing the western neighborhoods of the city of Lleida, the Route continues south to meet the Segre River again in the Rufea wetlands. Like the Mitjana park further north, the river creates small lagoons where the presence of birds is a real spectacle. There is a picnic area where you can rest and enjoy the scenery before going up the river and returning to the starting point, in the city of Lleida.

description: Hike the moderate Claverol Trail, a 6.5-mile (10.5 km) circular route with 881 feet (269 metres) of gain, offering views of La Pobla de Segu

name: Vía Verde Val del Zafán
name_original: Vía verde Val del Zafán
description:
The railway project that was to link Puebla de Híjar, in Teruel, with Tortosa, in Tarragona, was never completed. The line operated partially for a few decades, until the tracks were finally raised in 1973. In the following years, the route was conditioned, turning it into the current greenway that connects Alcañiz with Tortosa. This Route is perfect to start cycling with the family. Without a doubt, it is an ambitious proposal, since there are 102 kilometers to travel. But due to its characteristics, when pedaling on a road segregated from traffic that goes downhill for most of the route and that has a good offer of tourist services, it constitutes a very good proposal to start cycling trips with the family.

The Route can be divided into several days, depending on the kilometers that you want to do per day and the accommodation offer. To give you an idea, if you wanted to do it in five days, these would be the possible stages:

Alcañiz - Valljunquera (21 kilometres)
Valljunquera - Arnes (28 kilometres)
Arnes-Bot (17 kilometers)
Bot - Benifallet (20 kilometres)
Benifallet - Tortosa (24 kilometres)

After the first kilometres, little by little you approach the natural barrier of the Natural Park of Los Puertos. This large mountainous massif separates Teruel from Castellón and Tarragona and, as it passes through the towns of Arnes, Horta de Sant Joan or Bot, offers you a magnificent backdrop for the route, with its imposing rock formations, such as the Rocas de Benet or the Penyagalera. You can visit the historic center of Arnes, declared a site of cultural interest, or Horta de Sant Joan. In this peculiar town, perched on a promontory, you can visit the Els Ports Ecomuseoi or the Picasso Center, as well as its characteristic old town with concentric streets.

In Bot, the Route turns southeast again to find the course of the Ebro River and head towards Tortosa. 10 kilometers after Bot, next to a bar on the same greenway, you can take a detour to visit Pinell de Brai, six kilometers away by road. In Bot you can visit the Renaissance church of Sant Blai. In Pinell de Brai, in addition to finding accommodation and restaurants, you can visit the modernist-style cooperative winery, designed and built by César Martinell, a disciple of Gaudí.

Benifallet or Xerta are two of the riverside towns that you find when you reach the Ebro and that can serve as the end of the stage. In the case of Benifallet, when you arrive at the old train station on the greenway, you can take a road that takes you to the town, located five kilometers away. If you decide to go to Xerta, the greenway crosses it and you should not take any detours. In Xerta you can visit its dam (assut), an engineering work from the 15th century, located in the middle of the Ebro river. The last kilometers to Tortosa are very flat and run between crops, not far from the course of the Ebro. In Tortosa, We recommend visiting the Royal Colleges, the most important Renaissance monument in Catalonia, the spectacular castle of La Suda, from the 10th century, where the only open-air Islamic necropolis in Catalonia is preserved, or the Cathedral of Santa Maria.

name: Around the Sanctuary of Montgarri
name_original: Vuelta al Santuario de Montgarri
description:
Located at the eastern end of the Val d'Aran, the Montgarri sanctuary is one of the most popular destinations for hikers who visit the area. On this mountain bike route, which starts from Pla de Beret, you follow the southern bank of the Noguera Pallaresa river from almost its source to the sanctuary. From there, return to Pla de Beret along the north bank of the river. It is an ideal Route to do from late spring to autumn, when the temperatures are milder and the snow has disappeared. The Route runs through forest tracks that are usually in good condition, although you can find some sections complicated by the rains and also some hard ramp that can force you to push the bike momentarily. A mountain bike is the best option to fully enjoy the experience.

You leave Pla de Beret, next to the ski lifts, to take a track that descends to Montgarri, following the course of the river. You pedal surrounded by peaks that exceed 2000 meters in length, the ones on your left mark the border with France, while the ones you leave on your right mark the limit with the Pallars Sobirà region. They are landscapes of glacial origin, in which you can see the contrast between the pastures on the sunny slopes and the dense pine forests in the shade. You experience this contrast in first person, as you descend and leave the meadows of Pla de Beret and enter the forest as you descend towards Montgarri.

In Montgarri, a stop is obligatory at the sanctuary and the adjacent refuge. There you can have a drink in its bar-restaurant and rest on the wooden benches on its terrace before resuming your march and beginning the ascent back to Pla de Beret. Here you come across some hard ramps but luckily they are not very long. If you do this Route in summer, this stretch, more open, allows you to see cows and mares graze peacefully in the meadows next to the path.

name: Beaches and fields of Cambrils
name_original: Playas y campos de Cambrils
description:
In the heart of the Costa Daurada, the coastal town of Cambrils maintains a double personality. On the one hand, the fishing and maritime tradition of its port makes it a top-level tourist destination, with beaches and restaurants where you can enjoy the sea and Mediterranean cuisine. But it is enough to go a little inland to discover a rural character that survives thanks to its excellent soil, and which still preserves an important agricultural tradition. There you find an agrarian mosaic in which woody crops such as olive, hazelnut, almond and carob trees predominate, but also an irrigated fruit and vegetable garden. It is an easy Route that can be done with mountain or gravel bikes, to be able to circulate without problem on the dirt tracks of the interior.

The Route begins just in front of the port of Cambrils, from where you continue south along its promenade. You pass by different beaches of the municipality, such as the one of the Llosa or the one of l'Ardiaca. When you reach the end of the Riudecanyes stream, turn inland. After passing under the train tracks and the highway, the landscape changes radically. You leave behind the most touristic urbanization to enter the so-called Plana del Baix Camp, which is characterized by its eminently agricultural character. Between fields you arrive at the height of Mont-roig del Camp, where you turn towards the east, to start the return towards Cambrils.

Shortly after, the Route passes the Teixells castle, the old San Rafael farmhouse, built in exposed stone and excellently preserved. Teixells was previously a segregated municipality, center of the fertile plain of the Plana del Baix Camp. Due to its importance and being at a strategic crossroads, the farmhouse was fortified to defend itself against attacks. When you get to Cambrils, you pass by the Cambrils History Museum. It is impossible to get confused, since it is presided over by the Torre del Port, an old watchtower that since the 17th century was in charge of protecting the port from pirate attacks. You can finish the day with a bath and eating in one of the many seafood restaurants that are next to the port.

name: Route of Iron and Coal (Ripoll - Sant Joan de les Abadesses)
name_original: Ruta del Ferro i del Carbó (Ripoll - Sant Joan de les Abadesses)
description:
The Ruta del Ferro y del Carbó (Hierro y el Carbon in Spanish) is a greenway that is the old route of the railway that transported iron and coal from the mines of Ogassa to Ripoll. It is an ideal route to enjoy the nature and culture of this area of the Catalan Pyrenees, on foot or by bike.

The route has a length of 9.5 kilometers, with a gentle slope of 1%. Une Ripoll, at 682 meters of altitude, with Sant Joan de les Abadesses, at 775 meters. Along the way, you can see the historical and cultural heritage of the monastery of Santa Maria de Ripoll or the old town of Sant Joan de les Abadesses.

The "Iron and Coal Route" is a way to get to know the past and the present of this area of Ripollès, and to spend a pleasant day with your family. For more information you can consult the website www.viesverdes.cat
