name: Route 2: Peña el Águila
name_original: Parcours 2 : Peña el Águila
description:
This route is the one with the greatest distance-altitude ratio in the Collection: 1,000 meters to be climbed in 17 kilometers. Your thighs will be put to the test, especially at the start of the course.

While the first few initial slopes are not the steepest, they start from the start. As you get up to speed, the slope increases and rocks and roots take precedence over the dirt roads. You have six kilometers and 850 meters of altitude difference to cover to reach Peña el Águila, the main summit of this Tour, so take it easy.

The first flat and downhill portions appear. You follow the GR 10 on a superb ridge path. Very stony, it prevents you from extending your stride to the Collado de Marichiva pass, where the slope becomes steeper but less technical.

Back at the bottom, the respite is short-lived since you continue directly with a steep climb in bends. The effort is all the more intense as the ascent occurs just after the Peña el Águila. This is the ideal time to throw away your last bit of strength and push your cardio before returning quietly to a final rolling downhill section.

name: Route 6: La Maliciosa
name_original: Parcours 6 : La Maliciosa
description:
If most of the routes in this Collection are around 20 kilometers, this one increases the difficulty with 36 kilometers and 1,900 meters of elevation gain.

After a gentle start, the slopes steepen and the terrain becomes alpine by the fourth kilometer. At the ninth kilometer, you find flat ground until Puerto de Navacerrada. If necessary, you can refuel at the pass before starting the last two thirds of the route.

This may not be a luxury since you reach the heart of the route with the tough climb of La Bola del Mundo. The slope continues to slope, reaching 40% just before the statue of the Virgen de las Nieves. Enjoy its magnificent panorama before starting the last, much simpler part which leads to the famous antenna at the summit.

After the effort, the comfort, you embark on a superb descent along a nascent river, the Guadarrama. It's hard to imagine that this small stream will become the great river that runs through Madrid. Arriving at a small bridge, you go up slightly to the Collado de los Pastores, a pass which announces the most difficult part of the route. You see in the distance the Maliciosa that you will have to face. After climbing its little sister, the Maliciosa Baja, you set off on its terrible technical slopes of more than 40%. The view from the top is even more beautiful.

Save some strength for the ensuing descent, also very steep and very technical over two kilometers. Have fun jumping from one rock to another before reaching the twenty-fifth kilometer, which heralds the return of more lenient terrain and elevations. The next ten kilometers are more wooded, quieter, but just as pleasant with still many beautiful views until you return to Cercedilla.

name: Route 5: La Peñota
name_original: Parcours 5 : La Peñota
description:
This route is divided into two main parts: a difficult climb with several alpine passages up to the Peñota over the first five kilometers; then a long descent of 17 kilometers.

Once again, you discover an emblematic mountain of the Sierra de Guadarrama, the granite summit of Peñota. After the climb partially in the forest, you spend almost the entire outing in a mineral universe which requires a lot of technique. The long descent that awaits you does not promise to be easy.

Navigating between rocks, often without a path, requires skills that can only be acquired through practice. But when you get there, what a joy it is to twirl from one stone to another, letting yourself be caressed by the wind which sweeps the ridge path. This sensation follows you along the entire GR 10 trail which leads to Puerto de Guadarrama.

This last pass then marks a U-turn to return to Cercedilla. The route gradually becomes smoother again, guiding you up the mountainside to see the Peñota climbed a little earlier from below. Your stride lengthens. The beauty of the open landscape in which you move is increased tenfold by the adrenaline provided by the speed.

Just before arriving at Cercedilla, a final small bump cuts your legs. Easy at first, it can seem like torture at this point in the journey. That's it, you've crossed it, a well-deserved rest awaits you.

name: Route 4: Siete Picos
name_original: Parcours 4 : Siete Picos
description:
Lovers of technical terrain, your time has come. As their name suggests, the Siete Picos are a succession of seven peaks that offer breathtaking views over rocky terrain. Located between two famous passes of the Sierra de Guadarrama, the Puerto de la Fuenfría and the Puerto de Navacerrada, they are popular with Madrid hikers.

The route that I am proposing is, however, more demanding since you have to start from the foot of the massif, as with the other circuits in the Collection. The climb of more than seven kilometers to reach this famous section is arduous. For your pleasure, it is punctuated by a few stops with pretty views, such as at the Mirador de los Poetas.

Just before arriving at the Siete Picos, you reach another famous peak, the Pico de Majalasna. From the Pradera de Majalasna, its ascent is short but very technical. If you like to climb rocks, take this detour while paying attention to where you put your feet.

Otherwise, head towards the marvelous Siete Picos ridge. Despite the slight difference in altitude on this section of more than two kilometers, your progress remains difficult in this mineral setting.

At the Pradera de los Siete Picos, it is time to descend towards Cercedilla on terrain that remains demanding. It is only around the fourteenth kilometer that the environment ceases to be alpine. Your legs appreciate being able to lengthen your stride again on the last slightly downhill section. Use the adrenaline provided by the magnificent landscapes you have crossed to gain speed until the finish!

name: Route 3: Montón de Trigo
name_original: Parcours 3 : Montón de Trigo
description:
The principle of this circuit is simple: a return trip to Montón de Trigo, the emblematic summit of the Guadarrama mountain range. The departure is on a climb which climbs gently. You follow the road which goes north from Cercedilla for a little over two kilometers.

You then join trails more suited to your trail runner profile. The slope steepens and the asphalt gives way to earth and stones. But not only that, since you are treading on an ancient Roman roadway which requires you to run on the cobblestones. Think of the ancient Romans who used it to reach northern Spain via the Puerto de la Fuenfría.

For your part, once you reach this mountain pass, you continue to climb. In the distance, you see a strange pile of stone taking shape, it is the Montón de Trigo, which as its Spanish name suggests, looks like a “heap of wheat”.

Your trail circuit then transforms into a skyrunning course. The terrain becomes very technical and steep, the paths disappear and you must pay attention to the markings to guide you. The scarcity of vegetation exposes you to the harsh climate. Until the summit, the height difference continues to increase. Once at the top, admire the superb panorama. You dominate the massif, as well as the Iberian Meseta on both sides, towards Madrid and Segovia.

All you have to do is go back down the same technical slope by which you climbed to Puerto de la Fuenfría. To return to the starting point, this time take the GR 10 to Collado de Marichiva. Admire the magnificent unobstructed views of the pass one last time, then head into the forest and let yourself be carried by the slope to the finish.

name: Route of the Canals

name: Route 1: Cerros de Cercedilla
name_original: Parcours 1 : Cerros de Cercedilla
description:
With eight kilometers and 300 meters of elevation gain, this first route serves as an appetizer in comparison with the other circuits in the Collection. It allows you to discover the first small mountains located on the outskirts of Cercedilla, called “cerros” in Spanish.

However, be careful on the first climb. Only one kilometer long, it has slopes of around 30% on certain passages. However, a beautiful view awaits you at the top. You will then discover the highest peaks of the Sierra de Guadarrama, which you will encounter during the following routes.

Go back down from the rock you are on, and after a brief clear section, head into the woods on a wide forest path. At the third kilometer, you leave this rolling path to follow a small technical single track which descends onto the road.

After following the asphalt for a little over 500 meters, you quickly find a pleasant path in the forest where the landscape is discovered in fits and starts. Not very steep, you can tackle it without any problem by running until you reach Cerro Colgado, the second small mountain located near Cercedilla.

Its 360-degree panorama opens onto the Sierra de Guadarrama on one side and the central Meseta on the other, the high plateau of the Iberian Peninsula. After admiring the landscape one last time, all you have to do is take the final descent to complete this first circuit.

name: Bonus route: Maratón Alpino Madrileño
name_original: Parcours bonus : Maratón Alpino Madrileño
description:
Proof that Cercedilla is a true mountain and trail town, it has its own race, the Maratón Alpino Madrileño or Madrid Alpine Marathon. This essential part of the Spanish calendar welcomes hundreds of participants every year on the trails of the Guadarrama mountain range.

Unlike the other routes in the Collection, this one begins at the official starting point, in the city center. If you want to start and end your trip at the station, this extends your trip by just over three kilometers.

On the first part of the circuit, the trails will be familiar to you if you have completed other routes in the Collection. You have the pleasure of climbing the Pico de Majalasna, which opens the way to the superb Siete Picos. You continue along the extension of the ridge to the Puerto de Navacerrada, where you can treat yourself to a first refueling off-site.

You climb the slopes of La Bola del Mundo without reaching its summit. This is when the environment evolves. You begin a round trip of around twenty kilometers to the roof of the Guadarrama mountain range, the Peñalara. Along the way, you come across other emblematic peaks, such as Hermana Mayor. You discover the trails in both directions, putting your alpine skills to the test both on the descent and the ascent.

If you do not want to go back and forth, once at Peñalara you can continue straight and go down to Laguna de los Pájaros. Then head in the opposite direction to cross the Peñalara cirque and its lakes until you find the official route at the Mirador de la Gitana.

Back at Puerto de Guadarrama, the long final descent towards Cercedilla begins on smoother wooded paths. The bars and restaurants of the city center welcome you with open arms to recharge your batteries.

name: Route Salou-La Pineda-Vila-Seca-Salou – cycle touring in Catalonia
name_original: Ruta Salou-La Pineda-Vila-Seca-Salou – Cicloturismo en Cataluña
description:
This interesting circular route links the towns of Salou, La Pineda and Vila-seca using various bike paths. It is a flat itinerary, ideal for enjoying a pleasant pedal ride with the family.

The Route begins in Vila-seca and guides you to the d'en Dolça racecourse. If you like to watch horse races and other events related to the world of horse racing, you are in the ideal place. Take a seat and enjoy your first break of the day.

When you reach the coast, a few kilometers await you, enjoying very beautiful views of the sea while you walk along the beaches of La Pineda and Salou. On the hottest days, a good dip can refresh you for the rest of the day.

In the last kilometers of the Route, you come across one of the most visited places in the entire province, the PortAventura theme park. If you have energy left, I can't think of a better place to release adrenaline.

This exciting route offers you many corners to discover, get on your bike and go explore them.

name: Cal Rosal Greenway in Pedret – cycle touring in Catalonia
name_original: Vía Verde de Cal Rosal en Pedret – Cicloturismo en Cataluña
description:
This endearing Route runs through a small section of the old railway route between Manresa and Olvan, known as the Llobregat Greenway. If you are looking for a calm and peaceful pedaling, this itinerary is for you.

Before starting, there are some information panels that explain the interesting history about the old railway line. If you have time, it's worth spending a few minutes on it.

In the first section of the Route, you can find the old factories and houses of Cal Rosal, one of the most important textile colonies in Berguedà. It dates back to 1858, when the Rosal brothers bought some land very close to Berga, to build a spinning factory.

In the following kilometers, you cross a couple of tunnels: Vila-rassa I and Vila-rassa II and continue to the Pontarrón de la Fuente Ollera to enter the last tunnel: Peirota. As you leave, you see several pine trees and large stone blocks on both sides of the road, which reminds us of how these mountains were sculpted to make it possible for the train tracks to pass through.

Shortly before completing the outward journey, the Pedret bridge appears majestically, built between the 13th and 14th centuries. Below, the water is green, crystal clear and cold, ideal for taking a dip. On the other side of the bridge is the pre-Romanesque church of Sant Quirze de Pedret. It is worth a visit before heading back.

name: Amposta to the Encanyissada – cycle touring in Catalonia
name_original: Desde Amposta a la Encanyissada – Cicloturismo en Cataluña
description:
The old Sirga road ran along the river bank and was closely linked to the llaguts, boats that navigated the Ebro transporting, mainly, rice upstream and materials scarce in the Delta (stones, wood, etc.) downstream. The llagut was dragged upstream from the bank by animals, the males, or by people, the sirgadors. Downstream, he took advantage of the current and could use a lateen sail to take advantage of the wind.

In the middle of the 20th century, with the improvement of road and rail communication and the construction of the Ribaroja, Flix and Mequinenza reservoirs, the Ebro definitively lost its role as a communication route and, consequently, meant the loss of navigation. river and llaguts.
The route has hardly any unevenness and, despite its length, this itinerary is suitable for all audiences.

Amposta is the starting point of the circular route along the old Sirga path. A gentle pedal ride takes you to the natural reserve of Sapinya Island. Here you can make a stop and observe the birds that choose this fantastic place to stopover on their migratory journey.

In the small town of Balada, there is one of the monumental trees of Catalonia known as the Balada oleander. In Sant Jaume d'Enveja you can visit the Barracas del Delta interpretation center. Further ahead, La Comandanta awaits you, a good example of riverside forest.

Although the birds accompany you along the entire path, where you can best see them is in the Tancada lagoon and, above all, in the Encanyissada lagoon, the largest in the Ebro delta. Depending on the time of year, you can contemplate specimens such as the collverd duck, the focja, the stilt and the crested grebe, among others. Also flamingos. Watching them fly between the orange lights of dawn and dusk is a priceless spectacle. To learn about all these species you can visit the Wooden House, which houses the interpretation center of the Ebro Delta natural park.

The last stop on this exciting Route can be made in La Ràpita, where you have the opportunity to taste the delicious local rice.

name: Castles of La Segarra – cycle touring in Catalonia
name_original: Castillos de la Segarra – Cicloturismo en Cataluña
description:
If you are passionate about the history of Catalonia, the route of this Route is ideal for you. The region of La Segarra is known as the land of castles, due to the large number of fortifications built there, some of which can be visited and are highly recommended. In the 11th-12th centuries in this place, crossed by the Sió River valley, the border between the Christian Catalan counties and Arab Al-Andalus was drawn. This turned this area into an unsafe space with strong defensive needs.

The tour begins in the town of Cervera, where you can arrive by train. To fully enjoy the experience and due to the unevenness, it is preferable to have a medium level of physical fitness. Although you can always use an electric bike or take a bus back in one of the towns it passes through.

A town on top of a hill presided over by a castle is a typical image of La Segarra, rightly called land of human towers. Along this Route you will get to know several of these specimens.

Cervera is the starting point of the Route. Here you can see the remains of his castle, which was a quadrangular fortress with cylindrical towers. A historical gem that is worth a visit.

After passing through the castle of Florejacs, you arrive at Les Palargues. It is an impressive fortress that has undergone various reforms, but still maintains a large Gothic arch at the entrance.

The current castle of Aranyó is the result of a complete remodeling in the 16th century. The writer Manuel de Pedrolo was born there. Now it is a private residence and its interior cannot be visited, but its magnificent architecture can be appreciated from the outside.

The small town of Montcortès has a magnificent castle declared a Cultural Asset of National Interest. Rectangular in plan, it exhibits two twin towers, a large voussoir doorway, Renaissance-style windows and a gallery of lowered arches on the upper floor.

With the image of all these fortresses, which speak of history and defy the passage of time, you arrive at Cervera, the capital of La Segarra and the end point of this tour.

The Route coincides in some sections with the route of the “Castells del Sió” Route (https://www.aralleida.cat/es/rutes/la-ruta-de-los-castillos-del-sio/).

name: Varradòs – cycle touring in Catalonia
name_original: Varradòs – Cicloturismo en Cataluña
description:
This circular route, of certain physical demand, takes you to know the most mythical mountains, valleys and rivers of the Aran Valley. It is ideal to enjoy on your rally bike, especially gravel. They are about 50 kilometers full of emotion that will surely leave you with a good taste in your mouth.

You travel through the beautiful valleys of the Garonne, Varradòs and Unhòla, where you will discover unique places such as the Saut deth Pish and the Còth de Varradòs. In addition, the Route passes through numerous towns, where you can regain energy and enjoy traditional mountain architecture: Gausach, Bagergue and Salardú, among others.

The proposal we make here takes advantage of the new Garonne cycle route and avoids the trail sections, to make it more affordable and more suitable for gravel cycling. You can complicate it a little by looking for the trails from the Solana de Vila, and also from Bagergue to Vielha. The Route can also start in any of the towns it passes through. Keep in mind that from Bagergue to Vielha it is not marked, it circulates on the wide shoulder of the road.

name: TransAlmeria gravel circular to Velez-Blanco
name_original: TransAlmería gravel circular a Vélez-Blanco
description:
The fourth stage of this Collection is totally optional, since it is a circular route that begins and ends in Vélez-Blanco itself. Furthermore, it is almost entirely a route along dirt tracks, with steep slopes and which in some places are not in very good condition. It also does not pass through towns, so you must carry with you everything you need for the day. If you are one of those who like strong emotions also in gravel and your tires have enough grip and width, you can leave your luggage at the accommodation and enjoy the mountain landscape of the Sierra de María - Los Vélez natural park.

You leave Vélez-Blanco towards the east, with the imposing Muela Grande on the horizon. This rocky plateau of more than 1,460 meters above sea level is located in the Sierra del Gigante. The Route borders the mountain range through the shade of Las Muelas, to continue northwards, towards the Sierra Larga. At kilometer 41 you enter a loop that surrounds the Gabar hill, where there are also caves with rock paintings. This circular is done along wide forest tracks in good condition, but if you want to avoid it, simply continue south, back to Vélez-Blanco.

Back in the town, you can visit its magnificent castle, which combines Gothic and Renaissance architecture and houses a museum of contemporary art. I also recommend visiting the convent of San Luis, and above all, taking a walk through its streets and admiring some of its unique houses, such as the Corredera house or the theater/hermitage of San Agustín.

name: Opposite Aneto – 3,404 metres (11,168 ft): highest point in the Pyrenees
name_original: Face à l’Aneto - 3 404 mètres : Point culminant des Pyrénées
description:
The Pyrenees range extends from the Atlantic Ocean to the Mediterranean Sea for more than 400 kilometers. A natural border between France and Spain, it has 212 peaks exceeding 3,000 meters in altitude. The highest point, Aneto, reigns on the Spanish slope at 3,404 meters above sea level.

This beautiful loop offers a panorama of the king of the Pyrenees, from one of its most beautiful viewpoints: the port of Vénasque. Bordering, this impressive passage is reached from the Pique valley at the cost of an ascent as demanding as it is majestic. The route begins at the Hospice de France, accessible by car from Luchon.

The climb begins immediately upon exiting the refuge in a remarkable green setting. This section includes more than 1,000 meters of elevation gain. As you progress, the environment evolves. The green softness gives way to mineral terrain. The Vénasque refuge and lakes allow you to take a break before finalizing the ascent to the pass.

Nestled between two rock walls, the port of Vénasque is a narrow passage at an altitude of 2,444 meters. In front of you is Aneto, the highest point of the Pyrenees, while behind you the view overlooking the Pique valley is just as extraordinary.

You are now operating on Spanish territory. This part begins with a pleasant section which offers magnificent views of the Esera valley. This section is done on a gradual descent before going back up towards the Pas de l'Escalette where you find France. A long descent follows which follows the border before arriving at the Hospice de France.

name: The crown of Pallars
name_original: La corona del Pallars
description:
On this Route we offer you the opportunity to test your resistance by climbing practically unseen dream roads in the Catalan Pyrenees, visiting beautiful villages perched on the mountain and visiting magical places such as Lake Montcortés or the Collegats gorge.

The figures are impressive, close to 132 kilometers and more than 2,500 meters of positive slope. If you are a cyclist with many accumulated kilometers and experience on your resume, you know that you will take advantage of every pedal stroke of this adventure, both in the sports section and on the stage.

The itinerary is circular from the La Pobla de Segur train station. As soon as you start rolling you head towards the Noguera Pallaresa course and, instead of entering the tunnels of the Collegats gorge, you take the external alternatives of the old road. This detour allows you to contemplate the cliffs from the bottom of the ravine and pass through the Argentería gorge, a limestone wall shaped by a water source that served as inspiration for Gaudí to shape his architecture.

As soon as you pass Montardit de Baix you turn to the right to ascend a slight band of asphalt that climbs twistingly from Savarneda to Puigforniu, continue to Soriguera and take the Pyrenean axis to crown the first ascent in Villamur. From here you descend with an incredible landscape to Sort to undertake a new climb that takes you through Rialp, Caregue, Sorre and Pujalt, among others, along a road that only the countrymen seem to know.

When you go back down to Sort, you pedal through the Collegats gorge again until just before Gerri de la Sal, where you turn right for the last ascent of the day that takes you winding up to the wonderful spot of the karst lake of Montcortés. From here you undertake a fun and scenic descent that passes through Pobleta del Bellveí and Senterada, on the Pyrenean axis road. To return to La Pobla de Segur you take an alternative that takes you to contemplate the impressive limestone ridges of Pallars. Back at the train station, your legs will surely feel tired, but your heart will be full from this epic adventure.

name: La Ametlla de Mar and Terres de l'Ebre
name_original: La Ametlla de Mar y Terres de l'Ebre
description:
Are you up for a swim in the Mediterranean after traveling more than 80 kilometers and climbing nearly 1,100 meters along unknown roads, maritime mountain ranges and accompanying the Ebro on its way to the sea? Put the swimsuit in one of the pockets of your jersey and continue reading.

We propose a circular route that leaves from Ametlla de Mar beach itself and takes you to Terres de l'Ebre. The route has a good climb, worthy of cyclists who enjoy sporting challenges. The Gavadà road is a very little traveled road that takes you through an almost virgin mountain landscape, to this abandoned medieval town.

From here you begin the descent towards the bank of the Ebro that takes you to Ginestar, where the landscape changes completely from the views of the mountain to the views of the river, its bank and the towns that are on the other side, such as Benissanet or Miravet, with its castle that appears between the treetops.

Before returning to Ametlla, you have another section of lower mountain through the Sierra del Cardó, passing through Perelló and continuing towards the sea among endless olive groves. Back on the beach, a dip is the perfect culmination of this adventure.

name: The mining route from Berga
name_original: La Ruta minera desde Berga
description:
If you're one of those cyclists who enjoys spending the day pedaling and racking up elevation gain while taking in spectacular mountain scenery, today is your day. We propose nothing less than climbing over 3,300 meters in just 120 kilometers on the asphalt roads of the Cadí-Moixeró Natural Park.

The route is a loop starting from Berga and is a series of challenging climbs of varying difficulty to test your endurance. And be sure to make room in your digital memory to capture the multitude of panoramic views that await you along the way.

The first climb appears just outside Berga: the Coll de Jouet—or Coll de la Mina. This offers a relatively easy 12-kilometer ascent, except for a few short ramps that reach a 14% gradient. The climb rewards you with panoramic views of the extensive forests covering the slopes, their harmony broken only by the sharp rocky crags that emerge from the foliage. From here, you have a fun descent to Sant Llorenç de Morunys, passing by the Llosa del Cavall reservoir.

If you've used the descent to rest your legs, you're ready for what's next. The Coll del Port awaits you with 20 kilometers of steady climbing to an altitude of 1,669 meters. If you want to take it easy, you can stop at La Pedra or Fuentes del Cardener for a break and a drink; otherwise, there's a roadside inn at the top where you can eat and drink.

The descent takes you to Tuixent, in the Josa River valley, from where you begin the third climb to the pass of the same name. From here, you'll enjoy magnificent views of the village of Gosól and its stunning surroundings. Gosól is a beautiful stone village where, as in the other towns, time seems to have stood still. Once you're back in its streets, you have another chance to recharge.

The next climb is to the Coll del Pradell, a seven-kilometer ascent through a beautiful pine forest. From there, a thrilling descent leads to the Plà de la Barraca, where the final climb of the day begins.

The Coll de la Barraca is only 200 meters high, but after this day of epic climbs, it can feel tough. The final descent offers incredible views that invite you to savor the moment and reflect on the day's events as you make your way back to Berga.

name: Cycling the landscapes of Priorat
name_original: Los paisajes del Priorat en bicicleta
description:
The most striking feature of this route is its resemblance to a bunch of grapes, like the thousands that hang from the vines climbing the steep slopes of the Priorat region. This loop around the Montsant mountain range's natural park offers a cycling adventure through wine country landscapes, past sharp rocks jutting from the ridges, olive and almond groves, and charming stone villages and hermitages nestled in the ravines.

The circular route from Falset accumulates approximately 1,800 meters of elevation gain over its just over 90 kilometers. While it doesn't feature any major climbs, the route's profile is characterized by numerous short, steep inclines. However, the panoramic views, along with the opportunity to ride along peaceful roads and stop in welcoming towns, are a well-deserved reward.

The Montsant Natural Park is the only one on the Costa Daurada and boasts a unique beauty. It's a mountain range covered in Mediterranean forest, dotted with the colors of terraced vineyards, and crowned by impressive rocky ridges that rise continuously across the landscape.

The route passes through several charming towns, including Gratallops, the spectacular Vilella Baixa—known as the New York of Priorat—Cabacés, La Bisbal de Montsant, Ulldemolins, Cornudella, and Porrera. All are ideal for getting lost in their narrow streets and enjoying local products.

At the end of the loop, back in Falset, you have the perfect opportunity to savor Priorat wines and raise a toast to this beautiful land and its magnificent roads and landscapes.

name: Cycling the Maresme Mountains
name_original: Los puertos del Maresme en bicicleta de carretera
description:
The Maresme coast is an ideal place to rack up miles on flat terrain, enjoy views of the Mediterranean, the mountains of the Serralada Litoral, its charming villages, beaches, coves, and cliffs. We propose a circular adventure from Calella, approximately 90 kilometers long with nearly 900 meters of elevation gain, through the mountain passes of the Maresme.

To begin, between Calella and El Masnou, you have 40 flat kilometers to get comfortable on the handlebars, join any of the groups of cyclists that frequent the N-II highway, and achieve an average speed to be proud of. That is, if you resist the temptation to stop at one of the terraces in the coastal towns like Sant Pol de Mar, Canet de Mar, or Arenys de Mar, among others.

The climb from El Masnou to Turó de les Monges is gentle and takes you through a vineyard area of the Alella Designation of Origin. Next, enjoy a fun and scenic descent to Vilanova del Vallès. From there, a rolling section leads to La Roca del Vallès, where the climb to Òrrius begins along a beautiful mountain road with stunning panoramic views of the Vallès region. Òrrius is an ideal spot to stop and recharge.

The descent takes you to Argentona and Mataró, where you rejoin the N-II coastal highway and retrace your steps back to the starting point. Before arriving back in Calella, you'll be treated to a view of its lighthouse and long beach. To finish, indulge in a culinary delight at one of its many restaurants.

description: This moderate 38.5-mile road cycling route from Tordesillas explores the rural landscapes and historical sites of Torrelobatón, Castrodeza,

name: Vielha to Pla de Beret
name_original: De Vielha al Pla de Beret
description:
This route could perfectly be called “Vielha to Plan de Beret Time Trial” and included as a stage in the Tour of Catalonia. Despite being just over 22 kilometres long, the route exceeds 900 metres of positive gradient.

We suggest two ways to tackle this route. The first, as a sporting challenge where you pedal at full speed to make a good time on this climb and smile after watching the time trial at the end. The second, climbing at a good pace, but enjoying the incredible Pyrenean landscape, stopping to take some photos of the mountains, ridges, peaks and slopes.

In any case, you can enjoy the climb and the descent and include some of the ports and roads surrounding Plan de Beret itself.

name: Gravel loop to 'l'Havana Xica' of Garraf
name_original: Vuelta en gravel a “l’Havana Xica” del Garraf
description:
“L’Havana xica” (little Havana) is the nickname by which Vilanova i la Geltrú, the capital of Garraf, was known. This name responds to the strong relationship that was established between this population and the island of Cuba from the 19th century to the beginning of the 20th. Numerous villains traveled to America and specifically to the Caribbean island to make their fortune. They returned converted into "Indians" or "Americans" (pronounced "americanus"), to show their wealth and aesthetic taste through the construction of palaces and monumental estates. This Route offers you a route that does not go far from the center of Vilanova i la Geltrú, from the sea to the hills that surround it, visiting some of these beautiful Indian houses.

You start the Route at the Vilanova i la Geltrú commuter station, to head south following the contour of the coast, to the Prat de Vilanova beach. You can usually skirt this beach by taking the GR 92.5 to the mouth of the Foix River. However, in my last check (November 2023), the section that runs parallel to the train track is impassable due to weeds. Although some cyclists and walkers pass by there, I preferred to take a detour inland and return to the beach a few kilometers later.

Once on the seafront, you continue in a southerly direction until you reach the mouth of the Foix River, which you follow inland. You now head to the highest point of the Route, in the first foothills of the Teixidor mountain range. From here you begin the descent to return to the fields and paths that surround Vilanova i la Geltrú. In this area you have your first contact with the Indian houses, such as Can Cabanyes or the Torre del Veguer.

At the end of the tour you enter the old town of Vilanova i la Geltrú again, where you can admire some buildings such as the Torre de la Inmortalitat. When you pass by Plaça de la Vila, it is worth stopping for a moment to admire Indian constructions such as the Olivella house. This central square of Vilanova i la Geltrú is the authentic nerve center of the Indian constructions of the capital of Garraf. The route ends a few meters further down, in the railway station square.

name: On gravel from the beach to the Buddhist monastery of Garraf
name_original: En gravel de la playa al monasterio budista del Garraf
description:
This Route proposes a trip from the beaches of Sitges to the popular Buddhist monastery of Garraf, a classic of walking and cycling excursions in the area. After touring the seafront promenade of Sitges, you head inland to visit nearby Sant Pere de Ribes. From there you head towards Olivella and begin the climb to the monastery. The return to Sitges takes you back to the sea, to the Vallcarca cove, and from there to climb again towards the hills of the Garraf massif, a prelude to the final descent to the end of the Route.

The route starts from the Sitges commuter station and goes through the old town through its narrow streets until it takes you directly to its beaches. This part of Sitges has a lot of charm, and includes, in addition to the well-known church of Sant Bartolomeu and Santa Tecla, the rectory, the Maricel palace and the Cau Ferrat museum. After walking the entire seafront of Sitges, you head towards Sant Pere de Ribes, first crossing a section of the old coastal path that leads to Vilanova i la Geltrú, a wide track with very fun slides.

Before reaching Sant Pere de Ribes you pass by the Xoriguera farmhouse, a spectacular modernist palace, an example of the wealth and aesthetic concerns of the area's early 20th century industrialists. Between vineyards and small forests you arrive at Sant Pere de Ribes along the narrow path that leads to its castle, a construction from the 10th century. After crossing the town, you take the Olivella road for three kilometers, to enter again the forest tracks of area. The last stretch of this track narrows until it becomes a fun path, sometimes half-hidden by vegetation.

Then you enter the bottom of the Querol, a track that winds gently ascending into the Garraf park towards the monastery. The climb is momentarily interrupted when descending towards the Masets de Jafra, a couple of hamlets now in ruins. Be careful with the descent, since in some sections the steep slope adds to the loose stone to increase the difficulty. After these ruins you resume the climb towards the monastery. The landscape opens up, and the blue of the sea takes center stage.

After passing through the monastery, a neo-Gothic and modernist palace built by the Indian Pere Domènech i Grau, you begin the descent towards Vallcarca cove, but the path immediately ascends again. Here you find the hardest ramps on the Route, a hard kilometer that takes you to the so-called Sitges track, which returns you to the sea and the end of this route.

name: The heart of the Garraf park in gravel
name_original: El corazón del parque del Garraf en gravel
description:
This Route takes you into the heart of the Garraf Park, a space of natural interest located in the massif of the same name. Due to its proximity to the sea and geological nature dominated by limestone, this space stands out for its landscape and karst modeling, full of caves and balmas and a typically Mediterranean climate. The human presence inside the park and the massif comes from the cultivation of vineyards and corn. The route runs mainly through sparsely populated areas, but along the Route you find vestiges of human activity in the form of dry stone cabins and lime kilns.

The tour begins in the town of Sant Pere de Ribes. After crossing the town you head towards Can Coll after crossing the bridge of the same name that spans the Ribes stream. Can Coll is a monumental farmhouse that stands out for its two-story gallery with twenty-three semicircular arch porticos. From there you pedal between the fields that separate this town from Sitges, towards the Garraf massif. The climb to the massif is done along the so-called Sitges track. To get to it, and avoid roads or access through urbanizations in the area, I have chosen a path that has an uphill section of about 300 meters in which there are several points with a lot of stones. You may have to push the bike.

The climb along the Sitges track is not very long. It is about two and a half kilometers with some hard sections, over 10%, but the road is in very good condition, and is even partially paved. When the slope stabilizes, you have a seven-kilometer stretch ahead of you, much gentler, with only the occasional short, hard slope. After crossing the road that goes up to Rat Penat, you enter the Villagrassa trail, a delight to do on a gravel bike. Then you enter the paved road that leads to Olivella. Here you will find the hardest ramps to reach the Garraf astronomical observatory, from where you continue along the asphalt to the charming and picturesque town of Olivella. In Olivella there is a “food truck” open from Wednesday to Sunday, much appreciated by cyclists and hikers for its sandwiches, cookies and cakes.

The return towards Sant Pere de Ribes follows a path parallel to the road, until in the Mas Milà urbanization, you enter the track that follows the bottom of the Querol. After almost two kilometers, you enter a narrow path with a gentle climb surrounded by pine trees and ferns. You find some stones, but they are the exception, it is a very beautiful and pleasant part of the Route! When leaving the path comes the descent, through the streets of the Can Pere de la Plana urbanization. Before returning to the starting point you cross a small forest where you ride along a path where roots and stones abound, but it is very cyclable and has enough flow, even for a gravel bike.

name: Climb to the Garraf viewpoint on gravel
name_original: Subida al mirador del Garraf en gravel
description:
This Route goes into the interior of the region, and gains altitude to contemplate Garraf from the privileged viewpoint of Puig de l'Aliga. It starts from Sant Pere de Ribes and proceeds along the area's slopes to the Parc d'Olèrdola, border with the neighboring region of l'Alt Penedès. The castle of Sant Miquel d'Olèrdola and its museum is another of the attractions of the Route, which returns to Sant Pere de Ribes after taking you to another exceptional viewpoint, in the so-called Fita dels Tres Termes.

After leaving Sant Pere de Ribes you take the road that leads to Olivella for just over four kilometers. After turning left through the Mas Mestre urbanization, you immediately enter the slopes that, in a gentle ascent, take you to the Parc d'Olèrdola. These tracks are in good condition but in some sections you find stones; Be careful especially on the descents. The small and picturesque town of Viladellops awaits you. There you find a bar and also the wineries of the same name, as well as a small hermitage and a Romanesque tower.

Your next destination is the Olèrdola castle, a monumental complex that includes Roman ruins and a 10th century church. Here the climbs begin to be considerable, although short, first on a track and the last few meters along the access road to the castle. And you still have to climb further to reach Puig de l'Aliga, the border between Garraf and Alt Penedès, from where you can see the coast and also the interior plain. After enjoying these magnificent views you continue towards the south. Be careful here with some downhill sections where the slope and stones require caution.

Once again you face the sea, entering a peculiar paved path that runs along various ridges until you reach the foot of the nearby hills of Vilanova i la Geltrú. The last section of the Route takes you back to Sant Pere de Ribes through a peri-urban area between crop fields and industrial areas. Finally, you arrive at Sant Pere along a narrow path that leaves you right at its picturesque castle.

name: Urepel loop
name_original: Boucle sur les hauteurs d'Urepel
description:
This beautiful loop of approximately 23 kilometers and 1,000 meters of elevation gain takes you to the heights of the Basque Country, dancing on the Franco-Spanish border. It starts from the small village of Urepel, nestled in the Aldudes valley.

The first kilometers have little elevation gain and allow you to enjoy lovely views of the village. You then go up a beautiful track which rushes into the forest alongside a peaceful stream. A first dry but short climb lifts you above this wooded part. You are about to enter Spain when you find a wider track.

Moving through a magnificent forest again, you make a slight downhill portion before going back up on a large ridge introduced by the Enokerri. Here, the landscape is discovered and the panoramas open up. A very pleasant path leads from peak to peak, such as Artsalgo Gaina and Argintzu. As you advance, the horizon expands and you discover some of the mysteries of the Basque Country.

You conclude this magnificent section by bypassing the Lohiluze, before diving into a long downhill section. This rushes into a magnificent and generous forest until it brings you back to your starting point, the village of Urepel.

name: Course des bergers (the shepherds' race)
name_original: La course des bergers en off
description:
Departing from Saint-Étienne-de-Baïgorry, the race of the shepherds is a pretty trail which offers a loop of approximately 23 kilometers and 1,350 meters of elevation gain. Nestled in the valley of the same name, this village combines nature and history in an atmosphere faithful to the Basque Country.

Saint-Étienne-de-Baïgorry has also become a trail resort which offers numerous routes on the mountains that surround it. As soon as you leave the village, you embark on the climb which leads to the highest point of the race: Oilandoi. You are immediately rewarded with superb panoramas, before descending to the Aharza pass. You follow the pass that you have just climbed before going back down on a small portion.

You then begin a new long climb which ends on Munhogain, close to your first summit. Once again you slide towards the Aharza pass. This time, it marks the start of a long, fairly rolling descent which gradually brings you back to your starting point.

The event, which usually takes place in early May, also includes another 15 kilometer race. You will find all the information about the race here:
https://www.artzain.fr/.

name: Ispeguy pass
name_original: Le col d'Ispeguy
description:
Located at an altitude of 672 meters, the Ispeguy Pass is an emblematic border crossing in the Basque Country. It forms the link between the Spanish valley of Baztan in Navarre and that of Aldudes in France. This route leads to the discovery of the pass from the village of Saint-Etienne-de-Baïgorry.

In a loop, the route begins with a relatively steep climb, crosses a wood which leads to the pass, then concludes with a long gradual descent on a magnificent path. As soon as you leave the village, the route begins on a path that goes straight up the slope. On open ground, you climb along a wide ridge which leads to Elhorriko Kaskoa.

The highest point of the route, this summit offers sweeping views of the Aldudes valley from where you started. You then head towards Ispeguy following a path that goes deep into the forest. This pleasant section wanders along the foothills of the French-Spanish border.

When you reach Ispeguy Pass, the forest dissipates and many vistas are revealed. On the one hand the mythical Basque peaks of Rhune and Pic d'Orhy, on the other hand the Béarnaise mountains with the Anie and Gourette massifs. The terrace of the inn located there allows you to take a break to savor these beautiful views.

The last part of the route consists of returning to Saint-Etienne-de-Baïgorry via a long and magnificent descent. The path, very pleasant, dominates the road which leads to the pass by a few meters. You move along the foothills of the Iparla ridges in contact with open terrain, before plunging into the forest as you approach the village. The outing ends with small sections of roads which lead to the starting point.

name: Buztanzelai
description:
Located between the peaks of Iparla and Hautza, Buztanzelai is less crowded than its neighbors. However, this summit, easy to access, offers beautiful views of the cliffs which demarcate the border between France and Spain. The loop route starts from the center of the village of Saint-Etienne-de-Baïgorry.

The route is mainly divided into two parts: a first section of climb up to the ridges of Iparla, then a long section of descent which runs through the heart of the Baigorri valley. The path begins by crossing the town of Leizparze, before immersing itself in a small forest.

As you climb, the slopes become steeper and the terrain clears. Behind you, the view opens up over the Baigorri valley and its villages. You finally reach the ridge line at Astateko Pass. A final ascent brings you Buztanzelai. Bordering, it offers superb views of the Spanish and French slopes, as well as the cliffs of Iparla.

The long descent which slides down to the village follows the GR 10 route. However, before you set off in pursuit of its red and white markings, I suggest a short detour via the Aintziaga. This new summit, a belvedere of the Baigorri valley, offers other perspectives on the surrounding area. The descent takes place on a fun and playful path until reaching the center of Saint-Etienne-de-Baïgorry.

name: Hargibel
name_original: L'Hargibel
description:
This beautiful loop offers an immersion in the heart of the Aldudes valley, while traversing its heights. The departure is in the center of the village of Aldudes which shares the eponymous valley with its neighbors Banca and Urepel. This charming locality takes its name from the contraction of the Basque word “aldubide” which means “path to the summits”. This etymology perfectly illustrates the course of the route.

The first part of the route aims to climb the border ridge which marks the separation between France and Spain. The route climbs through a beautiful beech forest before the terrain opens up to offer the first views of the Aldudes valley. From then on, the slope softens and the path crisscrosses on both sides of the border.

Take the time to observe the superb panoramas on both sides. The beautiful rounded hills alternate with some rocky portions. Below the summit, just before reaching it, don't miss Harrikulunka, the tilting rock. This impressive, swinging stone appears to have been placed here by some mystical force.

The Hargibel then offers itself to you, dominating a beautiful and wide ridge line. Here nature mixes with history and religion. This section is punctuated with megalithic monuments dating from the first millennium BC. These sites testify to the existence of ancient funeral rites.

After this leap in time, you return to the starting point by following the HRP (Haute Route Pyrénéenne) path. The path is dotted with beautiful beech trees which accompany the descent, while allowing a glimpse of the landscape.

name: Ricobayo Reservoir Loop
name_original: De Tierra Alba por los pueblos de la ribera del Río Aliste — circular por Vegalatrave
description: This difficult 45.3-mile route near Vegalatrave follows the Río Aliste through villages and historical sites, best suited for mountain bikin

name: Lindus
name_original: Lindus
description:
This beautiful loop offers a little-used route on the borders of the Aldudes valley. From the village of Urepel located at the foot of a mountain belt, you gradually gain altitude until you reach the border summit of Lindus.

The last French village in the Aldudes valley, Urepel and its fish farms are located at the foot of the majestic Basque mountains. You start by going around some of the peaks that dominate the town. This first part takes place on open ground before entering an unfathomable forest.

For a moment, the trees part to let a road pass. This is the Col d’Hauzay, located on the route of the HRP (Haute Route Pyrénéenne). There you will find a small tap to fill up with water before continuing the ascent to the next pass.

Burdinkurutxeta marks your arrival on the French-Spanish border. An invisible line, it runs up to the heights of Lindus not far away. At the cost of a final effort you reach the summit.

The highest point of the route, the Lindus offers wonderful perspectives on the slopes of neighboring countries. Savor the panoramas that stretch to the high Pyrenean peaks on a clear day. You then pass at the foot of several Spanish peaks before beginning a long descent into the Aldudes valley. The path is populated with beautiful vegetation which accompanies the descent, while allowing a glimpse of the landscape until your return to Urepel.

name: Iparla ridges
name_original: Crêtes d'Iparla
description:
This large loop leads to the discovery of the magnificent ridges of Iparla. Dizzying and spectacular, they offer magnificent panoramas of the Basque Country and the Pyrenees. This natural rampart also marks the border between Spain and France.

The starting point is in the center of Saint-Étienne-de-Baïgorry. The first part consists of weaving through the neighborhoods surrounding the village, to get closer to the foot of the ridges. It is after the small hamlet of Urdoz that the slope begins to rise seriously. You embark on a climb of several kilometers, the last portion of which is the steepest.

Once the summit is reached, the panorama rewards all the efforts made. When the sky is clear, the view stretches to the Béarn peaks of the Pyrenees. While on the other side, the view of the Basque Country and its Atlantic coast is just as spectacular.

You then embark for the flagship section of this route. By following the GR 10 route, you take a magnificent path which runs along the ridges. This climbs quickly to the Iparla peak, the highest point of the route, before climbing a number of peaks including Astate. Although the trail approaches the edge of the vertiginous cliffs, it does not involve any difficulty.

This wonderful passage ends at the Ispeguy Pass. Bordering the border, this emblematic passage of the Basque Country includes an inn which offers an ideal setting for taking a break and refueling. The route ends with a gradual and very fun descent, on a path which runs through the foothills of the ridges until reaching the starting point.

name: Highest point of the Aldudes
name_original: Point culminant des Aldudes
description:
This magnificent route runs from the border village of Urepel to the highest point of the Aldudes valley: the Adi. Also called “Ahadi” in Basque, it rises to an altitude of 1,457 meters. Shared between France and Spain, the route offers you an immersion in authentic and preserved nature.

This journey begins in the center of Urepel, nestled in a basin at the foot of the border mountains. Leaving the village you embark on a long uphill session which initially leads to the Korrotako pass. The Isterbergi, peaking slightly above 1,000 meters above sea level, symbolizes your visit to Spain.

After a slight descent, it is a steep climb which concludes your ascent of the Adi. Arriving at the top, the 360 degree panorama is marvelous. On a clear day, many peaks present themselves to you. Savor this magnificent spectacle which rewards the efforts made to achieve it.

Before heading towards the heart of Aldudes, the path wanders past other peaks in the mountainous belt of the valley. Whether you pass over their summits or their foothills, they offer spectacular portions. The route ends on a path that plunges into the bottom of the valley, wandering through its beautiful forests.

name: Heights of Ispeguy
name_original: Hauteurs d’Ispeguy
description:
This route follows a beautiful ridge line that separates France from Spain. You juggle between the heights of the Baigorri and Baztan valleys in Spanish Navarre. From the Ispeguy pass to Hautza in a round trip, this route has a nice kilometer-altitude difference ratio.

Appreciated by cyclists, the Ispeguy Pass is an ideal starting point where many converge. The GR 10, a major hiking trail crossing the Pyrenees, passes not far from there. There is parking there as well as an inn-restaurant. The route begins on the GTR 5 path which runs along the hillside on the French slope. You deviate from it to climb Elhorriko Kaskoa, a natural viewpoint in the Baigorri valley. The view of the vertiginous cliffs of Iparla shares the panorama with the first Pyrenean peaks of Béarn.

You then head towards Hautza, following an uphill section which runs along a relatively wide ridge. Here you are juggling the Franco-Spanish border. You finally reach the highest point of the route which is located exclusively in Spain. L’Hautza offers panoramas of the Spanish side and its Baztan valley. On the other side, on a clear day, you can see the peak of Anie.

The last section of the route consists of returning to the Ispeguy pass on a part which is mainly downhill. This time you take a path which runs along the Spanish slope overlooking the Baztan valley. When you reach the inn, its terrace and kitchen invite you to enjoy beautiful plates of Iberian ham, valley trout, or other Spanish specialties.

name: Baigorri loop
name_original: Boucle de Baigorri
description:
This magnificent route sets out to discover the Baigorri valley and the Aldudes. On the program, some of its emblematic peaks, its mysterious beech groves and its villages of character. The start of this large loop is in Saint-Étienne-de-Baïgorry, located at the entrance to the valley. You start by climbing to Oilarandoi peak. This accessible summit offers the first panoramas of the Aldudes and the mountains that surround it.

You continue on a portion which mainly includes descent, until you reach the village of Banca. Renowned for its trout farms, this small town is nestled in the heart of the valley. A small fountain allows you to refill your water before starting a long section of climb.

From Banca, you climb continuously to the border ridge which separates France from Spain. You walk along this invisible demarcation for a moment towards Hautza. Located at an altitude of 1,306 meters on the Spanish slope, it is the highest point of the route. This rocky summit offers a 360-degree panorama that stretches from the Baigorri valley to the Pyrenees peaks.

A pleasant and fun descent leads to the Ispeguy pass. It is perfectly located to take a final break before starting the long descent back to the starting point. This border crossing includes a hostel which has a superb view of the Baigorri valley. A short detour via the Aintziaga offers a nice view of the impressive ridges of Iparla, before diving towards Saint-Étienne-de-Baïgorry. It is the red and white markings of the GR 10 which are responsible for bringing you back to your destination to complete this superb route.

name: Saint-Étienne-de-Baïgorry to Saint-Jean-Pied-de-Port
name_original: De Saint-Étienne-de-Baïgorry à Saint-Jean-Pied-de-Port
description:
This route takes up a classic stage of the GR 10, the one which links Saint-Étienne-de-Baïgorry to Saint-Jean-Pied-de-Port. The route, particularly well marked, winds as close as possible to the summits of Oilarandoi, Munhogain and Munhoa. Note that a bus connection between the villages is operated by line 10 of the Txik Txak company. All the information here: https://www.txiktxak.fr/.

You leave the center of Saint-Étienne-de-Baïgorry following the red and white markings of the GR 10 which will be your guide for the day. The route begins with a steep climb which takes place in a shaded area. When the vegetation becomes scarce, a beautiful view of the Aldudes valley is offered to you.

The path continues around the Oilarandoi peak and its summit chapel. If you wish, a variant climbs there before returning to the GR 10 a little further on. Otherwise the route evolves on the sides of the mountain towards the Aharza pass. From here, you explore the foothills of a new summit, that of Munhogain, which you can also cross with a small detour.

The Munhoa and its 1,023 meters of altitude constitute the highest point of the outing. It offers beautiful views of the adjoining valleys before embarking on the descent. This last section is done via wide tracks then ends with the road, near Lasse and its palombières.

Saint-Jean-Pied-de-Port then extends its arms to you. This medieval town of character and typical of the Basque Country forms an ideal destination. The numerous restaurants, bars and shops present provide a wonderful conclusion to your outing.

name: Gravel tour from Olot through the Garrotxa natural park
name_original: Vuelta gravel desde Olot por el parque natural de la Garrotxa
description:
The nerve center of the Garrotxa, Olot is an excellent starting point for the first ring on Spanish roads. Head along the river in a north-west direction, towards Hostalnou de Bianya, where a first glimpse of woods and dirt roads will give you an idea of what awaits you. Once you have crossed the asphalt provincial road, take a fast serpentine that recalls the valleys of the Flemish hills to reach Sant Joan les Fonts. Here the church of San Giovanni Battista, a waterfall, a Roman bridge and a panoramic point over some ancient lava flows will delight you.

Continue on country roads along the Fluvià river to reach a truly surprising destination, Castellfollit de la Roca. Located on a hill of basalt rock, Castellfollit is part of the Garrotxa Volcanic Zone Nature Reserve, which with over 40 volcanic cones that have been extinct for centuries is considered the most significant volcanic area of the Iberian peninsula. If up to now the road has always or almost always been slightly downhill, at the exit of Besalù a climb on a mixed concrete and asphalt surface allows you to enter a path that is as lost in the woods as it is magnificent.

It is a very long track, full of ups and downs and dust: a true paradise for those who love gravel. Don't let yourself be found without water here, because you will only come across one fountain near the town of El Torn. Go up the Ser river to Santa Pau, where you can take a break before entering the actual volcano area. Just beyond the very black volcano of Rocanegra, tie your bike to some trees (it is forbidden to cycle up there) and walk up to the cone of the Santa Margherita volcano: it is not a long walk and allows you to observe the spectacular little church built inside of the extinguished cone. You can do the same thing, parking your bike at the park ranger's house, for the Croscat volcano: one of its slopes has been excavated, so you will have the impression of seeing inside the belly of the volcano itself. An incredible show.

Subsequently, a silent beech forest takes you back towards Olot in the best possible way. The first day in Garrotxa could not have been more exciting.

name: The Néouvielle lakes via Col d'Estoudou
name_original: Les lacs de Néouvielle par le col d'Estoudou
description:
This pretty loop offers a variation on the traditional hike that leads to the Aubert and Aumar lakes. Its first portion, less frequented, offers a slightly steeper climb than that of the classic route. The start is at the Lake Orédon car park.

From the start, the trail climbs into a dense and lush forest. Paved with stones and roots, it quickly gains height above the lake. When the ground finally clears, you approach the Estoudou pass.

This crossing point leads to Lake Oule, it also offers a bird's eye view of the road which serves Lake Orédon. However, you take a different direction by following the sign indicating Lake Aumar. This new section begins with a slight descent on a path that runs along the side of the mountain. Then you gradually go up until you reach the lake which you follow for a beautiful part.

You then head towards its neighbor, Lac d’Aubert. It is at the dam that you approach the final part of the route which consists of returning to the starting point. Along the way, you pass along the magical banks of Les Laquettes, before concluding with a more technical portion in the forest.

name: Lac de l'Oule Tour
name_original: Le tour du lac de l’Oule
description:
This pretty route, accessible to all, takes a loop that goes around Lake Oule. Artificial, it is one of the largest in the Néouvielle reserve. The departure is at the free Artigusse car park, located at around 1,600 meters above sea level.

The first part of the route avoids most of the elevation gain of the route. This portion is on a fairly wide track which winds through a beautiful and dense forest. When the slope softens and the forest becomes less dense, you approach the lake. You discover it when you arrive under its imposing dam.

The first part of the path which goes around it always takes place at the edge of the forest. On the other side of the lake is the Col du Portet, taken by the GR 10. Two kilometers further, you reach a small cabin and a bridge which offer a clear view of the lake. This is where you begin the U-turn towards the starting point.

You are moving on the other side of the lake. Much clearer, it offers pretty panoramas. The tour of the body of water ends at the Oule refuge. Charming and welcoming, it allows a pleasant break near the dam, before starting the descent towards the Artigusse car park.

name: Aubert and Aumar lakes
name_original: Les lacs d'Aubert et d'Aumar
description:
This route, accessible to practitioners of all levels, offers two of the most famous and popular lakes in the Néouvielle reserve. The Aubert and Aumar lakes are two superb bodies of water that overlap at the foot of magnificent mountains. They have the advantage of being served by a shuttle from June 1 to September 30, for seven euros for a round trip. They are also accessible by car except between 9:30 a.m. and 6 p.m.

The departure point is in the paid parking lot at Lake Orédon. You follow a path which immediately plunges into a beautiful pine forest. This first portion includes the most elevation gain. You gradually climb to the heights of the lake, on a fairly stony path. When you reach Les Laquettes, the path softens and goes up the banks of the lake in a magical atmosphere.

The clear waters of the lake contrast with the steep, jagged mountains that border it. As you progress, you can see the peak of Néouvielle in the distance. Like a lighthouse, it guides the ascent towards the next lake, that of Aubert. You access it by arriving under its dam.

A wide track goes up the body of water towards the parking lot where the shuttle arrives. There you will find signs indicating the way to the next lake, that of Aumar. Bordered by pretty lawns, it is ideal for a pleasant break or a refreshing swim. The return takes place on the same path, exclusively downhill.

name: Vía de la Plata Greenway Loop
name_original: Del prado de las Pavas a la dehesa del Mosteruelo — Benavente
description: Enjoy an easy 10.2-mile touring cycling route from Las Pavas to Mosteruelo near Benavente, offering pastoral views and minimal elevation gai

name: The Madamète lakes
name_original: Les lacs de Madamète
description:
This route, without major difficulty, leads to the discovery of the Madamète lakes and their countless neighbors. From the perfectly equipped resort of Tournaboup, you set off for a spectacular immersion in the Néouvielle reserve.

The route begins at the station car park, before entering the superb Gaubie valley. There you find the GR 10 to enter this peaceful and preserved valley. Bridges and ruins bear witness to centuries-old pastoral activity as you advance. The slope gradually rises towards the Aygues Cluses refuge. Brand new, it allows you to refuel or fill up with water in a remarkable setting.

The climb continues and you quickly reach the Madamète lakes. Nestled at the foot of the mountains, their green banks contrast with the raw and rocky environment that surrounds them. A final uphill effort then leads to the Col de Tracens. The view of the Madamète lakes and the other slope rewards the efforts made to reach the highest point of the route.

The last portion of the route essentially consists of descending to return to the starting point in Tournaboup. Along the way, you discover an anthology of lakes: Nère, Tracens, Blanc and Dets Coubous. Be careful not to lose sight of the route in the rocky portions that border some of them. When you find the Gaubie valley, all you have to do is continue until the finish of the course.

name: Marcadau Valley
name_original: La vallée du Marcadau
description:
This hike, accessible to all levels, offers a lovely route which winds through the hollow of the Marcadau valley. Imbued with a unique atmosphere where history and pastoralism blend wonderfully with nature, it begins at the paid parking lot of the Pont d'Espagne. Note that if you wish to split this stage in two, it is possible to spend the night at the Wallon-Marcadau refuge, located in the middle of the route.

The route passes quickly above the Gave du Marcadau to reach the restoration sites located at the Pont d’Espagne. A beautiful waterfall is located slightly upstream. The route continues to climb towards the Clot plateau and its pretty eponymous chalet.

As the water flows, you gradually sink into the Marcadau valley. Crossed by the eponymous watercourse, it spreads out over several small plateaus. They are crossed without difficulty while following the banks of the Gave. In winter, this site offers beautiful ski touring trails, while in summer it is a delight for fly fishermen.

A short climb in the woods brings you to a new plateau which offers superb views of the mountains. Not far from there, you reach the Wallon-Marcadau refuge. Freshly renovated, it is possible to spend the night there or take a nice break before turning back into the valley. The return takes approximately the same route as the outward journey, with the only difference being that it takes the other bank of the Gave du Marcadau.

name: Lac de Gaube and Refuge des Oulettes
name_original: Le lac de Gaube et le refuge des Oulettes
description:
This is one of the most emblematic hikes in the Pyrenees. This pretty route connects the Pont d’Espagne to the superb Oulettes refuge. To do this, the route follows the markings of the GR 10 route. Along the way, you will discover the famous Gaube lake and incomparable landscapes. If you wish, you can limit yourself to the part that leads to the lake, which is already a very nice outing.

From the paid parking lot of the Pont d'Espagne, you quickly reach this iconic footbridge. Located between two rock walls downstream of a beautiful waterfall, the bridge sets the tone from the start. Following the red and white marks of the GR 10, you immerse yourself in a beautiful Scots pine forest. The clicking of the stream punctuates your steps as you progress.

As you advance, Lake Gaube reveals itself. Arriving at the hotel, treat yourself to an unforgettable break. Lake Gaube is one of the best known in the Pyrenees. The beauty of this site inspired a multitude of romantic artists, including Victor Hugo. The eternal snows of Vignemale are reflected in its clear waters.

The route continues on a very pleasant section which runs along its banks. Then, a brief climb leads to a large plateau nestled in the hollow of the valley. Here, pastoralism mixes with wildlife. By being attentive, you can observe numerous marmots around the rocks which litter the path.

A small waterfall presents itself to you, offering a superb panorama, with the Vignemale in the background. At the end of the valley, you reach the Oulettes refuge. Its exceptional panorama of the Vignemale rewards all the efforts made. Culminating at 3,298 meters, it is the highest peak in the French Pyrenees. Enjoy every second of your encounter before turning back, taking the same route as on the way out.

name: The cirque de Gavarnie
name_original: Le cirque de Gavarnie
description:
The Gavarnie cirque is one of the most remarkable sites in the Pyrenees and France. Victor Hugo described it thus: “It is a mountain and a wall at the same time; it is the most mysterious building from the most mysterious of architects; it is the colossus of nature; it’s Gavarnie.” Listed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site since 1997, it is home to the largest waterfall in mainland France which flows over 422 meters!

This hike starting from Gavarnie has the advantage of being accessible to as many people as possible. This route includes a variant which takes a little height to approach a balcony path which winds along the circus. However, if you wish, you can follow the classic path which is confined to the bottom of the valley.

From the car park, you cross Gavarnie and its many small shops which dot the start of the route. After a little over a kilometer, follow the signs for the Haute Route Pyrénéenne (HRP) which goes up towards the Chalet du Pailla. When you reach this small shelter, you are done with the climb. You then take the “balcony trail” which runs towards the Gavarnie cirque on a pleasant section which gradually descends.

The path leads to the circus hotel. Ideal for a short break, it is only a few hundred meters from the foot of the Gavarnie waterfall. Take the time to visit him before retracing your steps. Back at the hotel, follow the HRP signs again which take you back to Gavarnie.

name: Campbieil peak
name_original: Le pic du Campbieil
description:
The hike that leads to the Campbieil peak is probably one of those that offers one of the most complete panoramic views of the Pyrenees. Aneto, Mont-Perdu, Brèche de Roland, the Gavarnie cirque, the Néouvielle massif, are all part of the landscapes that the summit offers. Culminating at 3,173 meters, it is one of the most accessible 3,000 meters. Its relatively significant difference in altitude, however, requires you to be in good physical condition and to devote enough time to it.

The start is at Lake Cap de Long, perched at an altitude of 2,160 meters. Accessible by car, it has an impressive dam which overlooks the valley. The loop begins with a quiet part which goes down to the Estaragne valley. This is where the first climb begins which climbs to the pass of the same name. From here, you can see the two objectives of the day. In the foreground the peak of Estaragne, then that of Campbieil which is behind.

The trail climbs gradually below these two peaks of more than 3,000 meters. From time to time, it approaches a dizzying ridge which offers spectacular views. Once you arrive at Campbieil, the panorama opens up and allows you to discern the Mont-Perdu massif, the Gavarnie cirque and all the neighboring peaks of the Néouvielle massif. On a clear day, Aneto, the highest point in the Pyrenees, can be seen in the distance.

You then begin a long descent which plunges into the Cap de Long valley. This section is broken down into several portions. First of all, a beautiful path on small gravel, followed by a fairly rocky portion before ending with a beautiful balcony overlooking the lake.

name: Bastan lakes
name_original: Les lacs de Bastan
description:
This pretty route ventures to the heights of Lake Oule to discover the isolated lakes of Bastan. Nestled among them, the refuge of the same name welcomes you halfway. The route begins from the Artigusse car park, at around 1,600 meters above sea level.

Initially, the route climbs on a wide track in the forest. This leads to the Oule Lake dam. Built by the Midi railway company, it swallows up meadows which were once used as pastures. You go around it via its western banks, bordered by forest.

A small stone hut then crossing a bridge means that you have reached the end of the lake. From there, you attack a beautiful uphill section on terrain that gradually clears up. The environment is becoming more and more mineral. The lower lake of Bastan provides a first enchanted interlude. Lurking in the mountains, it stands in a magnificent natural setting.

A short climb then leads to the middle lake of Bastan. Dominated by the eponymous refuge, this body of water is larger than the previous one. Its raw and jagged banks give it a singular shape. From the refuge you can easily reach the upper Bastan lake. Otherwise, take the time to enjoy a nice break and a good meal before starting the long descent back to the starting point.

name: Taillon Peak
name_original: Le pic du Taillon
description:
The Taillon peak is one of the easiest peaks to access at over 3,000 meters in the Pyrenees. It peaks at 3,144 meters above sea level, on the French-Spanish border, in the Monte Perdido massif.
Le Taillon offers a breathtaking view of several remarkable sites in the Pyrenees. Note that, if you wish, you can shorten the route by stopping at Brèche de Roland.

The route starts at the Col de Tentes car park located upstream of the Gavarnie ski resort. The first section remains relatively flat until reaching the Taillon glacier waterfall. Be careful if some snowfields persist when you cross the waterfall. The climb becomes steeper on rocky terrain which takes you to the Sarradets refuge. Freshly renovated, it offers a very nice break with a direct view of the famous Breccia de Roland and, further on, the Gavarnie cirque and its immense waterfall. Take the opportunity to stock up on water before climbing up to the breach.

This imposing wall marks the border with Spain. When you cross it, a completely different landscape opens up to you and you can see the Ordesa canyon in the distance. You follow the cliffs of La Brêche on the Spanish side towards Taillon. You pass a large rocky mound nicknamed the Taillon finger, then the final ascent begins.

At the top, the spectacle is simply grandiose. The diversity of natural elements seems unlimited: the Gavarnie cirque, the Roland breccia, Mont Perdu, the Ordesa canyon and the Vignemale.

This hike can be done in one day, but you can sleep at the Sarradets refuge or bivouac at the summit of Taillon. I highly recommend it to offer you an unforgettable sunrise and sunset. You can also find a few pitches at the foot of the Brèche de Roland, on the Spanish side.

name: Seven Hanging Valleys Trail
name_original: Sete Vales Suspensos — percurso circular
region: Lagoa e Carvoeiro e Carvoeiro e Carvoeiro
description: Jog this moderate 7.3-mile (11.8 km) coastal route along the Sete Vales Suspensos, featuring cliffs, beaches, and the Alfanzina Lighthouse.

name: Antas das Pedras Altas Trail
name_original: Antas das Pedras Altas — percurso circular (PR9 TVR)
description: Hike the moderate 6.0-mile Antas das Pedras Altas Trail to discover ancient megalithic monuments and panoramic views in Portugal's Algarve r

name: Masmorra Trail
name_original: Antas da Masmorra — percurso circular (PR8 TVR)
description: Explore the easy 3.4-mile Antas da Masmorra circular route, featuring ancient megaliths and rural Algarve landscapes near Mealha.

name: Slopes of Raposeira Trail
name_original: Pelas Encostas da Raposeira — percurso circular (PR4 VBP)
description: Hike the moderate 8.4-mile Pelas Encostas da Raposeira circular route (PR4 VBP) to discover coastal views, ancient menhirs, and diverse terr

name: Slopes of Raposeira Trail
name_original: Pelas Encostas da Raposeira — percurso circular (PR4 VBP)
description: Jog the Pelas Encostas da Raposeira — circular route (PR4 VBP), a moderate 8.4-mile trail with coastal views and ancient menhirs.

name: Port of Vénasque
name_original: Le port de Vénasque
description:
This hike takes you to the gates of Spain on one of the most impressive border crossings: the port of Vénasque. A real fault nestled between two rock walls, this gateway offers a fabulous panorama of the largest peak in the Pyrenees: Aneto.

Concretely, the route is divided into two distinct parts: the first which is carried out exclusively uphill to the crossing point, then the descent via the same path.
The climb begins immediately at the exit of the Hospice de France, located in a remarkable green setting. This section includes more than 1,000 meters of elevation gain. As you progress, the environment evolves. The green softness gives way to mineral and rocky terrain. The Vénasque refuge and lakes allow you to take a break before the final part of the climb.

Nestled between two rock walls, the port of Vénasque is a narrow passage at an altitude of 2,444 meters. In front of you stands Aneto, king of the Pyrenees from its height of 3,404 meters, while behind you the view overlooking the Pique valley is just as extraordinary. Savor these breathtaking views before returning to the starting point on the same path.

name: Don Quixote Trail
name_original: Dom Quixote — percurso circular (PR1 TVR)
description: Hike the moderate Dom Quixote — circular route (PR1 TVR) for 10.3 miles (16.6 km) through varied Algarve landscapes and rural charm.

name: Incles Valley Loop
