name_original: Vall d'Incles — circular desde Soldeu
description: Moderate 4.4-mile touring cycling route through Andorra's scenic Vall d'Incles from Soldeu, featuring forests and wildflowers.

name: In Search of the Enchanted Valley Trail
name_original: Em Busca do Vale Encantado — percurso circular (PR8 ACT)
description: Jog the difficult 8.6-mile In Search of the Enchanted Valley — circular route (PR8 ACT) through the scenic Algarve interior.

name: Coma de Ransol Cycle Route
name_original: Coma de Ransol desde Canillo — ida y vuelta por la Sant Joan de Caselles y la Cascada de les Moles
description: Cycle a moderate 11.5-mile route from Canillo to Coma de Ransol, passing Sant Joan de Caselles Church and Cascada de les Moles.

name: Envalira Pass Trail
name_original: Les Bordes d'Envalira desde Meritxell — ida y vuelta por el Vall d'Orient
description: This difficult 17.7-mile touring cycling route from Meritxell offers challenging climbs and dramatic mountain scenery through the Vall d'Ori

name: Tosa de Losada from El Tarter — circular
name_original: Tosa de Losada desde El Tarter — circular
description: A difficult 10.1-mile (16.3 km) touring cycling route from El Tarter, gaining 2914 feet (888 metres) to Tosa de Losada summit.

name: Rocha dos Soídos Loop from Alte
name_original: A Rocha dos Soidos partindo de Alte — percurso circular
description: This moderate 10.8-mile (17.3 km) road cycling route from Alte features the challenging Alto dos Soidos climb and scenic Algarve views.

name: Lagos and Salema Coastal Loop
name_original: De Lagos até Salema — percurso circular
description: This moderate 24.9-mile (40.0 km) road cycling route from Lagos to Salema offers coastal views and charming villages with 1537 feet (468 met

name: Telheiros do Barro Trail (PR11 TVR)
name_original: Telheiros do Barro — percurso circular (PR11 TAV
region: Santa Catarina Da Fonte Do Bispo
description: Hike the moderate 7.6-mile Telheiros do Barro Trail (PR11 TVR) in Tavira, featuring varied terrain and sea views from Boavista.

name: From Salema to Monte Ruivo — circular route
name_original: De Salema até Monte Ruivo — percurso circular
description: This moderate 39.8-mile (64.0 km) road cycling route from Salema to Monte Ruivo offers a challenging ride through a national park.

name: Alcoutim and Guadiana River Loop
name_original: De Vila Real de Santo António até Tavira — percurso circular
description: This difficult 100.2-mile (161.3 km) road cycling route from Vila Real de Santo António to Tavira offers diverse Algarve scenery and histori

name: Faro - Alto do Malhão Loop
name_original: De Faro até o Alto do Malhão — percurso circular
description: This difficult 96.2-mile (154.7 km) circular road cycling route from Faro to Alto do Malhão offers panoramic views and a challenging climb.

name: D'Orhy peak
name_original: Le pic d'Orhy
description:
This superb hike leads to the top of the Basque Country while exploring its surroundings. Departing from the Iraty chalets, the round trip itinerary begins with an immersion in the largest beech forest in Europe: the Iraty forest. A true Basque natural treasure, its 20,000 hectares are spread between France and Spain while sheltering a number of remarkable plant and animal species.

The start of the route plunges into the thousand-year-old massif from the Bagargiak pass. The trees quickly part and the ridges of Organbidexka offer a breathtaking view of the Pic d’Orhy. You will continue on a relatively flat portion until you reach the Millagate pass.

Along the way, savor the panoramas that stretch towards the horizon. As you move along a magnificent portion of the ridge, the slope becomes steeper. Be careful not to stray from the main trail. When the peak of Orhy appears on the horizon, there is not much left for you to climb.

Arriving at the summit, you are at the highest point in the Basque Country, at 2,017 meters above sea level. The 360 degree panorama is magnificent. The Atlantic coast, the Basque Country, Béarn, the Pyrenees peaks: your eyes no longer know where to rest. All you have to do is return to the starting point by the same route as on the way out. When you arrive at the Iraty chalets, you will find a restaurant there, but also a shop to perfectly conclude this beautiful hike.

name: Jaizkibel mountain range
name_original: Le Jaizkibel
description:
Here is a lovely route forming a 17 kilometer loop in the Spanish Basque Country. Located a few steps from the French border, it offers an affordable elevation gain given its length.
The departure is at the parking lot of the Guadalupe sanctuary on the heights of Hondarribia, “Hondarribia” in Spanish. This seaside resort is separated from Hendaye by the bay of Chingoudy.

The route begins directly with an uphill climb, towards Mount Erramutz. Along the way, take the time to turn around to admire the superb view of the bay. The trail continues to climb to Jaizkibel, the highest point of the hike. Identifiable by its large antenna, it offers a 360-degree view. The landscape is shared between the Atlantic coast and the Spanish coast on the one hand and several peaks of the Basque Country, including the emblematic Rhune, on the other.

The first part of the descent takes place on a rocky portion before joining a wider track which runs towards the seaside. This winding portion is really pleasant. A succession of short climbs and descents regularly lead to pretty little coves. It is at one of them, the Artzuko portu, that you leave the coastal path. The route goes up through the vegetation towards an unusual site: the fort of Notre-Dame de Guadeloupe. This ancient fortress concludes this wonderful loop between land and sea.

name: Ibardin to Lac de Xoldokogaina
name_original: Le lac de Xoldokogaina depuis Ibardin
description:
The route that leads to Lake Xoldokogaina from Ibardin is accessible to hikers of all levels. It offers a bucolic immersion in Basque nature, between France and Spain. The departure is at the Col d’Ibardin, known for its numerous ventas and restoration sites.

From the car park, you leave the surrounding hustle and bustle behind by taking a fairly wide track. The route follows the red and white markings of the GR 10, a major hiking trail which crosses the Pyrenees from west to east. You leave it for a moment to climb onto the Manttale. This pretty summit, the highest point of the route, offers breathtaking views of the Atlantic coast and the surrounding reliefs.

Back on the GR 10, you follow it for a moment before turning off towards the second essential part of the hike: Lake Xoldokogaina. It’s a superb shaded path that takes you there. The single leads to the lake dam. This allows you to cross it while providing a beautiful view of the body of water.

The last portion of the route consists of walking along the banks of the lake for a moment before slowly going up to the Col d’Ibardin. This section offers an alternation of shaded parts and open clearings. On the way, you pass not far from a sheepfold made of old stones, which still shelters a few animals.

name: Mount Errebi and the chapel in Ainhoa
name_original: Le mont Errebi et la chapelle d’Ainhoa
description:
This pretty little loop is accessible to hikers of all levels. Growing on the heights of the picturesque village of Ainhoa, it offers superb panoramas. In addition to its pretty landscapes, the route takes two emblematic routes of the region: the GR 10 and the Camino de Santiago.

The start of the route is in the center of the typically Basque village of Ainhoa. Its houses with colorful facades give it an authentic charm. In a few streets, you are already at the exit of the village. A wide track winds up the sides of the hill. This leads to the chapel of the Hawthorn and the three crosses. In addition to its religious dimension, this place offers you a spectacular view that stretches all the way to the Atlantic coast.

Shortly after, you leave the GR 10 markings to climb Mount Errebi. The climb is as short as it is steep. However, arriving at the summit gives you a beautiful panorama of the surrounding mountains, including the Mondarrain and the Artzamendi.

Then, a fairly steep descent finds the GR 10 then, a little further, you join the Chemin de Compostela. Following in the footsteps of the pilgrims, all you have to do is return to the center of Ainhoa.

name: Olhette to La Rhune
name_original: La Rhune depuis Olhette
description:
This beautiful hike provides access to one of the best-known mountains in the Basque Country: the Rhune. Culminating just above 900 meters, this peak reigns over the Basque coast. Bordering, it offers spectacular panoramas over a large part of the Basque Country. Note that it is possible to make the round trip via its little train from the Col de Saint-Ignace. All the information is here: https://www.rhune.com/fr/.

The start of the route is at the parking lot located at the end of Chemin Xurien Borda, in the Olhette district. The route begins by following the tracks of the GR 10. It gradually climbs towards the Col des Trois Fontaines. After a short passage under the woods, the view quickly clears and you can already see your objective for the day.

The passage to the pass and the crossing of its small wood announce the final ascent towards the Rhune. This is the steepest portion. Numerous switchbacks allow you to quickly gain height before reaching the esplanade at the top. From there, a spectacular view extends to the Landes coastline on one side and the Basque mountains on the other.

Savor the panoramas, while enjoying the refreshments provided by the inn at the summit. Then, you begin the long descent phase to return to the starting point. This begins on a relatively wide track which plunges below the foothills of the summit. Then, you take a small path which goes off to the right. This path goes around Larrun Txiki for a little less than two kilometers until reaching Venta Yasola, ideal for taking a short break. After the venta, you continue the descent until you reach the GR 10 path, which brings you back to your starting point and concludes this superb loop.

name: Bidarray to Iparla peak
name_original: Le pic d'Iparla depuis Bidarray
description:
The hike that leads to the Iparla peak from the village of Bidarray guarantees its share of spectacular landscapes. The route, which includes a significant kilometer-altitude difference ratio, is carried out back and forth on the GR 10 route. Its markings facilitate your progress while ensuring comfortable progress.

From the center of Bidarray and its Basque pelota pediment, you start by taking a small road which goes up towards the famous Iparla ridges. The route goes around a farm before taking a small path that climbs under the forest.

Little by little, the ground becomes clearer. The first part of the climb is the one which includes the steepest portions. You gradually follow the ridge line while quickly gaining height. The slope gradually softens and you discover the first classified peaks such as Pagalepoa, Talatze and Larrateko Egia.

You are now flirting with the Franco-Spanish border while the ridges reveal increasingly spectacular viewpoints. The path evolves not far from dizzying cliffs which offer breathtaking panoramas. The Iparla peak brings this spectacle to life, just above 1,000 meters above sea level. Enjoy the views of the Pyrenees before descending the same way.

name: Ispéguy pass to Hautza
name_original: Hautza depuis le col d'Ispéguy
description:
This hike takes you on a border ridge between France and Spain. You progress successively on the heights of the Baigorri and Baztan valleys, located in Spanish Navarre. From the Ispeguy pass to the Hautza in a round trip, this route includes a significant kilometer-altitude difference ratio which has the merit of offering incredible views.

The Ispeguy border pass is an ideal starting point, where several paths converge. In addition to parking, there is an inn-restaurant perfect for refueling at the start or end of the hike. The route begins by following the GR 5 path, which runs along the hillside.

A first escapade offers the Elhorriko Kaskoa, a real belvedere over the Baigorri valley. The view of the cliffs of Iparla is shared with the first Pyrenean peaks of Béarn. You then head towards Hautza, via an uphill section which runs along a relatively wide ridge. You juggle the French-Spanish border before reaching the highest point of the route which is located exclusively in Spain. L’Hautza offers panoramas of the Spanish slope and the Baztan valley. On the other side, on a clear day, you can see the peak of Anie.

The last section of the route joins the Ispeguy Pass mainly via descent. This time, you are traveling on the Spanish slope overlooking the Baztan valley. Back at the inn, its terrace and kitchen invite you to enjoy beautiful plates of Iberian ham, valley trout, or other Spanish specialties.

name: Itxassou to Mount Mondarrain
name_original: Le Mondarrain depuis Itxassou
description:
This magnificent route departing from Itxassou offers a beautiful diversity of landscapes and atmospheres. Affordable for the day by everyone, it climbs to the summit of Mondarrain from the center of the village before returning via the famous Pas de Roland.

You leave the picturesque village of Itxassou heading towards the adjoining forest. After crossing a small stream, you begin to gradually gain height. Below the Atharri, the slope rises drastically before reaching the summit. Then, a path leads to the open foothills of Ezkondiaraï.

It is at the end of this pleasant passage that the final ascent towards the Mondarrain really begins. The path goes straight up the slope before entering the forest. Finally, at the cost of a final dry climb, you climb to this emblematic summit of the Basque Country.

Then, you go back down through the forest, before following another path which crosses a small place where there is a Basque pelota wall. You then plunge onto a small stony path which leads to Laxia. A few meters further, on the edge of the Nice, is the famous Pas de Roland. From there, all you have to do is follow the beautiful road which winds through the gorges towards Itxassou.

name: Artzamendi and its surroundings
name_original: L'Artzamendi et ses environs
description:
This spectacular route spans the heights of the Basque Country on the border between France and Spain. It offers an anthology of panoramas between iconic peaks and breathtaking trails. Although spectacular and sometimes dizzying, the trails do not involve any major difficulty. To get there, you have to reach the Mehatze pass by car from the village of Itxassou.

From the pass, you first make a large loop before finishing with the ascent of Artzamendi. If you prefer, you can reverse starting at the top and then loop. Otherwise, initially, the route follows the route of the GR 10, which leads to the Espalza pass. From here, a steep descent begins before branching off onto a balcony path.

You continue with an absolutely remarkable section on a balcony path which offers dizzying views. This portion alternates short climbs and descents, while providing numerous panoramas of the cliffs. The route then embarks on a gradual climb towards Iguzki. In addition to giving its name to a local beer, this summit offers a remarkable view of its neighbor, Artzamendi.

Identifiable by its white radar station in the shape of a large balloon, Artzamendi is a summit culminating at 924 meters. From the Mehatze pass, it is reached quickly with a steep climb then more gentle at the end. Once reached, it offers a panorama that extends to the Atlantic Ocean. Then all you have to do is go back down the same way as you went up.

name: Hargibel
name_original: L'Hargibel
description:
This beautiful loop offers an immersion in the heart of the Aldudes valley, while traversing its heights. The departure is in the center of the village of Aldudes which shares the eponymous valley with its neighbors Banca and Urepel. This charming locality takes its name from the contraction of the Basque word “aldubide” which means “path to the summits”. This etymology perfectly illustrates the course of the route.

The first part of the route aims to climb the border ridge which marks the separation between France and Spain. The route climbs through a beautiful beech forest before the terrain opens up to offer the first views of the Aldudes valley. From then on, the slope softens and the path crisscrosses on both sides of the border.

Take the time to observe the superb panoramas on both sides. The beautiful rounded hills alternate with some rocky portions. Below the summit, just before reaching it, don't miss Harrikulunka, the tilting rock. This impressive, swinging stone appears to have been placed here by some mystical force.

The Hargibel then offers itself to you, dominating a beautiful and wide ridge line. Here, nature mixes with history and religion. This section is punctuated with megalithic monuments dating from the first millennium BC. These sites testify to the existence of ancient funeral rites.
After this leap in time, you return to the starting point by following the Haute Route Pyrénéenne (HRP) path. The path is dotted with beautiful beech trees which accompany the descent, while allowing a glimpse of the landscape.

name: Baïgura and its neighbours
name_original: Le Baïgura et ses voisins
description:
This pretty course, located in the foothills of the Basque Country, offers beautiful panoramas of the Pyrenees chain as well as the Atlantic coast. The starting point is in the center of the village of Ossès, not far from Saint-Jean-Pied-de-Port.

The route is divided into two parts, a first ascending phase towards the summit of Baigura. Then a downhill section, which passes through the Laina before forming a loop to return to Ossès. You start by embarking on a relatively wide track which climbs gradually.

The start of the climb includes the steepest percentages. Then, the slope calms down and you take pretty balcony paths on the mountainside. You first pass the summit of Laina to reach Baïgura. On a clear day, it offers a great 360 degree panorama.

You then turn back towards Laina which also offers its share of viewpoints. Finally, you plunge into a long descent which alternates passages in the undergrowth with more open portions until you reach the center of Ossès.

name: Escaliers peak
name_original: Le pic des Escaliers
description:
This course offers an experience suspended above the enchanted forest of Iraty. Short and accessible to all, the route includes peaks which offer spectacular views of the Pyrenees mountains.

The largest beech forest in Europe, the Iraty forest is one of the jewels of the Basque Country. Its 20,000 hectares are spread between France and Spain and bring together many remarkable plant and animal species: beeches, firs, deer, roe deer, etc. The route begins in the heart of the thousand-year-old massif, at the Iraty chalets, on the Bagargiak pass. This starting point is ideal since it includes parking, a restaurant, a shop and even offers the possibility of sleeping there.

When you leave the forest, an uphill section awaits you. This leads to the summit of Arthanolatze, the highest point of the outing. A few hundred meters further, you reach the Pic des Escaliers. These two peaks offer their share of panoramas, dizzying and infinite, while overlooking the majestic Iraty forest.

The last part of the route takes a short descent towards the forest. From then on, you only have a short climb left before returning to your starting point, at the chalets.

name: Holzarte footbridge
name_original: La passerelle d'Holzarte
description:
Here is a hike that offers a superb route to one of the most popular sites in the Basque Country and the Pyrenees: the Holzarte footbridge. The loop offers a beautiful immersion in the Olhadubi gorges before gaining height to offer clear views.

The departure is at the parking lot of the Logibar hostel, not far from the small village of Larrau. The red and white markings of the GR 10 accompanies you for a large part of the journey. Many signs indicate the footbridge. This is accessible by a fairly steep path and presents itself to you at the last moment. Its suspension bridge rises almost 180 meters above the gorges.

Once the footbridge is crossed, the path continues to climb, still following the GR 10. The path winds through the forest before emerging into a green setting. From now on, you overlook the gorges for several kilometers while crossing waterfalls and streams.

A magnificent path runs along the side of a hill while providing magnificent panoramas of the mountains. You finally reach the highest point of the route. A steep but short descent plunges into the woods to finally arrive at the starting point, at the Logibar inn.

name: Atlantic Coast Route: Ria Formosa Loop
name_original: De Faro até Fuzeta — percurso circular
description: Cycle the moderate 32.7-mile Atlantic Coast Route: Ria Formosa Loop through Portugal's Parque Natural, exploring coastal towns and wildlife.

name: Mountain biking through the forges of Artikutza
name_original: Una vuelta en BTT por las ferrerías de Artikutza
description:
In the Sierra de Artikutza, several forges active until 1919 left old buildings and remains of the train that transported minerals, silent witnesses of its industrial past. This Route takes you into the heart of the Guipuzcoan forest to discover these vestiges and the wildest nature of the area.

You can arrive at the start of the tour in your own vehicle and park it in the designated area next to the Artikutza quarry. Once you start pedaling, you will immediately feel the magic of the Navarrese forest. The itinerary, made up of trails and tracks with challenging sections, will test your skills on the bike.

In the village of Artikutza there are vending machines for food and water. However, we recommend that you bring enough liquid and snacks to enjoy a picnic while you contemplate the wonderful views of this special corner. The weather is usually cool and humid, so it is advisable to wear waterproof clothing and be prepared for possible rain.

name: Mountain biking through Las Tuerces and Covalagua
name_original: Ruta en BTT por Las Tuerces y Covalagua
description:
In the vicinity of the Pisuerga River, in the province of Palencia, there are two natural treasures that are the protagonists of this Route: the natural space of Las Tuerces and the protected landscape of Covalagua. Pools, unique rock formations, caves and natural viewpoints make this an ideal destination if you are a nature lover.

We propose a circular route that begins in the parking lots on the outskirts of Villaescusa de las Torres, a town that is easily reached by your own vehicle and that still retains the charm of a typical Castilian town, with quiet streets and a welcoming atmosphere. . Spring here is really special, creating a spectacular setting with endless colorful flowers.

The Route takes you to discover Las Tuerces and then Covalagua, connecting paths and roads that ascend up to 1210 meters. We advise you to check the weather forecast before leaving to wear the appropriate clothing and enjoy the tour, regardless of the weather conditions. This region is characterized by hot summers and cold winters. Since the itinerary does not cross any town, don't forget to bring enough water and a snack with you.

name: Mountain biking through the Sierra de Gredos
name_original: Circular BTT por la Sierra de Gredos
description:
The Sierra de Gredos, located in the Central System of Spain, is a natural paradise full of majestic peaks, impressive gorges and beautiful lagoons. Its mountainous landscapes and rich biodiversity offer a unique experience for your next adventure. Here, nature is shown in all its splendor and goats, foxes or deer are likely to cross your path.

Arenas de San Pedro is the starting point for the circular route. You can get there by car on the AV-923 highway that borders the mountain range on its southern slope. Here, mountains of more than 2000 meters are awaiting your arrival. Especially in spring and autumn, the contrast of colors in the vegetation is really beautiful. Of course, we recommend that you check the weather forecast to decide your equipment appropriately.

The itinerary is an interesting combo of tracks and paths, with some short stretches of road and demanding slopes that take you into the most absolute beauty of the mountains. In addition, you have the opportunity to visit different towns nestled in the mountains with rich cuisine and where you can still see the typical architecture of the area.

name: Mountain biking in the Alto Tajo Natural Park
name_original: BTT por el parque natural del Alto Tajo
description:
The Alto Tajo, with its impressive gorges and gorges sculpted by the river of the same name, is a hidden paradise where crystal-clear water runs between limestone rock walls, creating a fairy-tale landscape.

The adventure begins in some car parks near the source of the Tagus River, which you can reach via the A-1704 road. In this remote area, the best option is to arrive in your own vehicle. In addition, it is preferable to carry some supplies with you, since along the route you only pass through one town where you can find supplies.

The route navigates through hills more than 1,500 meters high and immerses you in the purest nature. Pine forests, oak forests and, above all, aromatic plants surround you, adding a pleasant flavor to the experience. The profile of the Route is in the shape of a mountain range, a constant see-saw exploring the paths and paths and, on some occasions, short stretches of road. Spring and autumn are the seasons with the most pleasant temperatures, although you may be surprised by some days of rain.

name: Mountain biking in Sierra Norte de Sevilla
name_original: Sierra Norte de Sevilla en BTT
description:
The Sierra Norte of Seville, with its green pastures and lush forests of holm oaks and cork oaks, is a haven of peace where nature and history intertwine, offering an idyllic landscape dotted with picturesque white villages and paths full of charm.

The Route begins next to the Huéznar riverbank, just before reaching El Pedroso. Tracks and paths will guide you through the pasture, where you may have to open and close the gate of the fences that protect and shelter the local livestock as you pass. It is an ideal route to do when the temperatures are still pleasant, avoiding the scorching sun, especially in summer.

Cazalla de la Sierra is approximately halfway along the route. It is the ideal stop to recharge your batteries and fall in love with its historic architecture and Andalusian charm.

name: Mountain biking loop in the Serra d'Espadá
name_original: Circular en BTT por la Serra d'Espadá
description:
The Serra d'Espadà, with its lush cork oak forests and green valleys, is a corner of the Valencian Community where nature is shown in all its splendor, offering charming trails and panoramic views that invite tranquility and contemplation.

Eslida is the starting place of this adventure. In this beautiful town, full of narrow streets, public parking is available. Before starting the Route, make sure you bring supplies and, above all, water. The route challenges you at times with long and steep slopes. Also, if you decide to visit this area in summer, temperatures can be high. Therefore, we recommend avoiding the middle of the day or choosing any other time of year with more pleasant temperatures.

This circular route is made up of forest tracks, paths and paths that caress the mountainous terrain of the mountain range, offering you the best panoramic views from different angles of the route. In addition, you will find remains of past buildings full of history such as the Dalt mill.

name: A Tour of the Badlands by mountain bike
name_original: Una vuelta por Badlands en BTT
description:
The Cárcavas de Marchal, with their impressive earth formations sculpted by erosion, seem like a landscape from another world, where the ravines and earthy colors create a unique and overwhelming natural spectacle.

Purullena is the starting point for this circular route. You can easily get there by car from the A92 motorway and use the authorized parking spaces available in the municipality. During the Route you pass through several towns where it is possible to find supplies, although it does not hurt to bring a snack to eat during the itinerary. Along the route, you will come across natural viewpoints of the gullies where it will be difficult to avoid stopping to enjoy the spectacle.

The Route runs, for the most part, along roads and paths. In some sections there are reguerones that add a certain degree of fun to the adventure, always with caution. Summer is very hot, although if it is your favorite season you can always pedal first thing in the morning to avoid the stifling heat. Additionally, rain can add a degree of difficulty to the route, since you are on clay terrain.

name: Mountain biking loop in Sierra de Grazalema
name_original: Circular en BTT por Sierra de Grazalema
description:
The north of the Sierra de Grazalema is a paradise of lush forests and picturesque white villages. Here, the pure air and the crystal-clear waters of the rivers create a serene and charming environment, perfect for disconnecting and enjoying nature in its purest state.

The circular itinerary that we propose begins in Zahara, a charming town next to the Zahara reservoir, with a medieval castle that watches over it. You can arrive in your own vehicle and use one of the public parking spaces available. If you need supplies for the Route, do not hesitate to visit the local shops while you walk through the alleys of this charming place.

The Route runs along tracks, paths and some sections of road. It is a very steep area, which will surprise you at every bend with a different postcard. 

You ascend up to 930 meters above sea level and some peaks, over 1500 meters, will witness your adventure. Be sure to check the weather before leaving home to wear appropriate clothing. Summers are hot and we recommend avoiding the hottest hours. A grove or on the banks of a stream or lake will be a good option to rest.

name: Tour of Bardenas Reales by mountain bike
name_original: Vuelta por Bardenas Reales en BTT
description:
The Bardenas Reales, located in Navarra, are a semi-desert area that stands out for its rock formations and spectacular ravines. This area is a nature reserve and protected natural park. Its arid terrain and unique geology make this place a spectacular destination for mountain biking, as well as hiking, photography and bird watching.

The starting point of this exciting circular route is the park's information center, which can be easily reached by car from Arguedas along the Calle Bardenas road. Before entering the desert, make sure you bring enough supplies, especially water, as there are no places to resupply along the route and temperatures can be high. In the hottest months, it is advisable to avoid the hottest hours. We also advise you to bring sunglasses, sunscreen and technical summer clothing.

The itinerary follows dirt tracks and paths. Some sections can be somewhat technical due to the presence of loose sand and erosion caused by rain. This adds an interesting and fun element to the tour.

name: Salto del río Nervión – Monte Santiago Natural Monument
name_original: Salto del río Nervión — Monumento Natural del Monte Santiago
description:
At 270 meters high, the Nervión waterfall is the largest waterfall on the Iberian Peninsula. The waters of the river slide through the Délika gorge to create a spectacular waterfall. In addition, the path is flanked by beech and oak trees of great beauty.

To plan the Route, you must take two things into account. The first: in the summer months, the waterfall is dry or only carries a thin trickle of water. The second: in winter and during heavy rains, the trail is inaccessible.

The tour begins in the Délika neighborhood, where you can leave your car in the parking lot next to the restaurant.

name: Borosa River Route – Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y las Villas Natural Park
name_original: Ruta del río Borosa — Parque natural de las Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y las Villas
description:
The province of Jaén may be famous for its sea of olive trees and its castles, but it also hides a spectacular environment to enjoy hiking.

This Route takes you along the Borosa River to the place from which it emanates, in the incredible Segura mountain range. We are not lying to you when we say that it is one of the most interesting routes in Spain: a combination of forest, panoramic views, animals such as deer and foxes, and a river that forms spectacular waterfalls.

The best times to visit this place are autumn and spring, which is when the river carries more water and the waterfalls are in all their splendor. To get to the starting point, you will need your own vehicle, which you can leave without problem in the paid parking.

name: Ruta de los Pilones – Natural reserve of the Garganta de los Infiernos (Hell's Gorge)
name_original: Ruta de los Pilones — Reserva natural de la Garganta de los Infiernos
description:
Cáceres is a hiker's paradise and, if you don't believe it, you just have to go to the Hells Gorge to see it. 

The Route starts from an interpretation center with very valuable information about the environment and ascends among oaks until you reach the Pilones, where you can take a refreshing bath and recharge with water at the fountain. Afterwards, you will have the necessary strength to discover the incredible Hells Gorge and return to the same place where this circular route begins.

Any time is good to do this tour. To get to the starting point, it is best that you have your own vehicle. If you don't have one, you can also start the Route in the town of Jerte, which has a bus station.

name: Black Stones Route – O Grove Peninsula
name_original: Ruta das Pedras Negras — Península de O Grove
description:
The diversity of Galicia makes it famous for many facets. O Grove, in particular, attracts many people for its fine sand beaches, but here is also hidden one of the most beautiful trails in the Galician community.

The Pedras Negras Trail is perfect if what you are looking for is to walk by the sea and learn some information about the history of the place. The first part of the itinerary runs along wooden walkways and, later, moves on to a dirt path that takes you to the same point where you started. Along the way, you will see stones of all shapes and sizes. 
In the end, you arrive at the O Grove military battery, which hides a great anecdote: in the 1950s, in the midst of the Cold War, the Americans described it as one of the most efficient defense systems that existed.

We recommend the hottest months to venture to O Grove. This way, you will avoid the rain and you can take a swim in the Atlantic. If you need to use public transport, there are buses from Vigo and Pontevedra.

name: Ascent to Toloño from Labastida – Toloño Mountains
name_original: Ascensión al Toloño desde Labastida — Sierra de Toloño
description:
El Toloño is one of the most emblematic peaks in this area of Spain and partly separates La Rioja and Euskadi. As a consequence, it also acts as a natural border between the Mediterranean and Atlantic climates.

There are several ways to ascend to the top of Toloño. We have chosen the itinerary that starts from Labastida and goes up its south face to the top and then turns around and goes down its east face. In general, you must be in good physical shape, since the ascent is quite hard (there are 600 meters of elevation gain in just 4 kilometers). Luckily, the descent is much gentler, but it is also longer.

The Route can be done at any time of the year, although in autumn the beech trees that grow in some parts of the mountains are dressed in incredible colors and make the landscape even more beautiful. To get to Labastida, there are several buses from Logroño and Vitoria-Gasteiz, but it is best to use your own vehicle.

name: Cañón del río Bailón – Sierras Subbéticas Natural Park
name_original: Cañón del río Bailón — Parque natural de las Sierras Subbéticas
description:
This Route that leaves Zuheros is quite a discovery. The town is a network of streets with slopes with a very special charm, so you can already get an idea of what awaits you.

The route follows the Bailón River as it passes through an environment full of impressive rock formations. Here the water has carved a spectacular canyon over time. Furthermore, on both sides of the path there are numerous caves, among which the Fraile Cave stands out, a stalagmite with a more than curious shape. 

We recommend that you do this Route after a few days of rain. Otherwise, you will most likely find the river bed completely dry. In this case, the landscape will still be just as impressive, but accompanying your adventure with the sound of water is much more interesting. To get to Zuheros, it is best that you have your own vehicle. There are also buses from Córdoba. In addition, the town has all kinds of services.

name: Gollizno Loop – Subbetic Mountains
name_original: Circular del Gollizno — Cordillera Subbética
description:
Granada is much more than the Sierra Nevada and the Gollizno Route, which begins and ends in the beautiful town of Moclín, is good proof of this. This itinerary takes you to explore the closure of the Velillos River, where you have to cross a suspension bridge not suitable for those with a lot of vertigo. The surroundings are incredible and also hide a wall with cave paintings. In addition, Moclín and its castle are well worth a visit.

This circular route is short, but quite intense, because most of the way is uphill. Of course, you can choose to do the route in the opposite direction; You will have fewer kilometers of climbing, but the last part will be harder. Bring trekking poles, as they will be of great help.

The best times to enjoy this adventure are, without a doubt, spring, autumn and winter. Due to the heat, it is best to avoid summer. Moclín is very close to Granada and has all the services you may need. To get there, the most comfortable thing is to use your own vehicle. If you don't have one, there are buses from Granada.

name: La Alberca to Chorro de las Batuecas – Sierra de Francia
name_original: De La Alberca al Chorro de las Batuecas — Sierra de Francia
description:
Located in Salamanca, La Alberca is a place that belongs to the past. With its traditional architecture, it is one of the most beautiful towns in Spain. But its historic center is not the only interesting thing you will find here. And the natural park that surrounds this town hides incredible treasures.

This Route takes you to the Chorro de las Batuecas waterfall and is a mix of history and nature. Not only can you see the beauty of the 12-meter waterfall, but you also pass through several screes decorated with cave paintings.  In addition, along the way there are many pools where you can cool off in the hottest times. 

The Route can be done at any time of the year, but in spring and autumn the environment is especially beautiful and that is when the waterfall has the most water. You can get to La Alberca in your own vehicle and there are also buses from Salamanca and Ciudad Rodrigo (although the service is quite limited). The town has all kinds of services.

name: Gravel route of the walled cities
name_original: Ruta gravel de las ciudades amuralladas
description:
In the depths of Castilla y León, in the province of Salamanca, there are two great walled treasures with war heritage that are currently open-air museums of Castilian history. These are Ciudad Rodrigo, a medieval jewel, and the Real Fuerte de la Concepción, a 17th century stronghold.

The route between one and the other runs through the steppes and pastures of Salamanca and takes you to small hidden towns where time seems to have stopped. Likewise, you come across the Águeda River several times, which is home to the archaeological site of Siega Verde, where you can see rock carvings on the stones.

The Route is circular from Ciudad Rodrigo, where you can arrive by train or private vehicle. The route does not pose any technical difficulties, so you can use a gravel bike with high-rolling tires. Even if you pass through various towns, it is recommended that you carry enough water and food for the entire day. You can also choose to divide the tour in two and stay in one of the passing towns. The dry days of spring and autumn are ideal for discovering the walled cities.

name: Gravel ride to Sanabria lake from Puebla de Sanabria
name_original: Circular gravel al lago de Sanabria desde Puebla de Sanabria
description:
Between the Sierra de la Culebra and Galicia, in the most remote part of the province of Zamora, is the largest glacial lake in Spain, Lake Sanabria. The landscape, bathed by the Tera and Castro rivers, boasts lush pine and oak forests, moors and a fascinating rural environment of fields that stands out when passing through its small towns.

The route we propose is circular from Puebla de Sanabria, a spectacular medieval enclave that has a well-preserved historical complex, among which the castle of the counts of Benavente and its popular architecture stand out. The city is well connected by train and road.

This route runs mainly along forest tracks and wide roads, although some stretches of asphalt also await you. This way, you can use almost any bike, although a specific one is ideal. Remember to bring some provisions with you so you can enjoy a stop in the middle of nature. From mid-spring to mid-autumn, each season offers its charm to ride through the Zamorano landscapes.

name: Sierra Espuña gravel tour
name_original: Vuelta gravel a sierra Espuña
description:
Sierra Espuña hides in the most unknown part of Murcia, famous for its beaches, almost in the province of Almería. The Route takes you around the limestone massifs and runs through impressive gypsum ravines where erosion creates whimsical shapes, ascends in the shade of the trees and takes you to the heights revealing wonderful panoramic views.

The route is circular from the Sierra Espuña campsite. The best way to get here is by private vehicle and you can leave it parked without any problem. The itinerary avoids narrow trails, but climbs some mountain roads. To complete it safely, it is advisable that your gravel bike has resistant tires.

The Route immerses you in the depths of these mountains and does not include passing through any town, so we recommend that you bring with you enough water and food and some light spare parts that will allow you to complete it without setbacks. The seasons with the friendliest temperatures and best light to contemplate the landscape are spring and autumn.

name: Poveda Waterfall and Taravilla Lagoon – Upper Tagus Natural Park
name_original: Salto de Poveda y laguna de Taravilla — Parque natural del Alto Tajo
description:
This is one of the most emblematic routes of the Alto Tajo natural park. It is a circular and simple itinerary that takes you to discover the Taravilla lagoon and the Poveda waterfall, which originated after the collapse of the wall of an old dam.

You can start the Route from several points. We have chosen the parking lot located in the Taravilla lagoon, where you can park your vehicle. However, keep in mind that it has limited capacity and, in July and August, it is necessary to reserve and pay the corresponding fee through the Cañón del Tajo – Parque Natural del Alto Tajo platform.

Likewise, the park receives many visitors in summer, so we recommend that you choose any other time of year to discover this environment.

name: Climb to Castillo de Castro – Sierra del Espadán
name_original: Subida al castillo de Castro — Sierra del Espadán
description:
This Route in the Sierra del Espadán is truly wonderful. Starting from the Arab town of Alfondeguilla, you have a route ahead that takes you to the Castro castle, passing through the beautiful cork oak forests of the Horcajo ravine, the arch known as L'Arquet de la Rambla and the Castro refrigerator, a well of more than 12 meters that was once used to store snow. In total, there are five refrigerators in the natural park and this is the one that is best preserved.

The itinerary is long and the last climb to the castle will test the resistance of your legs, so don't forget to bring plenty of water and a snack to recharge your batteries. Of course, when you get to the top you will see that the views are well worth the effort.

To get to Alfondeguilla, it is best to have your own vehicle, since bus connections are scarce. Remember that summer in this part of Spain can be very hot, so we recommend the rest of the seasons to face this adventure.

name: Circular gravel from Quesa to Caroche Peak
name_original: Circular gravel desde Quesa al pico Caroche
description:
The interior of the province of Valencia is full of mountains. The Sierra de los Caroches is located almost in Castilla-La Mancha, in the heart of the Canal de Navarrés region, its steep limestone massifs are the ideal place for the water to play with the soft terrain and carve spectacular gorges of turquoise pools.

This Route is circular from Quesa, an important rural town surrounded by ideal swimming areas in summer that you can reach by private vehicle. The path ascends to the Caroche peak, while immersing you in a charming Mediterranean mountain landscape dotted with plots of olive trees, almond trees and some fruit trees, crowned by imposing calcareous cliffs.

The route is physically demanding and during the day you do not pass through any town, so we recommend that you bring enough water and food and some spare parts. The technical section is not difficult, so a gravel bike is more than enough to ride the slopes and enjoy the views. Given the high temperature contrast between winter and summer, we advise you to do this adventure in spring or autumn, both seasons offer the best of the territory and, on warm days, some swimming opportunities.

name: Gravel route through the Sierra de Cardeña Natural Park
name_original: Ruta gravel por el parque natural Sierra de Cardeña
description:
The dehesa is the most extensive landscape in Spain and the holm oak is the tree with the most specimens in the country. In this ecosystem, which covers a large part of Andalusia and Extremadura, Iberian pigs roam in search of their favorite food, the acorn. In addition to a wide variety of traditional agricultural farms, Iberian lynxes, wild boars, roe deer, deer, foxes and rabbits run through the pasture.

With this Route through the Sierra de Cardeña natural park, we want to offer you a brief immersion in its postcard landscape and its country culture. The itinerary is circular from the town of Cardeña, where you can reach by road, and passes through two other ideal towns to stop and enjoy local specialties, mainly Iberian pork products, in addition to contemplating its traditional architecture of whitewashed houses. .

The itinerary runs along dirt tracks between holm oaks, cork oaks and oak groves under whose shade aromatic bushes proliferate. Apart from the mild temperatures of winter, you can cycle through Córdoba in spring and autumn, each season offers you its benefits. Remember to bring enough water and food, as well as some spare parts and some sunscreen to enjoy riding under the warm Andalusian sun.

name: Gravel route through vineyards along the Ebro River
name_original: Ruta gravel entre viñedos por el Ebro
description:
Around the great river that gives its name to the Iberian Peninsula, the Ebro, countless crops sprout. On this Route we invite you to take your gravel bike through the vineyards of La Rioja and Álava that spread on both sides of the riverbed. The path takes you through various towns rich in history, architecture and views of the river and mountains, as well as a winery.

The adventure is circular from Haro, the Rioja wine capital, which you can reach by train or private vehicle. The route is designed to travel on gravel tracks and secluded roads with rough asphalt, but without technical difficulty. The tour is a taste of the many routes that can be taken in the area.

The mild temperatures of spring and autumn are ideal for riding among vineyards, in addition to the chromatic contrast that the vineyards offer in each season. If you don't mind the heat, the cool days of summer also have their charm. Remember to bring sunscreen and take with you: food, drink and spare parts for any eventuality. In any case, you are always close to towns with ideal services to make stops and let yourself be captivated by the gastronomy.

name: Gravel road between Campo de Criptana and El Toboso
name_original: Ruta gravel entre Campo de Criptana y El Toboso
description:
What could be more quixotic than riding your bicycle along the gravel tracks that cross the La Mancha plain between Campo de Criptana and El Toboso? The Route that we propose shares a section with the complete route of the Don Quixote Route.

This tour takes you into the landscape of Cervantes' work, and takes you through an extensive steppe of reddish earth covered by a collage of olive groves, vineyards and other crops such as almonds or cereals. In addition, you pass through locations where you can contemplate the traditional white four-blade windmills that in the mind of the ingenious gentleman become giants. And, if that were not enough, you pass through typical towns in La Mancha, which have a traditional configuration, apart from the possibility of tasting local products.

The adventure is circular from Campo de Criptana, where you can reach by train or by road. The distance of the journey is affordable, even more so if you take into account that you only ascend 300 meters. For this reason, the route does not represent any technical or physical difficulty, so a good gravel bike is enough to ride with guarantees. The roads of La Mancha are perfect for pedaling on the dry days of spring and autumn. Remember to bring supplies and spare parts for any eventuality and your photographic device on hand.

name: Gravel route through the upper Ebro
name_original: Ruta gravel por el alto Ebro
region: National Park Hoces del Alto Ebro y Rudrón
description:
Spain's great river gushes between the mountains of Cantabria and makes its way through the limestone, carving gorges and sinuous gorges. The landscape of the upper Ebro is lush and spectacular and that is why we want to propose this gravel bike route along the tracks of the natural park of the sickles of the upper Ebro and the Rudrón.

The route not only immerses you in beautiful beech and oak forests, but also constantly overlooks the riverbed with unforgettable views. In addition, you pass through towns that seem straight out of a story and are home to ancient bridges or a spring of water with a waterfall in the center, such as Orbaneja del Castillo.

The Cantabrian mountains can be explored from mid-spring to mid-autumn, so you can enjoy the best of all three seasons. The route is suitable for gravel bikes, apart from the desire to pedal and space on your device for photos, remember to bring supplies and some spare parts.

name: Gravel route between Burgo de Osma and the fortress of Gormaz
name_original: Ruta gravel entre el Burgo de Osma y la fortaleza de Gormaz
description:
More than a thousand years ago, battles were fought on the border between Muslims and Christians in the lands of the upper Duero. From those exploits, walled treasures remained, such as the Burgo de Osma or the caliphal fortress of Gormaz, the largest in Europe at its time.

With this simple tour along dirt tracks and secondary roads, we want to propose that you ride your gravel bike in the footsteps of the Castilian knight, Rodrigo Díaz de Vivar, El Cid, and contemplate from within the fort that he himself conquered. You will also cross the landscape of fields, with the largest apple orchard in Spain, and the Soria moor, cross the Duero over a medieval bridge and visit the beautiful Mozarabic hermitage—the Christians who lived under Muslim rule—of San Miguel.

The Route is circular from Burgo de Osma, a perfect town to give you a Castilian gastronomic tribute, which you can reach by road. The most recommended seasons to enjoy this adventure are spring and autumn, as long as you avoid rainy days and later, as some tracks get muddy. You will not find shadows on the itinerary, so it is advisable to bring sunscreen. It is also necessary that you carry provisions of water, food and spare parts because you do not pass through any town that has services.

name: Gravel route through the mines and mountains of Asturias
name_original: Ruta gravel por las minas y montañas asturianas
description:
The mountains of Asturias preserve an almost virgin landscape full of unforgettable postcards that overflow with greenery. For centuries, coal has been extracted from its bowels, the industry of which has spread an enormous mining heritage and culture. In this Route we propose an adaptation to the gravel of the cycling ring of the central Asturian mountain in which we have included river swims and an approach to the Nalón mining basin.

The route starts from Mieres, where you can easily reach by road or train. It is a demanding route thanks to the abrupt orography of Asturias that offers heart-stopping climbs and views that take your breath away. The adventure takes place on beautiful dirt tracks and some stretches of lonely roads, so you can complete it with a gravel bike and enjoy the challenge and the landscape.

Both the distance and the gradient are impressive, but you can always divide the route into more stages with the komoot planner. The ideal time to cycle through the Principality is from mid-spring to mid-autumn, remember to choose a sunny day, although it is wise to bring a raincoat, the rain waters the greenery almost daily. Finally, remember to bring some spare parts and bring enough food and hydration, although you will surely find where you can stock up on local products in one of the small villages.

name: Tour of Hourtin-Carcans Lake in Gironde
name_original: Tour du lac Carcans-Hourtin en Gironde
description:
This route offers you a unique experience: going around the largest freshwater lake in France. Lake Hourtin and Carcans are located in the Médoc regional natural park, a true cycling kingdom. 

Departing from Maubuisson, this loop of approximately 57 kilometers offers a route that winds between maritime pines and dunes. The route is equally divided between the energizing waves of the ocean and the peaceful waters of the lake for total immersion in nature. 

The perfectly equipped and secure cycle paths take you to discover Carcans-Maubuisson, its equipped beach, the lively port of Hourtin, as well as the nature reserves of the dunes and marshes of Hourtin. Without forgetting the Contaut lagoon. 

Take the time to recharge your batteries on one of the shaded beaches bordering the lake, for a well-deserved break with a beautiful view. Whatever the season, this walk offers a refreshing getaway in the heart of unspoiled nature.

name: Boucle au bassin d'Arcachon et à la Dune du Pilat
description:
This beautiful loop departing from Arcachon station leads to the discovery of some of the wonders of the Arcachon Basin. The largest oyster farming center in Europe and a major tourist destination, this exceptional natural site is home to a wide diversity of marine ecosystems and more than 260 species of birds.

The route begins with the seafront of Arcachon and its beautiful Eyrac beach, close to the port and the city center. Then you head towards the charming Moulleau district and its beach. This offers a view of your next objective: the emblematic Grand Site de la Dune du Pilat. Majestic and imposing, the largest dune in Europe (106 meters above sea level) is a protected natural area, offering a breathtaking panorama of the basin, the forest and the Atlantic Ocean. Park your mount in the main parking lot which borders it and climb to the top via the stairs.

You won't regret taking on the steep climb, as the panoramic view at the top is breathtaking. Your gaze travels between the Banc d’Arguin, Cap Ferret, the Arcachon basin, the Atlantic Ocean and the Landes de Gascogne forest.

The rest of the route crosses the majestic Landes de Gascogne forest and its many emblematic pines. This green break ends as you approach La Teste-de-Buch, the final town before your return to the banks of the Arcachon Bay. This seaside resort has all the amenities to welcome you in the best conditions. Note that along the entire route there are lively terraces, colorful markets and gourmet breaks available to you.

name: Between pines, lakes and ocean — Les Landes
name_original: Entre pins, lacs et océan — Les Landes
description:
This route offers you a beautiful immersion in the Landes forest while discovering magnificent lakes and villages. The departure is located in the parking lot of the Isle Verte sports center in the village of Soustons. The route begins on a small footbridge which spans part of Lake Soustons.

You then cross part of the town before quickly finding yourself immersed in the imposing Landes de Gascogne forest. The largest forest in Europe, it offers you a peaceful and pleasant setting. Like much of the route, this portion has very little elevation change. This leads to the village of Vieux-Boucau. You then enter the seaside resort along its superb marine lake.

Its developed banks, beaches and water activities contrast with the preserved island in its middle. Reserved for birds, this site and the effects of the tides guarantee a magnificent natural spectacle. Vieux-Boucau is also an ideal place for a refreshing swim or a gourmet break.

A new flat and pleasant section follows which leads to Messanges, a small village nestled under the pines not far from the ocean. You continue on a slightly hilly section towards Azur. This is where you find Lake Soustons. Take the time to visit its emblematic pontoon before returning to Soustons along the eastern shore of the lake. Along the way, take a detour to the bucolic Pointe des Vergnes and its century-old trees inhabited by numerous squirrels.

name: Saint-Jean-de-Luz to Sare cycle route
name_original: La voie verte de Saint-Jean-de-Luz à Sare
description:
This route allows you to discover the new Rhune cycle route, from the Atlantic coast to the Pyrenean foothills. The loop starts from Saint-Jean-de-Luz and its emblematic bay and then reaches Sare, labeled “Most Beautiful Village in France”.

You quickly discover the fishing port of Saint-Jean-de-Luz. It is the largest tuna port in France. Hake and anchovy are also other fishing specialties from this port. From the Chantaco district, a first section of four kilometers of greenway takes you to Ascain. This village with its typical architecture is lively with numerous games of Basque pelota on the fronton.
 
At the exit of Ascain, you then attack the climb to the Col de Saint-Ignace via a second portion of two-kilometer greenway. At the top, an inn allows you to enjoy a nice break before continuing. If your schedule allows it, you can also take the little train there to discover the summit of Rhune.

Otherwise, a pleasant descent leads to the picturesque village of Sare, nestled at the foot of the Rhune. We advise you to take a gourmet break to regain your strength before returning: the Basque cake is the traditional and emblematic pastry of the region.

name: La Vélodyssée cycle route from Bidart to Saint-Jean-de-Luz
name_original: La Vélodyssée de Bidart à Saint-Jean-de-Luz
description:
This route between Bidart and Saint-Jean-de-Luz offers an immersive experience in the heart of the natural and cultural beauty of the Basque coast. Authentic and atypical villages mingle with famous seaside resorts such as Saint-Jean-de-Luz, the final destination of the itinerary.

The route, which follows the route of the Vélodyssée, begins in the center of the charming village of Bidart. Start with a slight detour to discover the Madeleine chapel in Bidart. Perched on the cliff, it offers a sumptuous view of the Basque coast. Your gaze wanders over the ocean horizon before settling on the peaks of Rhune and Trois Couronnes.

You continue to cycle along superb unspoiled beaches and coves where the most experienced surfers face the waves of the Atlantic. These are all possibilities for taking beautiful and refreshing breaks.

Then the bay of Saint-Jean-de-Luz reveals itself. You reach it via its emblematic Sainte-Barbe point. This remarkable site offers a 360-degree panorama of the bay of Saint-Jean-de-Luz and the Rhune, the Three Crowns and the Jaizkibel, three emblematic peaks of the Basque Country.

Once you arrive in Saint-Jean-de-Luz, make a gourmet stop in front of the fishing port while tasting the famous macaroons from Adam's.

name: Saliencia Lakes Loop – Somiedo Natural Park
name_original: Circular de los lagos de Saliencia — Parque natural de Somiedo
description:
If we think about Asturias and its lakes, for most people the first ones that come to mind are the mythical lakes of Covadonga. But those of Saliencia, in the Somiedo natural park, are a diamond that has nothing to envy.

On this Route you will discover the four lakes that make up this group: Calabazosa, Cerveriz, Almagrera lagoon and La Cueva. The path gives you endless panoramic views that will keep your mouth open throughout the entire excursion. It is a long route, with some fairly steep climbs, and is well marked with markings and milestones. Keep in mind that you will not find fountains, so it is essential that you bring enough water. 

The Route can be done all year round, but in winter you may find snow and in summer it is very hot and there is no shade at all. Finally, the starting point is located at the top of Farrapona. There is a parking lot here where you can leave your vehicle.

name: Ascent to La Maroma – Sierras de Tejeda, Almijara y Alhama Natural Park
name_original: Ascensión a La Maroma — Parque natural Sierras de Tejeda, Almijara y Alhama
region: National Park Sierra de Tejeda, Almijara y Alhama
description:
La Maroma is the highest peak in Malaga, so it is not surprising that it gives you some of the most incredible views in the region. From its summit you can see the Mediterranean Sea and even the high peaks of the Sierra Nevada.

The path is a constant climb that begins protected by pine trees and ascends to a place where there is less and less vegetation. Halfway there you find the Temple viewpoint, the perfect place to regain strength and enjoy the landscape. You do the last stretch cresting, in an exercise to maintain your balance and ignore the vertigo. Special mention to the horses and goats that can be seen many days right at the summit.

As for the weather, it is best to avoid summer due to the heat. In winter, you may encounter ice and some snow. The Route begins in the El Robledal recreational area, where you can park your vehicle. The place is also a controlled camping area that has barbecues, services and a fountain. In addition, just five kilometers away, you will find the Los Caños de la Alcaicería Hotel Restaurant, which offers you accommodation and a hearty dish to recharge your batteries.

name: Requena, Chera, and Sierra de Chiva Loop
name_original: Gravel ride from Requena to Chera and sierra de Chiva
description: This difficult 58.8-mile (94.6 km) gravel ride from Requena to Chera and Sierra de Chiva offers challenging climbs and scenic views.

name: Coastal gravel route from Zarautz to Lekeitio
name_original: Ruta gravel costera de Zarautz a Lekeitio
description: This difficult 33.8-mile (54.5 km) gravel route from Zarautz to Lekeitio offers challenging climbs and stunning Basque Coast views.

name: Pla de Mallorca Gravel Loop from Palma
name_original: Ruta gravel desde Palma a Santa Maria del Camí
description: This difficult 56.9-mile gravel ride from Palma to Santa Maria del Camí offers diverse terrain and vineyard views for fit cyclists.

name: Els Empedrats and Refuge Sant Jordi
name_original: Els Empedrats y refugio de Sant Jordi
description:
This incredible route takes you to discover one of Catalonia's most spectacular natural features: Els Empedrats. To get here, you follow the riverbed, which forms pools and waterfalls that you won't be able to stop photographing. Halfway through the journey, we recommend taking a short detour to see the beautiful Bullidor de la Llet waterfall. Once back on the main trail, the best part of Els Empedrats awaits you: the central section, where the high stone walls that surround you are breathtaking.

The route takes you to the beautiful Sant Jordi refuge. Want to recharge your batteries before returning to the starting point along the same path? Order one of their delicious burgers. It's best to book in advance, though.

Keep in mind that you'll have to cross the river on occasion, so this route is not recommended in case of frost or if it has rained heavily and the river is carrying a lot of water. The starting point of the trail is the La Salle Nature Classroom. There is a parking lot where you can park your car.

name: Castillo de Montesquiu and Besora Castle
name_original: Castillo de Montesquiu y castillo de Besora
description:
The historical and heritage importance of the Montesquiu and Besora castles is undeniable. And with this itinerary, you can explore both, as well as the surrounding landscapes, which are spectacular at any time of year.

The route begins and ends at the parking lot of the 9th-century Montesquiu Castle. It's open to the public and can be visited, but you must book in advance. Once underway, the path offers incredible panoramic views before reaching the most spectacular section of the trail: the Mongia Pass. This spectacular pass runs through marl, a reddish carbonate sedimentary rock containing many marine fossils.

A little further on, you'll spot the other star of this adventure: Besora Castle. Visit its ruins and those of the Romanesque church of Santa Maria, and enjoy the privileged views from the top of the hill where it stands. Very close by is Santa María de Besora, where you'll find the necessary services to recharge your batteries before returning to the point where you started the Route.

name: l'Ametlla de Mar to l'Ampolla
name_original: De l’Ametlla de Mar a l’Ampolla
description:
The coastal path from l'Ametlla de Mar to l'Ampolla is one of the most popular sections of the GR® 92. The reason? It runs along one of the most beautiful corners of the Catalan coast. The trail offers incredible views of the Mediterranean, imposing cliffs, coves with emerald waters, and a pine forest that almost reaches the sea.

As you can imagine, in addition to passing through incredible places, this adventure has a very attractive bonus: the many opportunities it offers to stop at idyllic coves and beaches and take a refreshing dip. These highlights include the beautiful coves of Santes Creus, the Estany Podrit cove, and L'Illot beach.

The route begins in l'Ametlla de Mar and ends in l'Ampolla. If you don't have a backup car, we recommend taking the train back to l'Ametlla de Mar. Both towns have countless services.

name: Farena and the Toll de l'Olla
name_original: Farena y el Toll de l’Olla
description:
This circular route takes you to one of the most iconic sites in the Prades Mountains: the impressive pool of Toll de l'Olla, on the Brugent River. The route begins and ends in Farena, an architectural marvel located in the heart of the Prades Mountains. Don't start the route without exploring it.

The Toll de l'Olla hides a very interesting legend. It is said that, at the beginning of the 17th century, local residents fell ill and asked a "farebona" (good witch) for help. She made a pact with them: she would find the herbs needed to cure them, and they would find a pot that would hold one liter of water per sick person. When the residents failed, the farebona transformed this part of the Brugent River into the Toll de l'Olla.

Swimming is permitted. If you prefer, you can also go upriver a few meters and find more pools perfect for a dip.

name: Els Estrets d'Arnes and Toll Blau
name_original: Els Estrets d’Arnes y el Toll Blau
description:
The Estrets d'Arnes are located within the Els Ports Natural Park and are one of the natural wonders of Catalonia. The Estrets River runs through the center of the impressive gorge with its vertical walls, forming pools where swimming is not permitted.

The only pool along the route where swimming is permitted is the Toll Blau, located right at the end of the trail. Shallow and located in an incredible setting, it is the best reward after the hike.

The route is not difficult at all and begins at the La Franqueta recreational area. You can park your vehicle here. Please note that the city council restricts access in summer, so you should find out before you go: https://parcsnaturals.gencat.cat/ca/xarxa-de-parcs/ports/gaudeix-del-parc/equipaments-i-itineraris/itineraris-arees-lleure/franqueta-horta-sant-joan/

name: Cabrera de Mar to Castell de Burriac
name_original: De Cabrera de Mar al castillo de Burriac
description:
This circular route starts and ends in Cabrera de Mar and allows you to discover two very interesting spots: on the one hand, the ruins of Burriac Castle and, on the other, the impressive Creu de Montcabrer viewpoint.

To reach the castle, you walk through a Mediterranean forest to the top of the hill where it stands. Its 401 meters of altitude provide incredible views of the area.

Then, retrace your steps to descend the hill and continue to the next point on the route. The Creu de Montcabrer viewpoint rises 311 meters and the views are breathtaking. On one side, you can see the Mediterranean Sea. On the other, you can see the castle you left behind a while ago. Furthermore, the iron cross that rises above the viewpoint has a long history of legends. It's a magical place.

name: Penedès 360 by gravel bike
name_original: Penedès 360 en bici de gravel
description:
The Penedès color palette dresses up every season. Its green tones encompass vineyards, cypresses, pines, poplars, riverside reeds and endless crops. The perennial ochers spread between the earth and the stones that cement its towns, hermitages, farmhouses, wineries and castles. This Route takes you to explore the wonders of this region on its extensive gravel tracks.

The route begins and ends at the Vilafranca del Penedès train station and extends more than 160 kilometers that take you to visit humble Romanesque hermitages, the ancient castles of Penyafort and Castellet, beautiful towns with a medieval feel and wineries surrounded by a tapestry of vineyards where you can taste traditional cava. As if that were not enough, the majestic mountain of Montserrat appears on the horizon in many sections, giving the journey an unforgettable touch.

This itinerary is ideal in spring and autumn, and also on many winter days, thanks to the mild temperatures of Penedés and its numerous sunny days. Along the journey you pass through towns that have the tourist services you may need. Therefore, it is not necessary to carry too much luggage, but rather the desire to enjoy every pedal stroke through these lands of wine and sun.

name: Gravel in the Val d'Aran – Prüedo per Valarties
name_original: Gravel por la Val d'Aran — Prüedo por Valarties
description:
The ascent to Prüedo from Arties along the Valarties track is a concentrate of views and Pyrenean adventure. Although the distance and gradient figures are not striking, the amount of landscapes it crosses and the physical and technical challenge that some of the sections of this Route pose are an appetizer of the Catalan mountains.

The route is circular from a car park in Arties. We recommend that you bring enough water and food for the entire journey, in addition to bringing the bike in good condition and carrying some tools and light spare parts.

The climb is demanding, but the wonderful environment you climb through makes it worth every pedal stroke. Shortly before reaching the top, you can make a gentle detour to visit the beautiful lakes of Montcasau. The upper area is a spectacle: at more than 2000 meters above sea level, the panoramic views of the peaks are breathtaking.

The descent is also an adventure, try not to let the views distract you. Although, surely, you will make some stops to immortalize the landscape on a postcard. Before returning to Arties, the beautiful Tredós awaits you, a town of stone houses and winding streets. Both here and in Arties you can take the opportunity to delight in the local cuisine and recharge your batteries.

name: The heart of the Catalan Pyrenees – La Fera gravel
name_original: El corazón del Pirineo catalán — La Fera gravel
description:
The great La Fera crossing takes you on a comprehensive tour of the Catalan Pyrenees. The route not only covers the great natural heritage that is the Aigüestortes i Estany de Sant Maurici National Park and the Alt Pirineu Natural Park, but also immerses you in living mountains with ancient livestock traditions and culture.

The route is circular from the Pobla de Segur train station, but due to its length, you can start it where it suits you best and shorten it thanks to the variants (https://feragravel.com/) and links it has. We recommend doing this Route between the end of June and the beginning of October, when there is more guarantee of good weather and that the high areas are clear of snow.

We also recommend that you carry out prior maintenance on your mount and that you bring tools and spare parts. Also, bring enough food and drink, as there are long stretches through high mountains and remote places.

The Fera covers both sides of the Pyrenees and runs through the valleys of the Noguera Ribagorçana, Noguera Pallaresa, Garona and Segre rivers. Its landscapes are home to an enormous cultural heritage whose traditions have been cultivated in this mountain range for millennia. Remote mountain huts, high mountain meadows and villages where time stood still centuries ago give meaning to the word bucolic. This journey is one of those where, when you return, you are not the same person who set out.

name: Cambrils to Castell d'Escolnarbou by gravel bike
name_original: De Cambrils al Castell d’Escolnarbou en bici de gravel
description:
This circular route from the Cambrils train station is ideal for a morning, or full day, of enjoyment, pedaling and gastronomy. The route escapes main roads and passes through charming towns such as Riudoms, Montbrió del Camp or Riudecanyes, each perfect for a gastronomic stop.

The sporting highlight is the ascent to Castell d'Escolnarbou, a medieval stronghold among the hills covered by the Mediterranean forest of pines and oaks. To link the modernity of the coast with the traditions of the castle, you pedal along a beautiful network of gravel and asphalt tracks that cross a tapestry of orchards of all kinds.

We recommend doing this outing on sunny days from autumn to spring. Winter in this area is very mild and pleasant to ride quietly. Although the Route is not very long, it is preferable that you bring some spare parts, tools and snacks to help you out of any unforeseen event.

To finish, you can make a reservation at one of the restaurants on the Cambrils promenade and treat yourself to a gastronomic treat before returning home. What better ending to this adventure!

name: Tortosa to Deltebre by gravel bike
name_original: De Tortosa a Deltebre en bici de gravel
description:
We invite you to accompany the majestic Ebro on its final stretch to the sea. This Route enters the network of gravel tracks and irrigation canals that extends between the rice fields and wetlands of Deltebre.

The route is circular from the Bikefriendly SB Corona de Tortosa hotel (https://sbhotelsandbike.sbhotels.es/). This small city deserves a walk through the center, either on the way out or on the way back, to enjoy its architecture, charm and gastronomy. Between Tortosa and Amposta you get close to the banks of the Ebro, where you can pedal at a good pace as you enjoy the breeze and the landscape. Along the way you will find numerous lagoons ideal for contemplating birdlife.

Despite the distance, this Route is not too demanding. However, it is recommended that you bring enough water and food, as well as some spare parts and tools that will allow you to get out of any unforeseen event.

Any sunny day from autumn to spring is perfect for cycling along the Ebro Delta. At this time of year, temperatures are mild thanks to the many sunny days at the mouth of the river. In summer, it is also possible to do the route, but not during the central hours of the day, but rather early or late in the day, when you will enjoy enviable light.

name: Camí natural de l'Ebre (Ebro Natural Path)
name_original: Camí natural de l'Ebre
description:
This circular route takes you to discover the most charming corners of the Ebro Delta, offering you the privilege of cycling alongside the river in its last stretch before it flows into the Mediterranean Sea. During the tour, you have the opportunity to visit the pier and various bird observatories, as well as zigzagging through the vast rice fields of the delta. In September, the rice harvest becomes a fascinating spectacle, with locals working in the fields, adding a unique and authentic touch to your experience.

The adventure begins from the Plaça de l'Escorxador car parks in Tortosa, an ideal starting point for exploring the beauty of the Ebro Delta. The route covers approximately 93 kilometres and is characterised by its low gradient, making it accessible to a wide variety of cyclists. The route runs along compact dirt roads and quiet roads, offering a relaxed and pleasant ride.

You can do this route at any time of the year, although it is important to bear in mind that during the summer the sun shines brightly and there is little shade to rest in. However, this can be the ideal excuse to stop at one of the bars in the area, where you can replenish your energy by enjoying the delicious local food.

name: Wine Loop
name_original: La Wine Minera
description:
On this circular route, the Llobregat and Cardener rivers act as main guides, accompanying you throughout the journey. The adventure includes a climb to the top of the mountain, where the ancient town of Castelladral awaits you, a perfect place to take a break and enjoy the panoramic views from 652 metres high. In addition, if you are a wine lover, you have several opportunities to enjoy delicious tastings at local wineries, adding a special touch to your experience on this charming journey.

The route covers approximately 61 kilometres, with a cumulative positive gradient of 810 metres. To make the most of the experience, we recommend having an intermediate physical level, as the journey can be quite demanding. 

The roads used on the Route are in good condition and are generally quiet, although during rush hour and on holidays you may encounter a higher volume of traffic.

name: Via Augusta Penedès
name_original: Vía Augusta Penedès
description:
This route immerses you in history, as its route is based on the Via Augusta Penedès, which follows the layout of an ancient Iberian road transformed into a road by the Romans in the 2nd century BC.
