The landscape is made up of a mosaic of vineyards, whose tones change with each season of the year. If you like wine or cava, you are in the right place.

This is a linear route that starts in Sant Salvador de la Calçada (Gelida) and ends in Bellvei. Throughout its 53 kilometres, you experience a continuous up and down, accumulating 560 metres of positive gradient. The route takes place on secondary roads and compacted dirt tracks. Although it has some gradients, it is not particularly demanding, making it suitable for cyclists of any level.

The route offers you multiple opportunities to stop and enjoy the local gastronomy, as it passes through various towns along the route. This is why you only need to carry some snacks and enough water on the route.

name: Via Verda Pallaresa
name_original: Ruta dels Llacs
description:
This is a linear route that starts in La Pobla de Segur and ends in Cellers. Both towns are well connected, which simplifies the logistics of the trip. They are also perfect places to stock up on food and drink. Tremp is located halfway along the route and can be another strategic point for replenishing supplies.

The route immerses you in a landscape of diverse flora, where pine forests, bushes, local crops and wild plants intertwine, creating a natural environment full of contrasts. As you pedal, you follow the course of canals and rivers such as the Noguera Pallaresa, and border the serene swamps of Sant Antoni and Terradets. This Route offers such a pleasant experience that it will leave you wanting to continue exploring.

Perhaps the most enchanting seasons are spring and autumn, when colours transform the landscape into a vibrant canvas. In addition, temperatures are usually quite pleasant, which makes the journey more pleasant. However, we recommend checking the weather forecast to make sure you are wearing the right clothing.

name: Vía Verde Agost – Maigmó and Tibi pass: loop from Sant Vicent del Raspeig
name_original: Vía Verde Agost - Maigmó y puerto de Tibi — Circular desde Sant Vicent del Raspeig
region: Sant Vicent del Raspeig / San Vicente del Raspeig
description:
Trails through desert landscapes, paths between vineyards and orchards, mountain landscapes, ravines and beach, this demanding route has it all. The route is circular and goes from the San Vicente del Raspeig train station towards the Maigmó mountain range, riding along cycle paths, agricultural roads and the Agost - Maigmó Greenway in a constant, but gentle, climb.

Almost halfway along the route you find yourself in Tibi, an ideal town for a stop that allows you, if you want, to recharge your batteries. Next you have a short, but slightly more demanding climb, which includes a detour to crown the Tibi pass by road. If you prefer to continue, the agricultural landscape of the Monnegre valley awaits you, whose slopes are covered with terraced orchards where almond trees, olive trees, citrus trees and vegetables thrive.

Little by little, you will make your way through small streets in the more urbanised areas of the coast until you reach the beach of San Juan, ideal for a swim. You will continue along coastal cycle paths to Alicante and cross it on its friendly network of cycle paths until you return to the San Vicente del Raspeig station. Before leaving, you can take advantage of the opportunity to treat yourself in any of its bars or restaurants.

name: La Pedrera reservoir and Lagunas de La Mata and Torrevieja: loop from Torrevieja
name_original: Embalse de La Pedrera y lagunas de La Mata y Torrevieja — Circular desde Torrevieja
description:
The adventure is circular from a car park in Torrevieja, very close to the natural park of the Lagunas de La Mata and Torrevieja. Between the views of the pink waters of the Torrevieja lagoon, salt flats and palm groves, you advance towards the only climb of the day. Almost all the gradient of this route is accumulated in a pleasant ascent through the Mediterranean forest and almond plantations between San Miguel de Salinas and the port of Rebate.

From this height, where the views are spectacular, you descend through a sea of plots of almond and olive trees. The fertility of the landscape contradicts its desert appearance. Further on you find yourself before the turquoise views of the waters of the La Pedrera reservoir, surrounded by a sea of citrus trees. Between endless plantations and irrigation channels you reach the lagoons of Torrevieja and La Mata, an oasis for aquatic birds, especially flamingos. The route takes you to several viewpoints where you can take photos.

The route skirts, but does not enter, the different towns. This is why it is essential that you bring with you all the hydration and food you may need during the day. If you prefer, you can make a detour in San Miguel de Salinas, to make a brief stop.

name: Amadòrio reservoir and views of Puig Campana: loop from Benidorm
name_original: Embalse de Amadòrio y vistas al Puig Campana — Circular desde Benidorm
description:
From the vibrant city of Benidorm and its seafront, through the wild landscape of the Amadòrio River and the spectacular views of Puig Campana, this route has a bit of everything. You leave Benidorm train station and go down to the coast, ride along the beach and start the climb towards the mountains.

The route gradually leaves the intense urban area and climbs along the Amadòrio River while enjoying the views of its ravine. You cross the reservoir dam and, as you skirt the water, you reach a viewing point from which you can see the mountains and the Mediterranean forest. A little further on you find Bella Orcheta where you can refill water and stock up on supplies.

As you continue up the river ravine, the landscape becomes increasingly wild and the reddish and ochre tones of the land become vibrant. There are even some easy stretches of trail waiting for you to savour nature even more. Almost in the second third of the route, you pass through Finestrat, a steep town where you can take a break and recharge your batteries in any of its bars. From here, the presence of the majestic Puig Campana accompanies you until you return to Benidorm.

name: Tour of the Font Roja Natural Park: loop from Alcoy
name_original: Vuelta al Parque Natural de la Font Roja — Circular desde Alcoy
description:
To the southwest of Alcoi is the Font Roja Natural Park, where oaks mix with pines and other tree species, giving the Mediterranean landscape a different touch. This circular route goes around this natural park from the Alcoi train station.

The itinerary takes you along several sections of greenway with different surfaces, both paved and gravel. Although the more than 47 kilometres and the nearly 700 metres of gradient are not large figures, on this day you do not pass through any towns, so it is advisable to bring enough food and water to complete the route. Even so, almost halfway along the route, you border Tibi, in case you feel like or need a detour.

Both sides of the Carrascar de la Font Roja are very different. The south is dry and desert-like, the north is wooded and with shady stretches. But on both sides, humans have taken advantage of the land to cultivate whatever is possible. Apart from the typical olive, almond and fruit trees, you can cross areas with cereal fields, which are much less typical of the Mediterranean interior. At the end, another stretch of greenway takes you back to Alcoi. If you didn't stop at the start, you still have time to enjoy this magnificent city full of history and culture.

name: Serpis Greenway and Beniarrés Reservoir: loop from Alcoy
name_original: Vía Verde de Serpis y Embalse de Beniarrés — Circular desde Alcoy
description:
This route is an adventure along the network of dirt and asphalt tracks that stretches out between the olive groves, almond groves, vineyards and citrus fields of the Alcoy river plain. The route is circular from the Alcoi train station and the route links various towns that await you with a varied gastronomic offer. Even so, it is worth taking some provisions. 

If you look up to the west, as you advance through the valley, you enjoy charming views of the limestone massifs that break up the landscape and crown the Sierra de Mariola natural park and the Penyes Albes natural area. Before reaching Lorcha, you ride along a section of the Serpis Greenway that passes by the old station, currently in disuse. 

On the return leg, you skirt the Beniarrés reservoir, whose sheet of water contrasts with the steep crests of the mountains. The gradient is well distributed throughout this accessible route, ideal for enjoying a day in which you can ride calmly and enjoy every corner of inland Alicante.

name: Port of Sa Creueta: loop from Olla de Altea
name_original: Puerto de Sa Creueta — Circular desde Olla de Altea
description:
Fertile and wild river plains, mountain paths, charming rural villages and a touch of the coast make this demanding route the perfect catalogue for a cycling adventure along the Costa Blanca. Along the more than 90 kilometres of the route, you pass through various towns with tourist services of all kinds. Even so, the mountain and cropped sections are more remote, so we recommend that you take everything you need to be self-sufficient.

The route is circular from the Olla Altea tram station. The path begins with a short climb along the mouth of the Algar River and then through Guadalest. A little later, you pass through Polop and enter demanding climbs along agricultural tracks and secondary roads until you cross Callosa d'en Sarrià and Tàrbena, on the way to the Sa Creueta mountain pass. Take it easy, as almost 2,000 metres of positive gradient await you in total.

From the port, a final steep climb awaits you, which ends by taking you down the Camí de la Llacuna, a friendly track that crosses the spectacular Alicante mountain landscape. Once you finish the descent, you roll along the plain of the Xaló River and enjoy its endless collage of orchards. This section passes through several towns that are ideal for making another stop and recharging your batteries.

Before returning to the coast, you pedal along the highway on tracks that take you back to the desert-like views and shades of red and ochre earth. Once on the coast, there are a couple of opportunities for a restorative dip in the sea.

name: Pego-Oliva Marsh Nature Reserve: loop from Denia
name_original: Parque natural de la marjal de Pego - Oliva — Circular desde Denia
description:
Water is the star of this easy circular route from Dénia train station. To get out and back to the city, you can take advantage of the charming greenway. This runs through an area of agricultural plots where palm trees and fruit trees are greening up. Until the only climb on the route, you pass through a series of small towns that are ideal for taking a break and enjoying some tapas.

The route takes you along the Girona River plain to the foot of the short path, which has a challenging climb up the Pego mountain. During the descent, you enjoy magnificent views of the marsh and the rural landscape of Alicante. Once down, you go along paths between citrus plantations until you reach the Vedat River, a charming course of crystal-clear waters where you can probably get a little wet. From here, you cross the Marjal de Pego - Oliva Natural Park, one of the most important marshes in Alicante, as it is home to a great variety of local birds and flora.

Before returning to the Denia railway, you have a long stretch along a bike path very close to the coast, where you find numerous passages that invite you to take a nice dip in the Mediterranean before finishing the day.

name: Puerto del Espino and La Torre de les Maçanes – loop from Busot
name_original: Puerto del Espino y La Torre de les Maçanes — Circular desde Busot
description:
This route is a variant of the Busot Iron Gravel Sprint that starts from the public car park of the municipal school. Although the distance is not very long, the almost 1,300 metres of positive gradient make it a good sporting challenge. On the other hand, there is only one pass through town, in this case, La Torre de les Maçanes (Torremanzanas), at the other end, so remember to bring enough water and food.

The route mostly runs along dirt tracks or rough asphalt. One of the highlights of the day is the climb to Puerto del Espino, which has concrete ramps whose surface is a clear indicator of the gradient. What is guaranteed are the endless views that disappear over infinite blue mountain ridges.

The paths disappear into almost virgin landscapes and the visions of cliffs and crags follow one another at every bend in the road. You also cross areas of cultivated fields, but very little frequented by people, which increases the chances of wildlife sightings. Keep an eye out for goats, roe deer, partridges and hares that you may see along the way. In Busot you have several restaurants where you can enjoy a well-deserved treat at the end of the route.

name: Between Landes and Gascony
name_original: Entre Landes et Gascogne
description:
From Lake Clarens, in Casteljaloux, this route takes you to discover the southwest of the department. The beginning of the circuit crosses a pine forest, typical of the landscapes bordering the Landes region.

The So Vélo greenway, built on a former railway line, ensures a smooth ride to Marmande. A junction then leads to Montpouillan, in a bucolic setting on the banks of the Garonne Canal. You follow the canal to the village of Mas-d'Agenais, known for housing a Rembrandt painting in its Romanesque church. Further on, the Sénestis municipal forest offers a wilder and more refreshing section.

The route then returns to Casteljaloux via the greenway. The very moderate terrain makes this route accessible regardless of your fitness level. The entire loop is well-marked, with several options for rest and refreshments.

It is an ideal route for discovering varied landscapes, while enjoying cycle paths away from road traffic.

name: The Gascon hillsides of Albret
name_original: Les coteaux gascons en Albret
description:
From Nérac, a former royal city on the banks of the Baïse River, this route takes you to the heart of the Albret region and its Gascon hillsides. As you leave the town, the road gently climbs towards open ridges, offering vast panoramas of the surrounding valleys. On clear days, the Pyrenees appear on the horizon.

The route crosses peaceful countryside punctuated by small villages. Francescas and Moncrabeau mark the route. The latter, known for its Académie des Menteurs (Liars' Academy), offers a lovely rural village atmosphere. From Moncrabeau, the route rejoins the Baïse Valley cycle route, which is more rolling, for a leisurely journey to Nérac.

The diversity of the landscapes and terrain makes this route enjoyable at any time of year. The starting and finishing points, Nérac, allow you to combine the outing with a visit to the historic center or a break by the water.

name: Discovering the Buzet vineyards
name_original: A la découverte des vignobles de Buzet
description:
This trail explores the vineyards of Buzet, between the Garonne Valley and the hillsides bordering the Baïse River. The trail starts from the fortified village of Lavardac, once a crossroads for river trade. Now a marina, the village boasts an authentic rural atmosphere, perfect for a stroll.

The loop takes you through several major heritage sites, beginning with the largest fortified mill in France, the Moulin des Tours de Barbaste. Next come the Château de Xaintrailles and the fortified towns of Vianne and Damazan. The rural churches of Saint-Pierre-de-Buzet and Ambrus also punctuate the route. Between the two rivers, the landscapes vary from open vineyards to denser forests and flatlands.

The terrain is concentrated at the beginning and end of the trail. The central, flatter section runs along the banks of the Garonne in a calm and bucolic atmosphere. This circuit, when it does not use a cycle route, runs along small, quiet roads and offers a beautiful crossing of the region's wine-growing lands.

name: Terras de Sicó Loop from Ansião
name_original: Giro ad anello da Ansião
description: This difficult 38.0-mile gravel biking loop from Ansião in Terras de Sicó offers historical sites and scenic viewpoints.

name: The Pilones of the Garganta de los Infiernos (Gorge of the Hells)
name_original: Los Pilones de la Garganta de los Infiernos
description:
This incredible, little-frequented circular route allows you to discover one of the most impressive corners of the Jerte Valley. From the Garganta de los Infiernos Interpretation Center, the trail winds through oak trees and clearings overlooking the deep canyon carved by meltwater.

For centuries, the Jerte River has been shaping the so-called giant's marmitas, enormous natural pools you'll find in the Los Pilones area, one of the most photogenic spots along the route, where there's also a drinking water fountain and bathing areas. Depending on the time of year you go, take advantage of the opportunity to take a dip and enjoy this incredible and unique landscape.

We suggest completing the loop by taking the detour to the Chorrero de la Virgen viewpoint and the Escribano refuge. From here, the landscape changes, and the trail continues along a less attractive but peaceful forest track, among chestnut trees, until it returns to the starting point.

In short, this is an accessible and well-marked route through northern Extremadura, where water and rock tell stories of erosion, time, and silence. You can park in two designated areas in the village. It's a good idea to pack snacks and plenty of water, as well as sunscreen, especially in the summer.

name: Ponds and tranquility of Los Barruecos
name_original: Charcas y Silencios de Los Barruecos
description:
In the municipality of Malpartida de Cáceres lies one of the most striking landscapes in Extremadura: Los Barruecos. Declared a Natural Monument in 1996, this spot stands out for its enormous granite formations shaped by time. Its artificial pools and unique biodiversity will amaze you. The setting is perfect for a comfortable and accessible route. Remember to bring some snacks, as well as water and sunscreen, especially in summer.

During the route, you will follow paths that cross lagoons, streams, and rocks that appear to be hand-sculpted. You will also be able to enjoy the Barrueco de Abajo and Barrueco de Arriba ponds separately. The first, which is the best known and collects rainwater, is a good location to observe the storks nesting high on the rocks. The second, somewhat further away and less traveled, has a wilder feel.

This scenic spot, which you can reach by car or bus from Cáceres to Malpartida de Cáceres, combines history, natural art, and wildlife in a single trip. It's definitely worth setting aside a day to explore this special yet little-known corner of Extremadura.

name: Return to Proserpina Reservoir
name_original: Vuelta al embalse de Proserpina
description:
The Proserpina Reservoir Circular Route, located in Mérida, is an affordable and easy route that invites you to enjoy nature without major physical demands. It can be completed in about an hour at a leisurely pace, making it a perfect option for families and people of all ages.

This flat route follows well-defined trails surrounding the reservoir, allowing for both walking and recreational cycling. It is a popular destination for those seeking a nature escape without straying too far from the city.

One of the route's main attractions is the Proserpina Roman Dam, an archaeological remains that add character to the landscape. The surrounding area offers views of the reservoir waters and the surrounding vegetation, creating a peaceful and relaxing atmosphere. Comfortable shoes and water are recommended, especially on hot days, to fully enjoy the experience.

This hike allows you to reconnect with nature, birdwatch, or simply unwind in a serene setting, making it an ideal excursion for those looking for a simple yet enriching experience near Mérida.

name: Cornalvo Natural Park
name_original: Parque Natural de Cornalvo
description:
The Cornalvo Natural Park Route, in Mérida, is a circular route that begins very close to the Campomanes farmhouse. We recommend arriving by car, parking on the side of the road at the starting point. After a pleasant start, you reach the historic Roman dam of the Cornalvo reservoir. We invite you to stop and appreciate this beautiful spot.

After crossing it, you continue along a path between holm oaks and rockroses bordering the reservoir until you reach the Mentidero farmhouse. From there, an uphill and downhill trail crosses the Rugidero area, next to the Las Muelas stream, and flows into the reservoir of the same name, an ideal area for birdwatching. If you have brought binoculars, don't hesitate to use them; it's a good place to do so.

The route continues along a wide track between meadows until it connects with the park's main track. Head southwest to the top of Sierra Bermeja along the Suministros road, with the Terrero peak on your right.
The last trail of this beautiful and secluded route passes through a cork oak forest and the picturesque Calle Sierpes, ending with a stretch through brambles that takes you back to the starting point, in Campomanes.

name: Raso and the Castro Celta Loop
name_original: Circular del Raso y el Castro Celta
description:
This circular route begins next to the El Cardenillo restaurant, which has a parking lot where you can leave your vehicle. It is from this same point that you cross the Roman bridge of Madrigal and begin an ascent toward the El Raso road.

After several intersections, you continue through pine forests toward the Las Planas area. Along the route, you alternate between sections of track and trail, passing through areas such as Los Reverteros, which follows the GR-180 for a short stretch. A detour along a path leads to the Castro Celta, which you can skirt and visit inside, stopping at its viewpoint. It's interesting to read about its history at this point while you hydrate and have a snack from your backpack.

From the fort, the route descends toward the village of El Raso, crossing the Vejiga stream. In the village, you can stop for a drink at the bar, if it's open.

The return journey follows the GR-180 and trails until it connects with the same outward route. Finally, a path leads you to the Alardos Gorge, which you cross by hopping over rocks before returning to the starting point. Don't forget to bring sunscreen if you decide to do the route in spring or summer, as well as a coat during the rest of the year.

name: PR-CC 79 Ruta de los Pontones
description:
Route PR-CC 79 Los Pontones is a circular route that departs from the Tejeda de Tiétar bus stop in the Tiétar Valley, Cáceres. You can access it by public transport. The route follows well-marked trails, paths, and paths through the dehesa, gorges, and viewpoints.

You can do it in the opposite direction to the recommended route. Don't forget to visit the Chorrera de Matagarcía and enjoy the scenery among holm oaks, rockroses, and cattle. Depending on the time of year you take the circular route, you'll appreciate the vivid colors of autumn or the greenery that stands out in spring. We recommend both seasons.

After crossing the Garguera Gorge and reaching the waterfall, return to the main path to enjoy the natural surroundings: stone pontoons, waterways, and the banks of the gorges. On the final stretch, back to the village, you can visit the 16th-century Church of San Miguel, which houses the curious anthropomorphic tombstone called "La Muerte Pelona" (Bald Death), and the pillory known as "Micaela" in the main square.

This route combines heritage, water, and landscapes, making it ideal for autumn or spring, where you can enjoy both nature and local history. Don't forget to pack plenty of water and snacks to replenish your energy.

name: R2 – Reino del Carbón (Coal Kingdom)
name_original: R2 — Reino del Carbón
description:
This is one of the best options for those who enjoy short-travel bikes, even those who enjoy riding lycra, without losing the passion for riding narrow trails, taking on winding descents, and passing through forests while connecting villages.

This circular route from La Pola de Gordón is accessible to less technical cyclists. From the town center, you ascend toward the eastern slope, passing through Cueto San Mateo and under Alto de la Peña. The first climb is very steep, but you then continue on to short climbs while enjoying the high mountains. The descent includes the X Line Flow trail, where you'll enjoy letting off the brakes.

To return to the start, you pedal along paths and trails alternating between both sides of the Bernesga River and connecting charming small towns such as Puente de Alba, Peredilla, or Nocedo de Gordón. Any of these are ideal for getting to know the local culture.

name: R15 - Reino de León (Kingdom of León)
name_original: R15 — Reino de León
description:
From La Robla, you can take a loop to La Pola de Gordón, which climbs along the valley's eastern slope and descends along the most iconic flow trails.

This route is ideal for a short-range mountain bike with a dropper post. The route climbs toward La Muria Pass, Peña Castro, and the track overlooking Llombera, where you can make panoramic stops. It then descends along the Planeta Calleja and Flash Gordon trails, two of the red-category flow trails.

Halfway through the route, you pass through La Pola de Gordón. This is the perfect time to stop at one of its bars and refuel with local specialties. The return route to La Robla follows paths and trails along the beautiful banks of the Bernesga River.

name: Badalona & Parc Serralada Marina with the Gravelbike
name_original: Badalona & Parc Serralada Marina mit dem Gravelbike
description:
There is also a lot to discover in the direction of Badalona. On this tour you will explore the Park de la Serralada Marina, which also offers endless possibilities for mountain bike tours and hikes. You'll also get your money's worth with a gravel bike, because some routes promise pure gravel happiness.

You start in Glòries and drive the Diagonal straight to the Riu Besòs. Here a cycle path always leads along the shore, which you now follow out of town. Finally, you have already left the city limits behind you and turn right into the Parc Serralada de Marina.

It goes uphill on fine dirt and gravel paths and you reach the monastery de San Jerónimo de la Murtra. After a little exploration tour, we continue on beautiful gravel tracks lined with Aleppo pines.

Behind the Hospital Universitari there is a very steep driveway - here you can push a bit. After that, it won't be long before you discover Barcelona and the sea on the horizon.

The next gravel kilometers fly by. Then you make your way down and rush across the road back to the coast. In Badalona you can have a little refreshment on the beach promenade before you drive back to Barcelona.

name: Coast and Garraf Natural Park with the Gravel bike
name_original: Küste und Naturpark Garraf mit dem Gravelbike
description:
Steep climbs with an ocean panorama, the finest gravel roads, a Buddhist monastery and the famous Bodega Güell de Gaudí await you on the 60-kilometer tour through the Garraf Nature Park. You can take the train from Barcelona Sants train station to Platja de Castelldefels to save yourself the first 20 kilometers and get straight onto the more scenic route. You can also take the tour in the other direction and end on the coast.

From Platja de Castelldefels you first drive along the coast on a paved road, past Garraf and with a wonderful view of the blue sea to the horizon. Now you take the steepest climb of the tour under your tires: On the Camí de la Trinitat, the concrete surface is really steep. With a 14% incline, there's no shame in pushing the bike a bit. At the small chapel you can take a short breather and marvel at the view.

Soon you have conquered the first mountain and you are in the middle of the Parc del Garraf. On quiet gravel roads you roll through the special, gently rolling landscape with the typical Mediterranean thicket. A few kilometers further you will reach the monastery - it is worth taking a break here to admire the stupa and, if you have enough time, to take a guided tour. Or you'll come back another time; meditation courses and retreats are also offered.

For the next ten kilometers you drive on a fantastic gravel route across the nature park and finally reach Begues. You've managed the altitude difference for today - from now on it's only downhill.

After a kilometer-long, fast descent you reach the banks of the Llobregat, along which you drive a little further until you turn your back on nature and head back into the urban realm. Via the Carretera Reial, which later becomes the Avinguda del Paralel, you get back to the city and look back on a long, eventful day in the saddle.

name: Circular gravel of the Sierra de la Demanda
name_original: Circular gravel de la sierra de la Demanda
description:
You have a day ahead of you to enjoy a mythical and spectacular Route, one of which once you try it, you need to return from time to time to repeat it.

The starting and ending point of the route is the town of Ezcaray, which can be easily reached by bus from Logroño. Surrounded by the mountains, it offers you the best culinary qualities endorsed by Michelin stars and a good variety of accommodation, from hostels to luxury hotels. It is a perfect base camp to tackle this Route on a weekend getaway to La Rioja.

The itinerary runs practically entirely along good tracks, some paths and a bit of road.

You will climb to more than 2000 meters high. So take spare warm clothes, even in summer, as a sudden storm can always form and the temperature drops considerably.

You will find water sources along the way; the food you will have to carry it from the beginning.

If it is hard for you, there is a good alternative to shorten the Route by returning to a beautiful road, the LR-415 from the top of Cruz de la Demanda, at kilometer 22.

name: Utiel Urban Tourist Trail
name_original: El Sendero Urbano de Utiel

name: The Gorafe desert by gravel bike
name_original: El desierto de Gorafe en bici de gravel
description:
You are about to move to another planet. It does not matter what you have been told or what you have read about the Gorafe desert: it will surprise you, it will hook you.

There are almost 70 kilometers and just over 1,100 meters of positive elevation gain that are hard, although, yes, they will fly by thanks to the amazing scenery.

When you start pedaling from the town of Gorafe and immediately take the short but intense cement climb, you begin to get an idea of where you are going and that the day is going to be special. Little by little you gain height and take perspective on the valley and on the scale of the surrounding geomorphological formations. A couple more corners and suddenly it's over: you're on what looks like a plateau above the valley. Below you see the town, the river and the stone walls. Behind you, you see a huge, seemingly flat expanse. And you start pedaling along the track that goes north.

You leave behind the fields of almond trees and a couple of cave houses hanging on the wall. You have reached the Puntal de Don Diego viewpoint. Support the bike and approach the path to the esplanade of the geodesic vertex. Now you can see where you are going: you are entering the Gorafe desert.

The next 25 kilometres, until leaving the town of Villanueva de las Torres, make up the first part of the circular desert route. Wide tracks with good pavement, incredible ravines and an alien landscape.

After a short stretch of road, you will arrive at the town of Dehesas de Guadix, the last opportunity to replenish liquids and food until the end of the Route. Here begins the second part; The route goes back into the desert, this time along paths that are sometimes narrower, with good tracks and some sections along boulevards.

Reserve energy: the last 14 kilometers of the Route are uphill, most of the time flat but with a couple of notable slopes.

You just have to go down the cemented ramp to the town of Gorafe, recharge your batteries in one of its bars and enjoy having completed a stage that you will surely save among your favorites.

Some advices:

- You can visit the magnificent museum of the Megalithic Interpretation Center in Gorafe to round off the day.
- The wider the tires you have on your bike, the more comfortable the Route will be.
- To visit the Gorafe desert, the ideal is that it is not hot or that it has recently rained; so early winter or spring can be good dates.
- As in any good worthy desert, temperature variations during the day and at night are high. Bring a spare coat in case it gets late.

name: Circular gravel between Teruel and Albarracín
name_original: Circular gravel entre Teruel y Albarracín
description:
The growing fame of the award-winning Empty Mountains trip is putting this wonderful and uninhabited Aragonese territory of Teruel on the cycling map.

This Route proposes you, leaving the city of Teruel, to know some parts of the official route of the Empty Mountains route, spinning a circular itinerary that will take you to visit the town of Albarracín, a National Monument and proposed by UNESCO as a World Heritage Site. .

You will cross a good number of spectacular landscapes: the area of the Arcillas de Teruel, with its amazing colorful formations, the protected landscape of the Pinares de Rodeno and its dense forests, and part of the steep and ancient Sierra de Albarracín.

It is a comfortable route and perfect for gravel bikes. It is ideal to do it quietly in two stages, sleeping and enjoying the hospitality and culinary quality of the town of Albarracín. In total you will pedal about 77 kilometers and you will add 1200 meters of positive elevation gain.

The Route begins and ends at the Teruel railway station, a point that you can reach by train and which also has a large car park where you can also leave your vehicle during the days you spend pedaling in the area.

Since you are here, do not hesitate to visit both the historic and charming city of Teruel and the incredible town of Albarracín and the views from its walls at sunset.

Any time of the year is good to do this Route, as long as we have the proper preparation and equipment; although it is at the end of summer when you can enjoy the route the most, with pleasant temperatures and long days.

name: Gravel loop in Saja-Besaya natural park
name_original: Vuelta gravel al parque natural Saja-Besaya
description:
This Route takes you to discover the Saja-Besaya Natural Park, which takes its name from the rivers that delimit it.

The starting and ending point of the route is the town of Ruente, well connected to Cabezón de la Sal, where we can get there by train or bus.

After taking the track that goes up the Bayones river valley, you must turn south and start the first ascent of the day that will take you to the top of the Moral hermitage. As you gain altitude, the landscape unfolds, passing from dense valley bottoms to bare mountains.

After a nice descent towards the Saja river valley, take a stretch of road to Bárcena Mayor. Here begins one of the most spectacular parts of the Route: the climb through the beech forest to the Cruz de Fuentes.

The return, almost all downhill, is going to be done along the beautiful road that winds through the Saja river valley passing through the towns of El Tojo, Renedo and Valle until returning to Ruente.

When you complete the lap, you will have pedaled just over 85 kilometers and added more than 2000 meters of positive elevation gain. It is therefore a route in which good physical preparation is needed to face it safely.

It is an ideal route to do with a gravel bike, as it combines good tracks with sections that run along quiet roads.

I recommend you do the route in autumn, if it is not too cold, to enjoy the beautiful display of colors of the beech forests in their annual leaf shedding.

name: El Alto Tajo in gravel
name_original: El Alto Tajo en gravel
description:
Large gorges, turquoise waters, towns with history and a privileged environment to enjoy nature and visit the karst geomorphology. I suggest you pedal along this spectacular Route that runs through the Alto Tajo Natural Park, in the province of Guadalajara.

The start and end point of the route is Molina de Aragón. It is a very interesting town of medieval origin, head of the region, which offers a wide variety of accommodation and restaurants. I recommend a visit to its historic center with its Jewish quarter and the huge castle that presides over it.

In total, the route is 120 kilometers long and 1750 meters of elevation gain. It can be done in a single day. I recommend that if you have time, do it in two stages so you can enjoy it without rushing: the first to Peralejos de las Truchas and the second, back to Molina de Aragón.

The route runs through large dry farming fields until reaching the town of Taravilla. From there you will ascend to the wooded hills and passes to end up descending to Peralejo de las Truchas, the conceptual halfway point of the trip, where you can rest and recharge before entering the Tajo canyons, the most impressive part of the Route.

This second part takes place mostly on good tracks, a small section of non-cyclable path and a last section on a quiet road.

Upon reaching the San Pedro bridge, you will leave the Tagus River to take and go up the Gallo River canyon, until you return to Molina de Aragón.

I recommend that you make a stop to visit the sanctuary of the Virgen de la Hoz and, if you have time, walk up to the La Hoz viewpoint: a perfect ending to the trip.

It is very cold here in winter. It is one of the coldest areas of the Iberian Peninsula, so I recommend you come in the less hot parts of the summer.

name: Circular gravel on the Transpirenaica
name_original: Circular gravel por la Transpirenaica
description:
I propose a Route that combines sections of the Transpyrenean with others of the Pedals de Foc through a route that you will not forget.

If you have not already done the entire “Transpi” trip, with this itinerary you are going to try one of its most spectacular segments and you will have no choice but to plan a pedal vacation through the Pyrenees to cover the rest of the stages.

If you already know the area, you will know that you have ahead of you what for many are the most impressive landscapes in the whole of the Pyrenees.

83 kilometers and more than 2,000 meters of unevenness await you in a tough route, although without technical complications. Magnificent tracks and a bit of road to enjoy a day of authentic Pyrenean gravel.

The beginning and end of the route is the Sort bus station, with direct lines to Lleida and Barcelona, in case you decide to arrive by public transport.

name: Roger Lapébie and Entre-deux-Mers Loop
name_original: Boucle sur les hauteurs de Bordeaux
description: Cycle the moderate 29.2-mile Roger Lapébie and Entre-deux-Mers Loop, enjoying vineyards, forests, and a unique rail tunnel.

name: Entre-deux-Mers Excursion
name_original: Boucle entre vignes et châteaux
description: This difficult 68.4-mile road cycling loop takes you through picturesque vineyards and past historic castles, including Cadillac Castle.

name: Circular gravel through Ourense and Castrelo do Miño
name_original: Circular gravel por Ourense y Castrelo do Miño
description:
The region of Castrelo de Miño, cradle of the famous Ribeiro wine, is a fertile and undulating territory dominated by landscapes of extensive vineyards. Surrounded by mountains, it has a special climate that has favored its use and occupation by different peoples throughout history.

This route that I propose starts from the city of Ourense and takes you to visit the Val de Allariz and its villages until you reach Celanova and return, crossing the Arnoia river, to the Castrelo de Miño reservoir. In the last part of the route, you will go up the Miño River along its right bank until you return to the capital.

There are 107 kilometers and just over 1,700 meters of positive elevation gain in a demanding and somewhat leg-breaking route, although without major technical complications. The last 35 kilometers are very bearable; so if you get past the first 70, you'll have done the hardest part and the rest will be enjoying rolling along the riverbank.

The Route begins and ends at the FF station. DC. from Ourense, in case you want to combine public transport to get there.

name: PR-CV 370: Cova Bolumini and Cava de Don Miguel Loop
name_original: La Cava Don Miguel (PR-CV 370) — Circular desde Alfafara
description: Hike the moderate 8.4-mile La Cava Don Miguel (PR-CV 370) circular route from Alfafara, exploring ancient snow pits and panoramic views.

name: Roger Lapébie Trail and La Sauve-Majeure Abbey Loop
name_original: Boucle de l’abbaye de la Sauve-Majeure
description: Cycle the moderate 46.1-mile Roger Lapébie Trail and La Sauve-Majeure Abbey Loop, combining greenway riding with historical sites.

name: Bordeaux to Lacanau-Océan Greenway
name_original: Balade entre les pins jusqu’à Lacanau
description: This moderate 79.3-mile road cycling route takes you through pine forests and past Lacanau Lake, with 952 feet of elevation gain.

name: Boucle du Canal de Garonne et de la Garonne
name_original: Boucle du canal des deux mers
description: This 42.1-mile (67.8 km) moderate road cycling loop along the Canal des Deux Mers offers scenic views and historic towns.

name: Turia Gardens and Valencia Historic Center Loop
name_original: Los Jardines del Turia — Circular por el casco histórico de Valencia
description: Explore Valencia's historic center on this easy 4.0-mile (6.5 km) hike through the Turia Garden, featuring historic sites and lush greenery.

name: Port Saplaya and Playa de la Malvarrosa
name_original: Port Saplaya — Tour desde la Malvarrosa
description: This easy 4.5-mile (7.2 km) hiking route from Malvarrosa to Port Saplaya offers coastal views and unique canal-side architecture.

name: Old Devesa Trail
name_original: Parque Natural de L'Albufera — Tour por las playas del Saler
description: Hike the easy 4.9-mile (7.9 km) Parque Natural de L'Albufera — Tour of the Saler beaches, exploring fine sands, dunes, and pine woods.

name: Gola de Pujol and Pujol Lake Loop
name_original: Parque Natural de l‘Albufera — Itinerario Botánico
description: Hike the easy 3.1-mile Gola de Pujol and Pujol Lake Loop in L'Albufera Natural Park, featuring lake views and diverse birdlife.

name: Port de Catarroja Rice Fields Loop
name_original: Parque Natural de l'Albufera — Circular desde el Port de Catarroja
description: Enjoy an easy 3.1-mile circular hike through the rice fields and wetlands of Parque Natural de l'Albufera from el Port de Catarroja.

name: Cavall Bernat Panoramic Trail
name_original: Paraje Natural La Murta — Trail run hasta La Creu del Cardenal
description: The Cavall Bernat Panoramic Trail is a difficult 5.8-mile route with 1343 feet of elevation gain, offering dramatic coastal views but not su

name: Alzira to La Casella Trail
name_original: Paraje Natural La Casella - Trail run por el Pas del Pobre
description: This difficult 9.9-mile (15.9 km) jogging trail in La Casella Natural Park offers challenging ascents and panoramic views.

name: Jardí del Túria 5K Circuit
name_original: Valencia — Running por los Jardines del Túria y el Parque de Cabecera
description: Jog the Jardí del Túria 5K Circuit, a moderate 4.6-mile route through Valencia's Turia Gardens, featuring a low-impact surface.

name: El Picaio from Puçol Trail
name_original: El Picaio — Trail run desde Puçol
description: This difficult 7.7-mile trail run from Puçol offers challenging terrain and panoramic views of the Mediterranean Sea and Sagunto Castle.

name: Olocau Red Route: Sierra Calderona
name_original: El Penya Blanca — Running circular por la senda de Olocau
description: This moderate 3.6-mile (5.8 km) circular jogging route in Olocau offers 612 feet (186 metres) of elevation gain and scenic views towards Pen

name: Turia River and Vallesa Forest Loop
name_original: El Bosque de la Vallesa y el Parque Fluvial del Turia — Running circular desde El Clot
description: Jog this moderate 8.9-mile (14.4 km) circular route through Vallesa Forest and Turia River Park, with 325 feet (99 metres) of elevation gain

name: Comarca of Las Merindades in gravel
name_original: Comarca de Las Merindades en gravel
description:
To the north of the province of Burgos, on the Cantabrian mountain range, is the region of Las Merindades.

Glacial valleys, limestone mountains pierced by extensive and mysterious caves, flora, fauna, history and culture await you on this essential Route.

With almost 85 kilometers and 1,400 meters of cumulative elevation gain, you have a gravel journey ahead of you that will take you to discover a magical area that you will surely fall in love with.

Except for some short sections, the route is technically simple. The difficulties are more in the unevenness to overcome and in possible storms that may arise during the day.

The Route begins and ends at the Pedrosa de Valdeporres railway station, which you can easily reach by train with your bicycle.

name: Lac Vert Loop
name_original: Boucle de Canéjan vers le lac vert
description: Hike the moderate 5.1-mile Loop from Canéjan to Lac Vert, featuring forests, vineyards, and the serene Green Lake.

name: Gravel Northwest Greenway
name_original: Vía Verde del Noroeste en gravel
description:
This is one of the quintessential gravel courses in the Murcia region.

The greenway runs along the old route of the Murcia-Caravaca de la Cruz railway line, which was active until the mid-1970s.

Along its route you will cross nine beautiful viaducts and six tunnels with artificial lighting; in addition to passing by the old stations, many of them now used as hostels.

You can do the Route in an ascending direction from Murcia Capital to Caravaca de la Cruz, totaling almost 89 kilometers and just over 1000 meters of positive difference in altitude, or do it in the opposite direction and enjoy the descent. The real challenge is to do it there and back, approaching 180 kilometers and 1,500 meters of elevation gain.

I advise you to come in spring and enjoy the mild temperatures and the almond trees in bloom that you will find along much of the route.
To facilitate possible connections with public transport, the Route begins at the FFCC station in Murcia capital and ends at the bus station in Caravaca de la Cruz.

name: Longarisse Forest and Lake Loop
name_original: Boucle le long du lac de Lacanau
description: This easy 4.7-mile (7.6 km) hike around Lacanau lake offers picturesque scenery, diverse terrain, and serene lake views.

name: Panoramic Hillsides Trail
name_original: Boucle entre le parc de l'Hermitage et le parc Palmer
description: Hike a moderate 7.0-mile (11.3 km) loop through Parc des Coteaux, passing Hermitage Park and the Jacques Chaban-Delmas Bridge.

name: Bordeaux: Two Bridges Trail
name_original: Le tour des quais de Bordeaux
description: Hike the easy 4.6-mile (7.3 km) tour of the quays of Bordeaux, exploring the Garonne river waterfront and UNESCO-listed facades.

name: Bouliac Circuit Trail
name_original: Escapade sur les hauteurs de Bouliac
description: Enjoy an easy 4.4-mile hike on the Escapade on the heights of Bouliac, offering panoramic views and diverse terrain near Bordeaux.

name: Cenac and Roger Lapébie Greenway Loop
name_original: Boucle entre Latresne et Cenac
description: Hike the moderate 6.0-mile Cenac and Roger Lapébie Greenway Loop, enjoying flat, paved paths through forests, vineyards, and historical rail

name: Via Sancti Martini - Saragosse Way Trail
name_original: Boucle dans Bordeaux
description: Hike the easy 3.6-mile Via Sancti Martini - Saragosse Way Trail in Bordeaux, exploring historical sites like Roman ruins and the Water Mirro

name: The south of the plain in gravel
name_original: El sur de la plana en gravel
description:
The Route will take you to visit the southern part of the Penedès plain, a depression located between the coastal and pre-coastal mountains whose landscape is characterized by the mosaic between vineyards and small towns, and in this part of the plain, also industry. This southern part of the Penedès plain is where you will find more vestiges, in the form of castles and watchtowers, of the border of the Marca Hispánica.

The journey begins at the Vilafranca del Penedès train station, to immediately take a bike lane that will take you to Moja, a small town next to Vilafranca. Far from the hustle and bustle of the region's capital, in Moja take the Camí de Penyafel to continue to Santa Margarida i els Monjos. The path takes its name from the church and the castle of Penyafel, two constructions that you will find shortly after leaving Moja.

Without actually entering Santa Margarida, you will immediately see the silhouette of the Castell de Penyafort appear among the vineyards. After the castle, the route continues with a series of slides, at the highest point of which you will be able to see a good perspective of this part of the Penedès plain. The path now descends to cross one of the many streams in the area, before briefly joining the road and passing by the village of les Masuques, where you turn off towards Sant Esteve de les Masuques, a 10th-century Romanesque church.

The paved road continues to a hollow where you will cross the River Foix on a cement platform. The passage is usually not difficult, since the flow is small and there are barely two fingers of water on the platform. After crossing the Foix you will immediately arrive at the wooded hill where the Castell de Castellet is located, a place with privileged views of the Foix reservoir and where you will find a fountain and also a bar that is very popular with cyclists and motorcyclists.

After Castellet you go to Gornal, along a paved path between the forest and the vineyards, leaving the silhouette of l'Arboç on your right. After crossing the Gornal, you head towards Banyeres del Penedès, but first you will pass by the Sant Miquel stream picnic area, a place with tables and a good shade to stop. In Banyeres you pass through the Plaza de l'Olm, with its fountain covered with stone ashlars.

Leaving Banyeres, take the road to Sant Jaume dels Domenys via Lleger, but immediately take a small detour to avoid traffic. After Sant Jaume, the route passes near the Castell de Gimenelles, now converted into a rural hotel. And shortly after you will find a new rest area, with a table, shades, and a fountain, the Bassa Gran.

After traveling a few hundred meters along the Puerto de les Ventoses road, you will begin your return to Vilafranca, heading north again. The paths alternate between paved and dirt, finally arriving at Sant Sadurní de Castellví, which will welcome you with its bell tower hidden among the trees. Back on the road, you will cross the small town of Casas Noves de la Riera to then head towards Torre Vernet, a union of four farmhouses, which includes the Sant Pere Advincula chapel. It is one of my favorite places on this Route, with its grouped buildings, its particular cactus garden and a beautiful sundial.

Before finally facing the road to Vilafranca, you will pass by the Noucentista farmhouse of Can LLeó, after overcoming a popular slope in the area that has its own annual race. From here, the path crosses the residential area of Pla de Cavalls and again briefly by road and finally by dirt track, back to Vilafranca.

name: Gravel from the balcony of Penedès
name_original: Gravel desde el balcón del Penedès
description:
Starting from Vilafranca del Penedès, this route proposes to cross the central part of the Penedès plain towards the northwest, gradually gaining altitude until reaching the Miravinya del Balcó del Penedès viewpoint. Located in the Serra del Bolet, the ascent to this viewpoint presents a long and practically sustained ascent that rewards with excellent views over the Penedès plain, but also the Garraf, and on days of good visibility, even the Barcelones.

The route begins with an almost unexpected path, just after leaving the Vilafranca sports area, and which follows the Llitrà stream. You will enter Pacs del Penedès by another path, which follows the Pacs stream and leads to the racó de la Salzareda, a place with good shade next to the church of Sant Genís. Leaving Pacs behind, another flat covered with vineyards opens up and, in the distance, you can see the small mountain of Can Sogues, on whose slopes lies the town of Vilobí del Penedès and where the Parc dels Talls is located.

This park is the result of the recovery of gypsum mines and has several lakes that flood the old quarries. On the other side of the Can Sogues mountain there is another new flat covered with vineyards and, further on, the Serra del Bolet, where the highest point of the route awaits you.

The route now surrounds the nucleus of the Rovira Roja to then begin a 7-kilometre ascent in which you will only find a short break. It is not a very hard ascent, although there are specific slopes where it will be convenient to engage the large crown and in other places choose a good line to avoid any large stones that appear on the way. The Font-rubí fountain is a good place to take a short break, refill the drums and thus face an ascent section of almost 2 kilometers on asphalt to reach the Miravinya Balcó del Penedès.

After reaching the viewpoint, the path continues to climb a little more, bordering the Sierra del Bolet and leaving the entire Penedès plain on your right. The descent to l'Avellà requires a little attention. The track is in good condition, but the descent is steep, with a few hairpin bends where you will find some loose stone, so it is advisable not to go down too fast. After crossing the small town of l'Avellà and the road, you will continue descending again towards the flat, the track in its first section is steep and you will find stones, so, again, I recommend caution.

The next section, until reaching Santa María de Bellver, is a succession of farmhouses, some isolated, others very close to each other, forming small nuclei or "neighborhoods" on an undulating plateau that outlines the transition between the Serra del Bolet and the flat of the Penedes. Again you will find some section of steep descent, so be careful! Crossing streams, between vineyards, farmhouses and cellars, you reach Puigdàlber, which you will cross quickly until you reach Santa Fe del Penedès. Here the path deviates just before entering the town to go through the Torrent de la Casa Nova path, a good time to take advantage of the shade and the radical change in the landscape. Before leaving Santa Fe, you will go to the Parc dels Pins de Cal Ferret, a recreational area whose fountain and viewpoint are worth a visit.

After a brief ascent by road leaving Santa Fe, the route continues between vineyards until you can see Vilafranca del Penedès again, where the route ends.

name: Abécédaire Trail
name_original: A la découverte du Cap-Ferret
description: Discover the diverse landscapes of Cap-Ferret on this moderate 10.1-mile hike, featuring ocean views and oyster villages.

name: Super Balade 4: Tour and Detours at Bordeaux Lake
name_original: Entre le lac de Bordeaux et la réserve du Barail
description: Super Balade 4: Tour and Detours at Bordeaux Lake is a moderate 6.2-mile hike exploring natural areas and urban sights.

name: Blue and Green Lakes Loop
name_original: Entre le lac bleu et le lac vert de Léognan
description: Hike 6.4 miles (10.3 km) between the serene Blue Lake and Green Lake of Léognan, exploring forests and vineyards on a moderate trail.

name: Les Gabares Departmental Circuit
name_original: Promenade le long de la Garonne
description: Hike the 4.9-mile Les Gabares Departmental Circuit, a moderate route near Barie featuring the Garonne river and cornfields.

name: Jucar Canyons Trail from Cortes de Pallas
name_original: Los cañones del Júcar — Circular desde Cortes de Pallás
description: This difficult 36.9-mile road cycling route through the Jucar canyons from Cortes de Pallas offers dramatic scenery and 4719 feet of gain.

name: Gallinera Valley and La Llacuna Loop from Gandía
name_original: El Vall de la Gallinera y La Llacuna — Vuelta desde Gandía
description: This difficult 37.4-mile road cycling route from Gandía explores the scenic Vall de la Gallinera and La Llacuna with 3084 feet of climbing.

name: Contreras Pass Ascent — Utiel Loop
name_original: Subida al puerto de Contreras — Circular desde Utiel
description: This moderate 43.3-mile road cycling route from Utiel climbs to Contreras pass, offering panoramic views of vineyards and the reservoir.

name: Teresa de Cofrentes and Cinto de la Cabra Loop
name_original: El Alto del Cinto de la Cabra — Circular desde Teresa de Cofrentes
description: Challenge yourself on The High of the Goat's Belt, a difficult 32.9-mile road cycling route from Teresa de Cofrentes with 4220 feet of gain.

name: Gravel with sea views in Penedes
name_original: Gravel con vistas al mar en el Penedès
description:
This route again moves away from the landscape of the Penedès plain, this time to enter the Garraf massif. In fact, you leave the region for a few kilometers to get to know a different landscape, typical of the Mediterranean low mountains near the sea, with white pine forests and thickets and fewer watercourses. The itinerary will crown at the Puig de l'Àliga (420 meters), from where you can contemplate the sea and the Garraf and Baix Penedès regions.

Leaving from La Granada train station, the itinerary quickly enters the vineyards to reach Sant Cugat Sesgarrigues, from where it begins to climb along a track in good condition, but with the occasional ramp, to les Gunyoles. There you will go up to another Miravinya viewpoint, the Circell, with a spectacular 360° panoramic view.

Pay attention to the descent to the bottom of the Vedell, since, although it is not very steep, there are quite a few stones and the occasional rut. The landscape now combines forest and vineyard, a constant on this Route. The vineyards are now smaller in size, taking advantage of any more or less flat corner of the woods, and you will see more often the typical stone huts of the area.

After a short climb, you will see at your feet a small interior depression, the Santa Susanna plain. Be careful again on the descent, due to the loose stone and the sharp curves. Below you will have a privileged view of the Santa Susanna farmhouse, now abandoned, but which allows you to intuit how different life was in these places in the past.

On the track that leads to Olivella you can find loose horses and also electrified fences to prevent them from leaving the area. On a couple of occasions you will have to stop to raise the fence and be able to pass. The route does not reach Olivella, but rather deviates beforehand to circulate along the (dry) stream of Begues in the direction of the Mas Milà urbanization. This section next to the Begues stream has some rocky areas with roots, but there are no steep slopes and the path, although narrow in places, is quite clean.

In Mas Milà and after a brief incursion into the Garraf, you will be back in the Penedès, but you will continue in an eminently wooded landscape. After a few kilometers of slight ascent along a dirt track, you will arrive at Viladellops, a small aggregate of medieval origin (year 930) in which the Casa Gran stands out, home to some wineries, a defense tower and a church of Romanesque origin.

From Viladellops, continue along a paved path to the very base of the Castell d'Olèrdola, which you climb along a track that, taking a short detour, leads to the road that goes up to the castle. Before you will have passed in front of a restored dry stone cabin, with its particular plant of lilies on its roof that helps to fix the structure. The climbs don't end here. In fact, from the castle to Puig de l'Àliga you will have to overcome some important slope, but again, the views are worth it. From this privileged point you will have views of the sea, but also of the Penedès plain. On the descent, again be careful, as you will find loose stones.

After crossing the Daltmar urbanization, you will continue along the road for a couple of kilometers until you connect with the Muntanyeta track, in Moja. From there, the route borders Vilafranca between vineyards and orchards, to take the camí de l'Hostal Nou that will take you back to the La Granada train station.

name: La Serranía Villages Loop
name_original: Los pueblos de La Serranía — Circular desde Casinos
description: Tackle the difficult 42.8-mile road cycling route from Casinos, climbing 3944 feet through La Serranía's scenic villages and passes.

name: Gravel to the highest of Penedes
name_original: Gravel a lo más alto del Penedès
description:
This is the longest Route in the Collection and the one with the greatest slope, remaining just a few meters from the highest point of the Penedès, Puig de Solanes (913 metres). Gone is the flat and its undulating terrain due to the passage of rivers and torrents, here the climbs are longer and more sustained, and the landscape is totally different: pre-coastal mountains that rise quickly and are only interrupted by narrow gorges that fluvial erosion has been modeling in the calcareous rock. It is a wilder Penedès, less densely populated, with fountains, sanctuaries and castles, and of course, vineyards.

Starting from the Vilafranca del Penedès train station, the route quickly leaves the urban center to the south, crossing the Foix River under the Bleda tower and quickly advancing along local roads and paths to the Sa Roca Monumental complex, in Sant Martí. Sarroca. After visiting it and contemplating the beautiful view of the plain and the Montserrat mountain from "Sa Roca", you return along the same path to continue advancing to Torrelles de Foix.

In Torrelles you will not get to enter the town, but you will take a paved road to the left, starting a long climb of 12 kilometres, first on paved road and then on a track to the Creu de Sapera pass (646 metres). This climb has some breaks, and near the top of the track it has some slides with loose rock that will require you to pay attention to driving.

The descent to Pontons is done along a perfectly paved path, until you reach the road, which you will follow briefly to the town. In Pontons there is a bar very popular with cyclists, and also a fountain in front of the town hall, highly recommended for the freshness and good taste of the water that flows. From Pontons, the path climbs again, first along a track and then along a paved path, to the highest point of the Route. After the first dirt ramps, harder, the difference in level becomes smoother. In total there are 5 kilometers with an average slope of 4.7%, quite manageable.

After crowning, you will return to the road for just 2 kilometers, to take a track to the left. Do not get lost, because the detour is in the middle of a curve to the right of the road. This descent track is quite wide and in good condition, but the descent is long and steep, so be careful. Before arriving at the Can Baltà farmhouse, you will pass through a picnic area, with shade and stone tables, although without a fountain. The track continues downhill until you reach les Cases Noves de Can Pardo, where you will find a fountain, and from there continue by road for 3 kilometers to Sant Joan de Mediona, a larger town where there are, in addition to fountains, bars and restaurants.

A narrow paved road will take you from Sant Joan de Mediona to the Castell de Mediona, a fortification with more than a thousand years of history that presides over a narrow gorge through which the Mediona river runs and the path that used to connect Sant Quintí de Mediona with Sant Joan of Mediona. The exterior of the castle can be visited (visits to the interior can also be arranged), taking the detour from the path for a hundred meters. The paved road continues after crossing an urbanization, until Sant Quintí, which you will cross through the interior of its historic urban area.

The exit from Sant Quintí is through the area known as the Raval, full of small neighborhood gardens, next to the Mediona stream, which you will cross on a metal and wood bridge that gives access to the Ca l'Oliver mill, a complex of houses, some half in ruins, and the building of an important mill, in a good state of conservation. Following the path and after a short ascent, the route returns to the road to reach Sant Pere de Riudebitlles, where you will take a track again in the direction of the Miravinya la Cadira viewpoint, from where you can contemplate both the Valls d'Anoia, looking at towards the Montserrat mountain, like the Penedès plain.

The return to Vilafranca will first take a path to reach the C-15 service road and, from there, you will not leave the small local and regional roads until you practically reach the capital of the region and the beginning of the route, crossing towns such as Guardiola de Font-rubí, the neighborhood of La Rovira Roja, Vilobí del Penedès and Pacs.

name: Cazaux Lake and Forest Loop
name_original: Boucle à l'étang de Cazaux-Sanguinet
description: Hike a moderate 8.1-mile loop around the picturesque Cazaux-Sanguinet Lake, enjoying wooded paths and clear waters.

name: Longarisse - Lacanau Lake Loop
name_original: Le long de l'étang de Lacanau
description: An easy 4.8-mile (7.7 km) hike around Lacanau Lake, offering tranquil views, pine forests, and wildlife spotting opportunities.

name: Montesa Castle Loop from Moixent
name_original: El Castillo de Montesa — Circular desde Moixent
description: This difficult 46.2-mile road cycling route from Moixent offers 3616 feet of elevation gain and panoramic views of Montesa Castle.

name: Júcar Water Route Loop
name_original: La Ruta del Agua del Júcar — Circular desde Torrent
description: This difficult 62.4-mile road cycling route from Torrent explores the diverse landscapes of the Júcar River, requiring very good fitness.

name: La Hoya de Buñol and Serra Perenxisa Loop from Picassent
name_original: La Hoya de Buñol y la Serra Perenxisa — Circular desde Picassent
description: This difficult 51.1-mile road cycling route from Picassent offers challenging climbs and varied scenery through the Serra Perenxisa.

name: Gravel through Penedès, the capital of cava
name_original: Gravel por el Penedès, la capital del cava
description:
Sant Sadurní d'Anoia is known as the capital of cava, for its manufacturing tradition and for bringing together in its municipal district some of the largest cavas in the region and throughout the state. This Route passes through some of these caves, but first it will go through the foothills of the Serra de l'Ordal, to gain some height and be able to contemplate this part of the Penedès, which geographically and in terms of landscape is integrated into the so-called Valls de l'Anoia. On the way back, it goes up parallel to the Bitlles River to cross the northern part of the Penedès plain.

The La Granada train station is the beginning of this route, which quickly takes you to the small town of Sant Sebastià dels Gorgs, where there is an interesting Romanesque monastery. The first ramps go up to Miravinya La Bardera, one of the five themed viewpoints scattered throughout the Penedès. From there you can see your next destination, Sant Sadurní d'Anoia, which you reach after a quick descent along a dirt track, be careful with the tight curves with loose sand!

After crossing a level crossing with a barrier, you will arrive at Sant Sadurní, leaving the Freixenet cellars on the right. After crossing Sant Sadurní, the route crosses the Anoia river, but first you will pass through the cellar, and the oldest company in the country, the Codorníu cellars, founded in 1551! The cellar building is modernist in style, the work of Puig i Cadafalch.

Shortly after, the Anoia river is crossed for the first time, it is a pass with little flow, but it is possible that your feet get a little wet. On one side there are cement pylons that allow safe passage if the flow is greater. After crossing the center of Can Catasús and a brief incursion by road, you will cross the Anoia river again, this time over a narrow bridge, almost a footbridge, very particular.

You now enter a different landscape, of small valleys, marked by the presence of the Anoia River and its tributary, the Bitlles River. In this area of the Valls d'Anoia you will find old paper mills, although few retain their original function. Heading towards Sant Pere de Riudebitlles you will see some. The section from Torrelavit to Sant Pere is one of my favorites on the Route: the Camí de les Hortes, true to its name, surrounded by orchards, and passing by a spectacular restored mill, the Molí Blanc, now a rural hotel.

After crossing Sant Pere, a short ascent begins to another Miravinya, that of La Cadira, with a good perspective of the Penedès plain. Located on a small mound, it can be cycled to the top, although it may be better to push the bike depending on the tires and/or gear. After a new section between vineyards, you reach another viewpoint, the Creu de Lavit, to say goodbye to the Valls de Mediona and definitively return to the flat.

On the way to Pla del Penedès, next to the Can Bas wineries, is the small church of Sant Joan Salerm, one of my favorite images of the Penedès. You will enter the Pla through the small torrent de la Rovira, which is crossed by a somewhat precarious plank, I recommend dismounting. After leaving the town, you cross an extensive succession of vineyards to Granada, where the route passes by the remains of its 10th-century castle, before returning to the train station, closing the route.

name: From the mountains to the sea with the Gravelbike
name_original: Von den Bergen bis zum Meer mit dem Gravelbike
description:
The green lung of Barcelona rises behind the streets and houses of the city: the Serra de Collserola Natural Park. Covering more than 8,000 hectares, it offers city dwellers an expansive space for walking, cycling and relaxing from the hustle and bustle of the city.

On this tour you drive along the Carretera de les Aigues along the very edge of the park - you always have magnificent views down to the city and the sea on the horizon. At the very end, a sea bath at the Platja de la Mar Bella lures.

With only 40 kilometers, you can also do the tour in one afternoon. It starts from the Avinguda Diagonal straight ahead and past the Zona Universitaria. Then you zigzag uphill on a bike path. You reach the entrance to Collserola Park and can look forward to kilometers of gravel happiness with an outstanding panorama of Barcelona.

Since the route is also used by people on foot, you should adjust your speed on the bike accordingly. In several places you have the opportunity to pause and enjoy the great view in peace - just the right thing to escape the hustle and bustle of the big city for a moment.

You slowly make your way downhill again. A ten-kilometer descent beckons, which is just plain fun. If you keep going straight through Vilapiscina, el Clot and Poble Nou, you will get straight to the Platja de Mar Bella. A dip in the sea is the perfect end to your little trip. How awesome is it to live in a city nestled between the mountains and the sea?

name: Through the Parc de Collserola to La Floresta By Gravelbike
name_original: Durch den Parc de Collserola nach La Floresta Mit dem Gravelbike
description:
It starts in El Raval and meanders through Gracia - how about coffee and breakfast in Plaça Vila de Gracia? Freshly strengthened, it's time for the crisp climb. While the beads of sweat are running down your forehead, the view over Barcelona is getting better and better.

Between the stately Aleppo pines you can always catch a magnificent view of the city, which now spreads out below you, and the sea, which appears as a broad blue stripe on the horizon.

On wide and easy-to-drive gravel paths, it is now more and more purely in the nature park. Many wild boars live here, some of which are hardly shy and can get pretty close to people walking and cycling. The shaggy companions are still wild animals - so you should better not approach them unnecessarily and not feed them.

Not long now and you will reach La Floresta. A longer lunch break is worthwhile here, as there are several restaurants with beautiful terraces and delicious dishes. When you're ready, it's time for the last few meters of altitude for today.

The way back leads over the road and is therefore much faster, and it doesn't get particularly steep anymore. An adrenaline-packed descent takes you back to the city and back to where you started on the Rambla del Raval. Here you have more than earned your Caña!

name: Delta del Llobregat & Montjuïc With the Gravelbike
name_original: Delta del Llobregat & Montjuïc Mit dem Gravelbike
description:
Would you like a little after-work stroll along the banks of the Llobregat? Even if this side of Barcelona is strongly characterized by the port, airport and industrial zone, it is also worth a trip in this area, because you gain a completely new perspective on your city.

From Plaza España, the extension of the Paralel goes dead straight, across Sants and Esplugues de Llobregat. After only ten kilometers you will reach the banks of the Llobregat. It is the second longest river in Catalonia and you are now following its last section to the mouth of the sea.

A varied gravel path leads along the shore, which you follow for the next ten kilometers. Just before the mouth you can climb a lookout tower and admire the delta from above. At the very end of the track, where the path ends, there are two abandoned houses of the Carrabineri barracks to discover. The planes take off and land over your head, because you are right next to the airport - a really special atmosphere.

Then it goes across the Zona Industrial and Zona Franca back towards the city. You will be amazed that even in this area there are real bike lanes. You're getting to the foot of Montjuïc - how about one last little climb to enjoy the sunset? A small dirt road leads you up, you reach the Castell de Port, a ruined castle that towers over the port on a steep slope. A final descent brings you back to where you started, Plaza España. If you don't want to make the detour over Montjuïc, just go straight on and back along Gran Vía.
